citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using the Manual
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using the Manual

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using the Manual

body: The SNAP Manual is an essential tool for taking action and making decisions regarding eligibility for SNAP. The Manual provides information and guidance about the policies governing the SNAP program in Connecticut, and to support the needs of workers and other agency staff.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using the Manual 1
heading: Introduction

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using the Manual

body: For the best view, use either Internet Explorer (IE) or Microsoft Edge browsers.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using the Manual 2
heading: Viewing the Manual

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using the Manual

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation

body:
The SNAP Program Manual displays in a 3 pane format:

Menu Bar

Navigation Pane

Policy Pane

The following is a brief description of the positions where information can be displayed, navigated to, and searched.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation 1
heading: Navigation

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation

body:
The Menu Bar is positioned in the top pane and displays the following:

Contents – displays the listing of Items contained in the manual.

Index – provides an alphabetical list of all key words or phrases in the manual. Type a key word or phrase in the text box or click on an Index item to go to that location in the manual.

Search – Type the word or phrase in the text box, then click GO.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation 2
heading: Menu Bar

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation

body:
The Navigation Pane is located on the left side of your browser. It shows the structure of the manual with a series of Items, Sections, and Sub-Sections I & II. The Items are presented in a logical order to understand the policy needed to process a case.

Click on a closed book icon to open it to see the contents. Once a book is opened, the icon changes to an opened book. Click on a subfolder until you get to the lowest level of organization. When you click on the document icon, the policy will appear on the right side of your browser window.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation 3
heading: Navigation Pane

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation

body: The Policy Pane appears on the right side of the web browser and will display the SNAP policy selected in the Navigation Pane.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation 4
heading: Policy Pane

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation

body:
To resize the Navigation Pane or the Policy Pane, click on the vertical line that divides them and drag to the left or right as needed.

To expand the Policy Pane to fill the screen, click on the “X” in the upper right hand corner of the Navigation Pane. This closes the Navigation Pane. To re-display the Navigation Pane, click Contents in the Menu Bar.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation 5
heading: Viewing Content

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation

body:
Like an Index in a printed manual, the Index is designed to help in locating specific information. Click the Index icon on the Menu Bar to display a list of words found in the SNAP Manual.

Locating Index information is done in one of 2 ways:

Type a keyword in ‘Type in the keyword to find:’ text box.

Scroll through the displayed words/phrases and select to display the information in the Policy Pane.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation 6
heading: Using the Index

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation

body:
Click the Search icon on the Menu Bar and type in a specific word or phrase in the text box to find instances within the manual. To search for a phrase, enter it in quotation marks in the search box.

Example: “medical expenses”. The results will be displayed in the Navigation Pane. Select the “Highlight search results” box to help identify where the word/phrase is found in the policy.

All search results will be found with the total number of results indicated. The default is set to view 10 results per page. If there are more than 10 results there will be an option to select additional pages of search results at the bottom of the search pane. You may also change the value to a larger number such as 99 to get more results per page.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation 7
heading: Using the Search Function

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation

body:
Terms in the manual that appear as blue underlined text are hyperlinks. There are 4 different types of hyperlinks used throughout the manual that will display additional information.

Link within the Item – these hyperlinks are shortcuts to a specific place within the Item. These allow a “jump” from one part of the document to another without scrolling. To return to the previous location in the manual click the back button in the top.

Link to a different Item – these hyperlinks are shortcuts to a specific place within different Items of the manual. To return to the previous location in the manual click the back button in the top.

Link to an Intranet site – these hyperlinks will open the Intranet site window. To return to the manual just close the window.

Link to an Internet site – these hyperlinks will open the Internet site window. To return to the manual just close the window.

Link to Glossary – these links will appear as blue , underlined and italicized text and will link directly to the Glossary term. To return to the previous location in the manual click the back button in the top toolbar. You can also get to the Glossary by selecting the Glossary Item in the Navigation Pane. The Glossary is alphabetized and you may select the letter of the alphabet at the top of the Glossary Item of the term you are looking for.

Note: After a link has been selected it will turn gray. To refresh the manual so that all links will again appear blue, go to Internet browser settings and clear your browsing history, then select the refresh icon.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation 8
heading: Hyperlinks

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation

body: Text highlighted in grey in the Policy Pane indicates changes made during the last release.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation 9
heading: Grey Highlighted Text

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Navigation

body: Legal Base identifies the federal law and regulations the Item policy is based on. Federal legal base is shown immediately below the Section and Sub-section headings, in smaller font and italics.

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Information
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Information

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Information

body:
7 CFR § 271.1(a)

The Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended, authorizes and is the foundation of the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP). SNAP helps low income individuals and families stretch their food budget and put healthy meals on the table every day.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Information 1
heading: SNAP Purpose

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Information

body:
7 CFR § 271.4(a)-(b) & 7 CFR § 271.6 (a) & 7 CFR 272.1(c)-(d) 7 CFR § 272.2(a)-(f) & 7 CFR § 272.3(a)-(d) & 7 CFR § 272.4(a)-(g) & 7 CFR § 272.5(a)-(c) & 7 CFR § 272.6(a)-(h) & 7 CFR § 272.8(a)-(e) & 7 CFR § 272.9 & & 7 CFR § 272.10(a)-(b) & 7 CFR § 272.11(a)-(e) & 7 CFR § 272.12(a)-(c) & 7 CFR § 272.13(a)-(c) & 7 CFR § 272.15(a)-(b) & 7 CFR § 272.16(a)-(c)

The USDA ’s Food and Nutrition Service (FNS) delegates to the Connecticut Department of Social Services (DSS) the responsibility for the administration of the SNAP program within the State. These responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

Approving applicant EDGs in compliance with Federal regulations.

Approving homeless meal providers, unless delegated to another State agency.

Complying with administration and personnel requirements.

Complying with and providing equal service without regard to age, color, disability, handicap, national origin, political belief, race, religion, or sex, based on the following:

Age Discrimination Act of 1975

Rehabilitation Act of 1973

Section 11(c) of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended

Title IV of the Civil Rights Act of 1964.

Complying with Automated Data Processing/Computerization of Information Systems (ADP/CIS) Plan requirements.

Complying with computer matching requirements including but not limited to:

Deceased individual match

Income and Eligibility Verification System

National Directory of New Hires

Prisoner Verification System

Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements Program

Conducting performance reporting reviews such as Quality Control (QC) and Management Evaluation (ME) .

Developing and maintaining complaint procedures .

Developing, conducting, and evaluating training.

Following nondiscrimination compliance

Implementing programs and being responsive to special needs of Native Americans on reservations, and working in good faith with tribal organizations.

Issuance, control, and accountability of SNAP benefits.

Keeping records necessary to determine whether the program is conducted in compliance with Federal regulations.

Processing recipient claims and underpayments .

Program informational activities .

Providing staff with the needed forms, and written guidelines and operating procedures to administer the program.

Reporting any major changes in the program operation.

Submitting accurate and timely financial and program reports.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Information 2
heading: State Agency Responsibilities

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Information

body:
Source #

Effective Date
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Information 3

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers

body:
7 CFR § 271.2 & 7 CFR § 272.1(c)(3) & 7 CFR 273.2(c)(1)(i) and (iii) and (7)(iv)(B) and (d) and (e)(1)-(3) and (f)(1)(viii) and (5)(i)-(ii) and (i)(4)(i)(B) and (j)(1)(iv) and (n)(1)-(3) & 7 CFR § 273.14(b)(2)-(3)

Authorized representatives (A-Reps) and authorized shoppers are chosen to act on behalf of the EDG by the:

head of household ,

spouse, or

other responsible member.

EDGs note in writing the name of the individual they choose as an A-Rep or authorized shopper.

The EDG may appoint one A-Rep and up to two authorized shoppers . The A-Rep or the authorized shopper receive their own EBT card.

The EDG may provide written notice to stop approval of an A-Rep or authorized shopper at any time. Deactivate the A-Rep or authorized shopper’s EBT card.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers 1
heading: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(n)(1)(i)-(ii) & 273.11(e)(1)

Ensure the A-Rep or authorized shopper meets the following requirements:

a nonhousehold member,

18 years of age or older,

aware of the EDG’s situation, and

designated in writing by the EDG, or

the assigned A-Rep for the Addiction Treatment Center. See Addiction Treatment Center for A-Rep requirements.

Note: Group Living for the Disabled facilities may also have designated A-Reps for their residents. See Group Living for the Disabled for A-Rep requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers 2
heading: Eligible A-Reps and Authorized Shoppers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers

body:
7 CFR 273.2(n)

A-Reps can complete the following actions when acting for the EDG:

Apply for SNAP benefits for the EDG.

Complete the interview.

Complete work registrations .

Gather and provide required information or verifications.

Obtain and use the EDG’s SNAP benefits to purchase allowable food .

Exception: The A-Rep cannot obtain and use the EDG’s SNAP benefits if the EDG appoints an authorized shopper.

Renew SNAP benefits.

Report changes .

Request a fair hearing .

Review case information .

Signing agency records on behalf of the EDG.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers 3
heading: A-Reps

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers

body:
7 CFR 273.2(n)(2)-(3)

Authorized shopper actions are limited to obtaining and using the EDG’s SNAP benefits to purchase allowable food for the EDG.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers 4
heading: Authorized Shopper

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(n)(4)(i)-(iii)

Restrictions regarding who can be an A-Rep:

DSS employees who approve or issue benefits may only act as an A-Rep/shopper if no one else is available. A DSS Administrative Official must provide written approval.

Homeless meal providers are not eligible to act as an A-Rep/shopper for a homeless EDG.

Individuals currently disqualified for IPV may not be an A-Rep/shopper for the EDG unless:

there is no other adult in the EDG, and

a new A-Rep/shopper cannot be assigned.

Note: Other excluded individuals may be an A-Rep for the EDG if the individual is an adult with knowledge of the EDG’s situation.

Retailers who accept SNAP may only act as an A-Rep/shopper if no one else is available. A DSS Administrative Official must provide written approval.

A-Reps/shoppers are not limited to the number of EDGs they can represent, unless DSS sets a limit to prevent SNAP abuse.

Use caution when A-Reps/shoppers are the employer, such as those who employ migrant or seasonal farmworkers, or single A-Reps/shoppers who represent large numbers of EDGs. Assure:

the EDG freely requested the A-Rep/shopper,

the EDG’s circumstances are correctly represented, and

the benefits are used appropriately.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers 5
heading: A-Rep and Authorized Shopper Restrictions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers

body:
7 CFR 273.2(n)(4)(i)(B)-(C) and 7 CFR § 273.11(e)(7) and (f)(6)

A-Rep/shoppers are disqualified from being an A-Rep/shopper for one year when the agency determines the individual:

knowingly provided false information about the EDG, or

used the benefits improperly.

Exception: This does not apply to Addiction Treatment Centers or Group Living for the Disabled facilities. See Institutions Approved as Retailers Acting as an A-Rep/Authorized Shopper .

Notify the EDG and the individual 30 days before disqualifying the individual as an A-Rep/shopper. Include in the notice:

the action that will be taken, and

their fair hearing rights .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers 6
heading: A-Rep and Shopper Disqualifications

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(n)(1)

Inform the EDG they are responsible for any overpayment caused by information the A-Rep reported.

Exception : Addiction Treatment Centers and Group Living for the Disabled facilities are responsible for overpayments caused by information reported by their A-Reps.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers 7
heading: Overpayments

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(B) & 7 CFR § 273.11(e)(8) and (f)(6) & 7 CFR 273.13(b)(11) & 7 CFR § 278.6

Addiction Treatment Centers and Group Living for the Disabled A-Reps approved as retailers by FNS are disqualified by FNS, not the State . This action is taken only after an administrative or judicial determination shows benefits were:

misappropriated, or

used for purchases that were not used for the EDG’s meals.

Refer institutions suspected of these actions to the Fraud Unit for referral to FNS.

Note: Addiction Treatment Centers and Group Living for the Disabled facilities who lose their retail food store authorization by FNS also lose their institution exemption. The resident is no longer eligible for SNAP because they are a resident of an ineligible institution.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers 8
heading: Institutions Approved as Retailers Acting as an A-Rep / Authorized Shopper

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(vii)

The A-Rep must verify their identity when applying for an EDG.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers 9
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Authorized Representatives (A-Rep) and Authorized Shoppers

body:

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Records
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Records

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Records

body:
7 CFR § 272.10(b)(1)-(2)

Case records contain information regarding EDGs. This information is entered into ImpaCT. All case record information is considered confidential .

The case record and ImpaCT provide the means for workers to determine eligibility and provide the EDG with appropriate benefits and services.

See Record Retention for the length of time case record information must be kept.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Records 1
heading: Case Records

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Records

body:
7 CFR § 272.10(b)(1)-(2)

Case records contain:

Applications

Benefit computations

Case notes

Correspondence

Current and historical information about the EDG

Eligibility determinations

Forms

Individual information

Issuance information

Overissuance and claims information

Supporting records and verifications

If the EDG disagrees with the case record information, they can make a written request to have their case record updated. Act on the request and provide notice to the EDG within 30 days advising:

if the information was corrected, or

the reason the information was not corrected.

Note: The EDG retains the right to add to the case record their own statement regarding what they believe is the accurate information.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Records 2
heading: Case Record Contents

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Records

body:
Source #

Effective Date
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Records 3
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Records

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Complaints
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Complaints

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Complaints

body:
7 CFR § 271.6(a)(1)-(2)

Complaints are issues regarding processing standards and service to individuals. An EDG member, A-Rep , or other individual can make a complaint.

See Nondiscrimination for complaints regarding discrimination.

DSS provides information to individuals regarding the complaint process and how to file a complaint:

in applications and renewal applications,

NOA s ,

on the agency website, and

verbally during interviews.

DSS reviews complaints to determine if the agency’s actions were correct and, if required, make changes. The EDG or A-Rep may request a fair hearing if they are not satisfied with the results of the complaint review and outcome.

Note : Follow confidentiality procedures to determine if any information can be released.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Complaints 1
heading: Complaints

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Complaints

body:
7 CFR § 271.6(a)(3)

DSS must keep records of all complaints and their disposition. Complaint records will be reviewed annually to determine if there are patterns and issues that need to be addressed. Additionally, complaints are reviewed by:

ME ,

Regional or State Office staff, and

FNS auditors.

Complaint review information is provided to the coordinator for the Performance Reporting System, and may be included in the State Corrective Action Plan. FNS monitors the State’s compliance through the Performance Reporting System.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Complaints 2
heading: Complaint Records and Reviews

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Complaints

body:
7 CFR § 271.6(b)(1)(vi) and (2)

Individuals or other agencies needing program information, or wishing to file a complaint, may contact the FNS Regional Office at:

Northeast Regional Office,

U.S. Department of Agriculture,

Food and Nutrition Service,

10 Causeway St., Boston, MA 02222-1069

FNS advises the individual of the State agency complaint process and fair hearing procedures. FNS will pursue the complaint upon request by the individual.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Complaints 3
heading: Complaints to FNS

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Complaints

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

body:
7 CFR § 272.1(c)(1) and (2) and (d)

SNAP case information is confidential under Federal and State law. Use or release of information from SNAP case records is limited to approved individuals, who must adequately protect the information against unauthorized disclosure.

Confidentiality policy is provided through the form W-0016RR, Rights and Responsibilities, and the W-1E, Application. Ensure the EDG understands the policy for confidentiality, release of information, and penalties, before signing the application.

Give the W-0016RR to applicants, recipients, other agencies, and whenever releasing information, to provide information about the rights, requirements and penalties when receiving confidential information.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality 1
heading: Confidentiality

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

body:
7 CFR § 272.1(c)(1)(i)-(viii) & & 7 CFR § 272.8(a)(1) & 7 CFR § 272.11(b)(2)(v) and (e)

Information may be released to individuals directly connected with the administration of:

Child Support

Federal assistance programs,

Federally assisted State means-tested programs,

General assistance programs,

The National School Lunch Program for the purpose of approving eligibility of children for free meals based on their receipt of SNAP

Programs which are required to participate in the Income and Eligibility Verification System (IEVS)

Verification of immigration status through SAVE .

Any Local, State, or Federal law enforcement official, upon a written request, for the purpose of investigating an alleged violation of the SNAP program. The request must include:

the individual’s name;

violation being investigated; and

identity and authority of individual making the request.

Any Local, State, or Federal law enforcement official, upon a written request, for purpose of investigation of a felony crime or a probation or parole violation. Allowable information to provide is limited to the address, social security number and any available photograph of:

a SNAP recipient who is fleeing to avoid prosecution, custody, or confinement for a felony crime or a violation of parole or probation; or

an EDG member who has information about another EDG member who is under investigation for a felony crime or probation or parole violation.

Employees of the United States Comptroller General’s Office, upon written request, for audit purposes.

Individuals directly connected with the administration or enforcement of the provisions or regulations of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality 2
heading: Administrative Disclosure

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

body:
7 CFR § 272.1(c)(3)

SNAP EDG members may review materials contained in the case file during normal business hours, if a written request is received from:

a responsible member of the EDG,

a current A-Rep, or

any individual authorized to act on the EDG’s behalf.

The EDG must provide written permission if the A-Rep or other individual making the request for information is not a member of the EDG.

Exception: Written permission is not required if the individual has legal authority to act on behalf of the EDG, and the case record contains verification of that authority. Legal authority includes, but is not limited to, conservators or power of attorney (POA).

Verify the individual’s identity before releasing the information.

Information from case records may be copied and provided to members. The member may be charged for the cost of producing the information unless the information is for a fair hearing.

The agency may withhold confidential information such as:

the names of individuals who disclosed information about the EDG without the EDG's knowledge,

the nature or status of pending criminal prosecutions, or

medical information the agency determines could harm the EDG if released.

Exception: The agency will provide the information if:

the EDG makes a written request for a qualified medical doctor’s review of the information, and

the doctor determines the information should be released to the EDG.

The EDG can appeal to the Superior Court the agency’s decision not to release the information. The appeal request must be made no later than 30 days from the agency’s decision.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality 3
heading: EDG Access

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

body:
7 CFR § 272.1(d)(1)-(2)

The public may access the following records:

Federal regulations and procedures (during office hours at FNS offices and DSS State office),

Connecticut SNAP manual (Hard copy - during office hours at FNS offices, DSS State and field offices. Online - at any time),

State Corrective Action Plan (during office hours at FNS Offices and DSS State Office), and

State Plan of Operation (during office hours at FNS Offices and DSS State Office).

Copies of the following items are available from FNS:

Federal procedures

Plans of operation

Regulations

State corrective action plans

State manuals
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality 4
heading: Public Access

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

body:
7 CFR § 272.1(c)(1)-(2)

A court may subpoena the agency for a case record, or for a DSS employee to testify about an EDG. If you receive a subpoena for SNAP case records, forward the subpoena to the DSS Privacy Officer. The Privacy Officer determines whether DSS must comply, or if a legal objection will be filed. During the hearing, advise the court of Federal and State laws and DSS policy that restricts providing information. Provide the information if so ordered by the court.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality 5
heading: Subpoena of Case Records

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

body:
P.L. 104-191

Protected Health Information (PHI) and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) must be kept confidential. A breach is unauthorized use, obtaining, disclosing, accessing, or compromising of PHI or PII.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality 6
heading: Breaches of PHI or PII

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

body:
CT Process

Immediately report a suspected or confirmed breach of PHI or PII to your manager.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality 7
heading: Worker Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

body:
CT Process

Within one day of a reported breach, the manager will complete form W-1701, Report of Breach of Unsecure PHI or PII, and fax or email the form to the DSS Privacy Officer or legal counsel.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality 8
heading: Manager Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

body:
CT Process

Business Associates (BA) will immediately report a suspected or confirmed breach by completing the W-1701, and emailing the form to the Privacy Officer at PrivacyOfficer.dss@ct.gov . Additionally, the BA must complete form W-1702, Risk Assessment of Breach of PHI, and provide to the Privacy Officer no later than 30 days after the breach was discovered.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality 9
heading: Business Associate Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

body:
CT Process

Within one day of receipt of the W-1701, the Privacy Officer will determine whether a breach occurred. If the Privacy Officer determines a breach has occurred, a risk assessment is completed to determine if notification is required. If there is a high probability the information was compromised, the Privacy Officer or legal counsel will notify all affected individuals no later than 60 days after the breach was discovered.

Based on the risk assessment, the Privacy Officer makes recommendations to the Commissioner on whether notification is required. The affected individual may be notified, at the Commissioner’s discretion, even if not required by HIPAA .

The Privacy Officer will send the manager or BA a W-1703, Breach Resolution form, notifying them of the disposition of the breach report.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality 10
heading: Privacy Officer Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

body:
Source #

Effective Date
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality 11
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Confidentiality

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Conflict of Interest Procedures
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Conflict of Interest Procedures

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Conflict of Interest Procedures

body:
CT Process

Workers cannot work on any case involving:

co-workers,

friends,

known associates,

relatives,

themselves, or

individuals the worker has a personal interest with.

Personal interest includes but is not limited to:

Boy/girl friends

Friends (including family friends)

Significant others

Notify the supervisor of potential conflicts of interest, and for direction on handling the case.

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Notices
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Notices

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Notices

body:
7 CFR § 272.3(c)(1)-(6) & 7 CFR § 273.18(n)(2)(i)-(ii)

Waiver 2160010 of 7 CFR § 273.2(c)(5) and (e)(3) & 7 CFR § 273.10(g)(1)(i) and (ii) and (2) & 7 CFR § 273.13(a)(3) & 7 CFR § 273.14(b)(1) & 7 CFR § 273.15(l) & 7 CFR § 273.16(b)(13) and (e)(3) and (9) and (f)(1) and (3) and (g)(3) and (h) & 7 CFR § 273.18(e)(3)(i)-(vi) and (4)(i)-(ii) and(g)(2)

EDGs have the option to receive and view their notices online. EDGs must electronically sign a disclosure statement and agree to receive and read electronic notices sent by DSS. A confirmation email and paper notice are sent to the EDG with instructions on how to log into their ConneCT Account to view notices.

Emails with a link to the EDGs home page are sent to the EDG whenever a new notice is added to their account. EDGs choosing the online option receive all notices online.

Exception: EDGs are mailed paper notices for tax offsets of a claim overpayment.

The EDG can opt out of electronic notices at any time. DSS will opt out the EDG from receiving electronic notices if the EDG’s email account is invalid or no longer active. The EDG can opt in again at any time by providing a valid email address.

DSS must provide paper copies of any notices upon request by the EDG.

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Nondiscrimination
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Nondiscrimination

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Nondiscrimination

body:
7 CFR § 272.6(a) and (f)

Do not discriminate against SNAP applicants or recipients in administering SNAP including, but not limited to:

approval,

issuance of benefits,

fair hearings, or

any other program service.

Provide services and SNAP benefits to all eligible EDGs without regard to age, color, disability, national origin, political beliefs, race, religious creed, or sex.

Display the FNS Form AD 475B, And Justice For All poster, and any State developed posters in all offices administering the SNAP program.

All applications and eligibility notices provide information regarding nondiscrimination and how to file discrimination complaints. Click here for the current FNS required nondiscrimination statements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Nondiscrimination 1
heading: Nondiscrimination

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Nondiscrimination

body:
7 CFR § 272.6(a)-(b)

Individuals or agencies wishing to file a complaint alleging discrimination on the basis of age, color, disability, national origin, political beliefs, race, religious creed, or sex may contact any or all of the following:

CT Commission on Human Rights and Opportunities

DSS Affirmative Action Division

US Dept. of Agriculture

US Dept. of Health and Human Services

If an individual expresses an interest in filing a discrimination complaint:

Advise that a complaint can be submitted to any or all of the agencies.

Explain the nondiscrimination complaint systems.

Explain what information is necessary for investigation.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Nondiscrimination 2
heading: Right to File a Complaint

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Nondiscrimination

body:
7 CFR § 272.6(b)

Forward written or verbal complaints to the ADA Coordinator .

The ADA Coordinator provides assistance, if needed, to put the allegations in writing. The ADA Coordinator submits all complaints to FNS.

Discrimination complaints may be submitted to any or all of the following agencies:

DSS ADA Coordinator

55 Farmington Ave.

Hartford, CT 06105-5033

Phone: (860) 424-5040

Fax: (860) 424-4948

Email: affirmativeaction.dss@ct.gov

Connecticut Commission on Human Rights and Opportunities

450 Columbus Blvd,

Hartford, CT 06103

Phone: (860) 541-3400

Toll free: (800) 477-5737

TTD: (860) 541-3459

Fax: (860) 246-5265

Online: http://www.ct.gov/chro/site/default.asp

U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services,

Office for Civil Rights

JFK Federal Building, Rm 1875,

Boston, MA 02203

Phone: (617) 565-1340

Toll free: (800) 368-1019

TTY: (800) 537-7697

Fax: (617) 565-3809

Online: http://www.hhs.gov/ocr/office/file/index.html

Discrimination complaints may be submitted to FNS by written letter, or using the USDA complaint form. The AD-3027, Discrimination Complaint Form, is available:

Online: https://www.ascr.usda.gov/ad-3027-usda-program-discrimination-complaint-form

by email request: Cr-info@ascr.usda.gov , or

by phone: (866) 632-9992.

Submit the written letter or complaint form to FNS by:

Email: program.intake@usda.gov

Fax: (202) 690-7442, or

Mail: USDA, Director, Office of the Assistant Secretary for Civil Rights, 1400 Independence Avenue, SW, Washington, DC 20250-9410.

Note: Individuals with disabilities requiring a different way to submit their complaint may call (202) 720-2600 (voice and TTY), or (800) 877-8339.

Civil rights complaints may also be filed with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office of Civil Rights:

Online: https://ocrportal.hhs.gov/ocr/cp/wizard_cp.jsf

Mail: US Dept. of Health and Human Services

200 Independence Avenue, SW Room 509F

HHH Building

Washington, D.C. 20201

Phone: (800) 368-1019 or (800) 537-7697 (TDD)

Individuals wishing to obtain information or file an online or paper civil rights complaint may obtain the information or form at: https://www.hhs.gov/civil-rights/filing-a-complaint/complaint-process/index.html

Investigations are completed by the agencies receiving the complaint.

Record the complaint and the required information .

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation

body:
Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act

Reasonable accommodation means making reasonable changes to the Department’s policies, practices, or procedures to allow an individual with a disability to fully participate in the services, benefits, activities, and programs administered by the Department. Reasonable accommodation includes, but is not limited to:

Changes in the application or renewal process to allow an individual to establish and maintain eligibility.

Completing a home visit .

Providing special machines or interpreters for hearing impaired individuals.

Providing materials in Braille, tape, or large print.

Requesting assistance of a specialized worker.

Reasonable accommodation does not include:

altering the nature of the program,

causing undue fiscal or administrative burden on the Department, or

waiving any eligibility factor.

Advise the individual in writing or verbally that reasonable accommodations are available at:

application,

renewal, and

with any NOA .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation 1
heading: Reasonable Accommodation

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation

body:
CT Process

The individual can request reasonable accommodation in writing or verbally. Applications and renewal applications request information regarding any needed reasonable accommodations.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation 2
heading: Reasonable Accommodation Requests and Determinations

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation

body:
CT Process

Review the accommodation request and ImpaCT to see if the accommodation has been recorded or approved. Advise the individual of any information needed to determine if the request can be completed. Offer assistance to obtain the information if the individual is unable to provide it, and there is no one else who will act on behalf of the individual. Enter case notes for your actions on all reasonable accommodation requests.

Note: Approval by the supervisor or regional manager is required for requests outside of the worker’s control, but within the regional office’s control.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation 3
heading: Worker Determination

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation

body:
CT Process

Contact the ADA Coordinator if you are unsure if the accommodation can be provided, or the accommodation involves a greater administrative or fiscal burden. The ADA Coordinator will acknowledge the request within 10 days. The ADA Coordinator will advise the individual of any information needed to make a determination. The ADA coordinator makes and advises the individual of the determination within 20 days, unless additional information is required.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation 4
heading: ADA Coordinator Determination

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation

body:
CT Process

If the individual disagrees with the ADA Coordinator’s determination, the individual can make a written request, within 15 days of the determination, for review by the Deputy Commissioner for Administration. The Deputy Commissioner for Administration’s decision is final.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation 5
heading: Deputy Commissioner for Administration Decision

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reasonable Accommodation

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Record Retention
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Record Retention

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Record Retention

body:
7 CFR § 272.1(f)(1)-(3)

Keep all case information for no less than 3 years for audit and review purposes.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Record Retention 1
heading: Record Retention

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Record Retention

body:
Keep fiscal records and accountable documents for 3 years from the date of fiscal or administrative closure . Fiscal records and accountable documents include but are not limited to:

Claims

Documentation of lost benefits
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Record Retention 2
heading: 7 CFR § 272.1(f)(1)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Record Retention

body:
7 CFR § 272.1(f)(2)-(3)

Keep IPV records and related notices until:

the individual who committed the IPV dies, or

FNS advises, through eDRS , the individual who committed IPV is 80 years old.

Exception: Delete eDRS records when the information is inaccurate, incomplete, or no longer applies.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Record Retention 3
heading: Intentional Program Violation Records

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
52 U.S.C. § 20501(a)-(b) & 20506(a)(1)-(2)(A)-(B)

The National Voter Registration Act (NVRA) of 1993 designated as voter registration agencies all State agencies providing:

public assistance,

State-funded programs providing services to disabled individuals, and

other state designated offices.

The goal of NVRA is to increase the number of eligible voters and increasing voter participation in elections.

DSS applicants and recipients can register to vote in Connecticut by completing a voter registration application:

online at: http://portal.ct.gov/SOTS/Election-Services/Voter-Information/Voter-Registration-Information , or

mail-in card.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 1
heading: Voter Registration Services

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
52 U.S.C. § 10101(a)(1) & 10701

To register to vote, an individual must :

be a U.S. citizen,

be at least 17-years old, and will turn 18 on or before election day,

completed confinement and parole if convicted of a felony, and

be a resident of Connecticut.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 2
heading: Voter Registration Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
52 U.S.C. § 20506(a)(4)(A)(ii)-(iii) and (5) and (6)(A)(i)-(ii) and (C)

Provide the ED-671A/S or W-614/S, Mail-In Voter Registration Application Card , and ED-682/S, Declination Form , at:

application,

renewal,

name change, and

when a change of address is reported.

Provide the same assistance to complete voter registration forms as you provide for other forms. When providing assistance, do not :

Cause an individual to believe their decision to register or not will affect their eligibility for benefits.

Discourage an individual from registering.

Display any political preference or party allegiance.

Try to influence the individual’s political preference or party registration.

Completed voter registration applications received by DSS are forwarded to the appropriate town hall, within 10 days of receipt, by the Voter Registration Coordinator (VRC) .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 3
heading: Agency Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
52 U.S.C. § 20505(a)-(b) & 20506(a)(6)(A)(i)-(ii) & 20508(a)(2)

Two mail-in versions of the voter registration application are available:

The ED-671/S is kept in stock at all field offices. The form is provided by the Office of the Secretary of State and ordered through the Economic Security Unit.

The W-614/S is mailed by the DSS mail room with all applications and renewal applications.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 4
heading: Mail-In Voter Registration Application Card

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
CT Process

A W-619/S, Receipt of Application, is sent to the individual when a completed voter registration application is received by DSS.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 5
heading: DSS Receipt of Voter Registration Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
52 U.S.C. § 20506(a)(6)(B)(i) and (7)

The ED-682/S, Declination Form, identifies whether or not the individual is registered to vote, and if they wish to register to vote. The ED-682/S is included in applications and renewal applications.

ED-682/S forms are kept for 3 years by the DSS office that received the form.

Note: Information related to declining to register to vote is used only for voter registration purposes, and doesn’t affect benefit eligibility.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 6
heading: Declination Form

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
CT Process

The DSS Scanning Center forwards all completed voter registration applications to the Economic Security Unit. The Economic Security Unit sends:

the voter registration application to the appropriate town hall, and

a W-619/S to the individual who submitted the voter registration application.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 7
heading: DSS Scanning Center

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
CT Process

Send completed voter registration applications received at the regional office to the VRC . The VRC sends:

the voter registration application to the appropriate town hall, and

a W-619/S to the individual who submitted the voter registration application.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 8
heading: DSS Regional Offices

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
CT Process

Service Center staff completing an application during an interview, or recording a change of address, or change of name, must complete the following actions:

Ask the individual if they would like to register to vote.

Encourage the individual to register online using the Secretary of State’s web site at: http://portal.ct.gov/SOTS/Election-Services/Voter-Information/Voter-Registration-Information .

Ensure the ED-682/S page is completed and make a copy of the page.

Back scan the full application/renewal application, including the ED-682/S.

Give the tear off portion of the ED-682/S to the individual.

Send a copy of the ED-682/S to the VRC.

Enter case notes for your actions.

If the individual requests to register to vote in the office, complete the following actions:

Offer assistance to complete the voter registration application .

Give the individual a W-619/S , and the tear off portion of the ED-682/S .

Send the completed voter registration application and the ED-682/S to the VRC. The VRC sends:

completed voter registration application to the appropriate town hall within 10 days of receipt, and

retains the ED-682/S.

Enter case notes for your actions.

The individual may complete the voter registration away from the office. Enter case notes that the individual chose to complete the application away from the office. Advise the individual they can complete the voter registration application:

Online at http://portal.ct.gov/SOTS/Election-Services/Voter-Information/Voter-Registration-Information , or

complete the mail-in voter registration application and:

return to the DSS office, or

send to their local town hall.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 9
heading: Service Center Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
CT Process

Complete the following actions if the individual refuses to complete the form:

Note in the “For DSS Worker’s Use Only” section.

your name,

worker number, and

date.

Tear off the bottom portion of the ED-682/S and give it to the individual.

Send a copy of the ED-682/S to the VRC.

Enter case notes for your actions.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 10
heading: Individual Refuses to Complete the ED-682/S

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
CT Process

Staff in the Processing and Benefit Centers will:

Answer questions from individuals about voter registration.

Mail a voter registration application:

when requested by phone, or

when the ED-682/S indicates a request to register to vote.

Send a copy of the ED-682/S to the field office VRC.

Enter case notes for their actions.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 11
heading: Processing and Benefit Center Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
CT Process

Individuals applying for DSS services online are provided a direct link to the Secretary of State’s website to register to vote: http://portal.ct.gov/SOTS/Election-Services/Voter-Information/Voter-Registration-Information .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 12
heading: Online Applications and Renewals Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
CT Process

Locally print a W-1348, Proofs We Need, and enter one of the following statements:

“You may apply to register to vote online at: http://portal.ct.gov/SOTS/Election-Services/Voter-Information/Voter-Registration-Information , or by returning the enclosed form.”

“Usted puede inscribirse para votar en linea a través del enlace http://portal.ct.gov/SOTS/Election-Services/Voter-Information/Voter-Registration-Information , o devolviendo el formulario adjunto.”

Send the individual the W-1348 and an ED-682/S .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 13
heading: Online Reporting Change of Address or Name Change Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services

body:
CT Process

Individuals who are homeless should use the mailing address of a friend or relative where they can receive mail, or if approved, the DSS office address.

Advise the individual that the Secretary of State offers a Homeless Voter fact sheet under “Important Resources for Voters” at: http://portal.ct.gov/SOTS/Election-Services/Voter-Information/Voter-Fact-Sheet .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services 14
heading: Homeless Individuals Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voter Registration Services
