citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(a) & (c)(1)-(4)

The W-1E , Application for Benefits, must be readily available to potentially eligible households. Provide an application to anyone who requests an application. Mail the application to the household no later than the next work day when requested by phone.

Encourage the household to file an application the day they contact DSS requesting information about SNAP. Tell the household that applying for SNAP does not affect time limits for other programs.

Households can apply:

in-person at any field office ,

by mail by sending a W-1E , Application, to the DSS Scanning Center, or

online at: https://connect.ct.gov/access/jsp/accessThe/Home.jsp .

Note: Offer the household a copy of their completed application, regardless of how they apply. Offer the household the option to receive the copy in paper or an electronic format.

Workers must provide assistance, when requested, to help applicants and recipients:

complete the application

obtain verifications if needed, and

register to vote. See Voter Registration Services .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application 1
heading: Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application

body:
Source # 17

Effective Date: 10/01/2025
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application 2
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(a)(2) & (d)(1)

Applicants must complete the following actions as part of the application process:

file and complete the application,

be interviewed, and

verify all required information.

Deny the application if the household refuses to cooperate with any of these actions.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process 1
heading: Application Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process

body:
7 CFR 273.2(b)(1)(v) and (c)(1)

The application process begins when the household files an application for SNAP benefits with, at a minimum, the:

name,

address, and

signature of a responsible household member or authorized representative (A-Reps).

Note : Electronic signatures through electronic transmissions are acceptable. Applications signed by a “mark” must also have the signature of a witness.

Return applications missing any of the 3 minimum requirements to the applicant for completion. Obtain any other missing information needed to complete the application during the interview.

Application for SNAP may be made by any of the following individuals:

Head of household

Other responsible household member

Authorized representative (A-Rep)

See Authorized Representative for A-Rep requirements.

See Special Situations for A-Rep requirements related to these households.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process 2
heading: Filing and Completing the Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process

body:
7 CFR 273.2(c)(1)(iv) and (vi)(A)

The application filing date is the date an application with name, address, and signature is received by

DSS

the DSS Scanning Center, or

Social Security office.

Applications dropped off in field offices must be date-stamped with the application filing date.

Applications received after business hours have a filing date of the next business day. This rule applies to applications that are dropped off, mailed in, or submitted online.

Example: an online application submitted at 6:00 PM on a Friday will have a filing date of the following Monday (if Monday is a holiday then the filing date is Tuesday).

Exception: Pre-release Institution Resident Applying for SSI and SNAP .

Accept applications with only the minimum requirements and record the date of the applicant’s release as the file date.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process 3
heading: Application File Date

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process

body:
7 CFR 273.2(c)(1) & (i)(2)

On the day the application is received

register the application, and

screen for expedited service .

Note: Online and mailed applications are automatically screened for expedited eligibility based on the applicant’s response to screening questions.

Review eligibility system to see if the household members are known.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process 4
heading: Registering the Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process

body:
7 CFR 273.2(c)(6)

Households may withdraw their application at any time before their eligibility is determined. Workers must :

Contact the household to confirm the withdrawal,

Record:

the contact with the household, and

the reason for withdrawal, and

Send a notice to the household to advise the:

application was denied due to the withdrawal request, and

the household may reapply.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process 5
heading: Withdrawal of the Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process

body:
7 CFR 273.2(b)(3)

Households may use the same W-1E , Application and apply at the same time for SNAP, Medicaid , SAGA , SSUP , or TFA .

Complete a single interview for households jointly applying.

Process SNAP benefits following the required SNAP policies and timeframes. Do not delay processing SNAP because another program is pending.

Households are not required to file another application for SNAP when their application for another program is denied. SNAP cannot be denied solely because of denial of the other program’s application.

Process SNAP benefits without including cash benefits when it’s uncertain the cash benefits will be approved or the amount is unknown.

Process SNAP to include the cash benefit when that program is approved.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process 6
heading: Joint Applications for Agency Programs

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process

body:
7 CFR 273.2(k)(1)(i)(A)-(P) and (ii)(A)-(H) & SSA SI BOS01801.300 (TN 5-253 — 07/2005)

Households whose members are all applying for, or recipients of SSI , may apply for SNAP through SSA if they:

haven’t applied in the last 30 days, and

do not have any pending applications .

The application file date for applications through SSA is the date the signed application is received by SSA.

SSA sends completed applications and supporting documentation to DSS within one business day.

Expedited service processing timeframes begin on the date DSS receives the application.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process 7
heading: Application through Social Security Administration

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process

body:
7 CFR 273.1(b)(7)(vi) & 7 CFR 273.2(c)(1) and (g)(1) & (i)(3)(i)

The application file date for residents of an institution jointly filing for SSI and SNAP is the date the resident is released from the institution.

Processing timeframes are based on the resident’s release date. Eligible households receive initial month benefits from the date of release to the end of the month.

SSA will notify the agency of the date of release from the institution. Restore SNAP benefits to the date of release, if eligible, when SSA doesn’t provide timely notification of the release date.

Workers are not required to complete a 2nd interview (if SSA has completed an initial interview) unless needed to resolve issues or questions.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process 8
heading: Pre-release Institution Resident Applying for SSI and SNAP

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Process

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(a)(2) & (c)(1) & (d)(1) & (e)(1)

An interview must be completed at application.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews 1
heading: Interviews

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews 2
heading: Scheduling

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(1) and (3)

Complete the interview the day the application is filed. If unable to complete the interview the same day, promptly schedule an on-demand interview.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews 3
heading: Applications in Person

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews

body:
CT Waiver of 7 CFR § 273.2(e)(3)

After the application is registered in the eligibility system, make 2 attempts to cold call the applicant to complete the interview.

Send a W-3015N, Interview Notice if the household cannot be reached after 2 attempts. The notice advises the household to call the Benefit Center, using the toll free number, Monday through Friday, between 8:00 am and 4:00 pm, to complete an interview, or to schedule an in-person interview. Schedule the on-demand interview window to ensure completion of the interview no later than 12 days from the date of processing.

Exception: For households qualifying for expedited service , schedule the on-demand interview window within 7 days from the date of application.

Note: A 2nd interview is not required if SSA completed the interview and provided a complete application, unless needed to resolve issues or questions.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews 4
heading: Applications Dropped off, Mailed, Submitted Online, or Sent from SSA

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(1)

Hold the interview with one of the following individuals:

Head of household

Spouse

Other responsible household member; or

Authorized representative

Note: Individuals may bring anyone they choose to the interview.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews 5
heading: Who to Interview

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(1) and (2)

Complete the interview by telephone.

Exception : Complete a face-to-face interview for the following reasons:

Household member or A-Rep requests a face-to-face interview

In-person application

Needed to resolve or clarify specific issues that cannot be done over the phone

Face-to-face interviews may be completed at:

a DSS field office ,

a mutually acceptable location, or

the applicant’s residence.

Note: The interview must be scheduled in advance if conducted at the applicant’s residence.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews 6
heading: How to Complete the Interview

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(1)-(3) and (f)

Review the eligibility system for household information known to the agency. Treat the interview as a confidential discussion of the household’s situation.

Review the application by:

going over each question, and

resolving any unclear or missing information with the individual being interviewed.

Advise the applicant of the following information:

Rights and Responsibilities

Reporting Requirements:

Income going over 130% FPL

Time limits for able-bodied adults without dependents (ABAWD), if applicable

Lottery or Gambling winnings in a single game

Processing timeframes

Rules and requirements of other programs don’t apply to the receipt of SNAP

General Work Requirements, including SNAP Employment & Training (SNAP E&T)

Provide the applicant with:

Basic SNAP facts

A W-1348, Proofs We Need, listing the required verifications needed to determine eligibility and the due date to return the verifications.

Eligibility Determination Document (EDD)

Informational materials

Return envelope

Verification forms

Advise the applicant required actions and verifications must be completed and returned within 10 days of the interview.

Make referrals for other services as needed.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews 7
heading: Interview Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(3)

A W-0108N, Notice of Missed Interview (NOMI), is sent to households failing to complete an interview by the 12th day.

Exception: See Expedited Service, Interview .

The NOMI advises the household that:

they have not completed their interview,

they must call to complete their interview or their application will be denied,

the days, work hours and phone number to call the Benefit Center, and

the date by which the interview must be completed.

Households requesting to reschedule their interview by the 30th day are not processed as an Household caused delay if by the 30th day they:

complete the interview, and

provide all verifications required to determine eligibility.

The application is denied on the 30th day following the application file date if the household fails to complete the interview by the 30th day and does not reschedule.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews 8
heading: Missed Interview

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Interviews

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Refusal to Cooperate
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Refusal to Cooperate

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Refusal to Cooperate

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(d)(1) and (2)

Deny households refusing to cooperate in completing the application process at the time of refusal.

Note : Disqualified and Ineligible Members are part of the household and must cooperate.

Refusal to cooperate means the household is able to cooperate, but shows the worker they will not take required actions to complete the application process.

Example : A household telling you they will not be interviewed shows refusal to cooperate. A household missing their scheduled interview is not a refusal to cooperate, and the application must not be denied for refusal.

Individuals are not found uncooperative when someone outside of the household fails to provide requested verifications. Example : An employer.

See Disqualification for Refusal to Cooperate for information about cooperation with Quality Control.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Refusal to Cooperate 1
heading: Refusal to Cooperate

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Refusal to Cooperate

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(6)

Record the following information when a household refuses to cooperate:

the action the household was asked to take,

the reason the action is required to determine eligibility,

the household is capable of completing the action,

how the agency informed the household of the actions they need to take, and the dates they were informed, and

the household has indicated verbally or in writing that it chooses not to take the action.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Refusal to Cooperate 2
heading: Record Refusal

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Refusal to Cooperate

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(a)(2) & (g)

Review the household’s:

financial and non-financial eligibility requirements,

data match information , and

verifications.

Enter in the elgibility system:

case notes about the actions taken,

information from the interview, and

new or updated household information.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application 1
heading: Processing the Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application 2
heading: Fraud Early Detection (FRED) Investigation

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(g)

Process applications as soon as possible when all requirements are met, but no later than 30-calendar days from the application file date.

Exception: See Expedited Service, Processing Timeframe .

See Delayed Processing if the eligibility determination was not completed within 30 days.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application 3
heading: Processing Timeframes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(h)(1)(i)(A)-(D) & 7 CFR § 273.10(g)(1)(iii)

Review determinations of eligibility not completed before the 30th day for the cause of the delay. Complete the review:

on the 30th day from the application date, or

the first business day after the 30th day when it falls on a weekend or holiday.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application 4
heading: Delayed Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(h)(1)(i)(A)-(D) and (ii) & (3)(i)-(ii) & (4)(i)-(ii)

Continue pending the application until eligibility is determined. Do not deny the application when delayed due to the agency or SSA.

Send the household a pending status notice on the 30th day, or the next work day when the 30th day falls on a weekend or holiday.

Determine eligibility as soon as possible, and if eligible, restore benefits from the application date.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application 5
heading: Agency Caused Delays

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(h)(1)(i)(A)-(D) and (2)(i)–(ii) and (4)(iii)

Delays are the fault of the household if the household failed to complete the application process and the worker cannot take any further action. Deny the application on the 30th day.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application 6
heading: Household Caused Delays

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(h)(2)(i)(A) and (ii)

Reactivate the application and determine eligibility when the household completes required actions 31 to 60 days from the application file date.

Note: A new application is not required.

Complete the eligibility determination as soon as possible, but no later than the 60th day from the application file date.

Issue benefits from the date the required actions were completed, if eligible. Benefits are not issued for the month of application when the household caused the delay.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application 7
heading: Household Completes Actions on Denied Applications 31 to 60 Days from Application File Date

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing the Application

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(g)

Complete the eligibility determination as soon as possible, but no later than 30 days after the application file date .

Exception: See Expedited Service, Processing Timeframes .

When eligibility determinations aren’t completed within 30 days, Follow Agency Caused Delays or Actions Completed on Denied Applications 31 to 60 Days from Application File .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination 1
heading: Eligibility Determination

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(g)(1)

Provide eligible households the opportunity to receive benefits as soon as possible, but no later than 30-calendar days from the application file date.

See Benefits for issuance information for:

Initial Month

Combined Benefits

Prorated Benefits

Electronic Benefit Transfer
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination 2
heading: Approval

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(a)(1)(iii)(3)

Households eligible for the initial month but not eligible for future months receive benefits for the initial month only.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination 3
heading: Eligible Initial Month Only

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(a)(1)(iii)(3)

Households ineligible for the initial month but eligible for the next month do not need to reapply. Begin the household’s benefit period the month of application, but issue benefits beginning the next month. Do not issue benefits for the initial month.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination 4
heading: Ineligible for Initial Month Only

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(g)(3)

Deny the application for households found ineligible when the determination is made but no later than 30-calendar days from the application file date.

In all cases of denial due to ineligibility, the household must file a new application if they wish to receive benefits.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination 5
heading: Denial

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(g)(3) & (h)(2)(i)(A)

Deny the application on the 30th calendar day from the application file date due to household caused delays.

If the household completes the required actions within 31 to 60 days from the filing date, and is not eligible, deny when the ineligibility determination is made.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination 6
heading: Household Caused Delay

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(h)(3)(ii)

Deny the application as soon as the ineligibility determination is made.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination 7
heading: Agency Caused Delay

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligibility Determination

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Periods
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Periods

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Periods

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i) & 7 CFR § 273.10(f)(1) and (3) & 7 CFR 273.12(a)(5)(iii)

Provide the longest benefit period possible based on the household’s situation.

Coordinate cash assistance benefit periods whenever possible.

Assign a 36-month benefit period to households where all members are:

elderly or disabled , and

have no earned income .

Households who do not qualify for a 36-month benefit period receive a 12-month benefit period.

See Transitional Benefits Alternative (TBA) for TBA benefit period requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Periods 1
heading: Benefit Periods

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Periods

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notices
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notices

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notices

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notices 1
heading: Notices

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notices

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(g)(1)(i)(A)-(C)

Provide a W-0001N, Notice of Action, when the household is determined eligible for benefits.

Provide the following information in the approval notice:

benefit amount,

benefit level changes at approval,

benefit period,

combined benefit amounts when appropriate,

contact name and number for questions,

fair hearing rights,

legal services availability,

other information deemed necessary, and

requirement to reapply at the end of the benefit period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notices 2
heading: Approval Notices

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notices

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(g)(1)(ii)

Provide a W-0001N, Notice of Action, when the household is determined ineligible for benefits.

Provide the following information in the denial notice:

the reason for the denial,

their right to a fair hearing,

their right to reapply,

contact information for questions, and

the actions the household must take to reactivate the application within 30 days of the notice date when denied for not completing required actions.

Note: A new application is required if:

the application is not reactivated within 30 days of the notice date, or

when denied as SNAP ineligible.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notices 3
heading: Denial Notices

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notices

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verifications
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verifications

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verifications

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(i)-(xiv) and (2)(i)-(ii) and (3)(i)-(ii)

The applicant must provide required verifications before approving their application.

Exception : Expedited Service .

See individual items for verification requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verifications 1
heading: Verifications

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verifications

body:
See Verification Requirements by Element and Action

See Suggested Verification Sources
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verifications 2
heading: Resources

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verifications

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(6)

Record in the eligibility system the actions taken for the determination, including:

cold-call dates and times,

eligibility and benefit level,

ineligibility,

interview notes,

issues and questionable information,

verification sources, and

when processing is delayed for any reason.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes 1
heading: Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service

body:
7 CFR § 272.4(e) & 7 CFR § 273.2(i) and (3)(v) & 7 CFR § 273.14(f)

Expedited service allows eligible CG s to quickly receive an initial benefit with fewer required verifications . All SNAP requirements must be completed before providing future months’ benefits.

Expedited service applies for the month of application only .

Exception: Combined benefits for the month of application and the next month are issued, if eligible, when the EDG applies after the 15th of the month.

EDGs are not eligible for expedited service at renewal when they reapply before the end of the current benefit period.

EDGs are not eligible for expedited service if they received SNAP benefits in another CG or another state in the current month. Follow processing timeframes to issue benefits for the 2nd month, if eligible.

Exception: EDGs in shelters for battered individuals can receive benefits in the same month, even if they received SNAP benefits in another CG or another state in the current month.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service 1
heading: Expedited Service

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service

body:
Source #

Effective Date:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service 2
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Screening Requirements
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Screening Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Screening Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(1)(i)-(iii) and (2)

Screen applications submitted in-office the same day to determine eligibility for expedited service. Screen online and paper applications that have requested SNAP, and indicated that they are eligible for expedited service, as soon as possible. Review ImpaCT to see if the EDG is known and whether they receive or have received benefits in Connecticut.

EDGs must meet one of the following criteria to receive expedited service:

Anticipate receiving less than $150 countable gross income in the month of application, and their liquid assets are not more than $100.

Combined monthly countable gross income and liquid assets are less than the EDG’s monthly:

rent or mortgage payment, and

utility expenses using the appropriate utility standard .

Migrant or seasonal farmworker EDGs who:

are destitute , and

have less than $100 in liquid assets.

Note : Use the liquid asset amount when screening for expedited service even if assets are excluded following categorical eligibility policy.

Exception: An EDG may meet the financial screening criteria for expedited service, but may have other circumstances that make them ineligible for expedited service or SNAP benefits. The most common reason is the applicant is an ABAWD who used up the initial 3 months of benefits and there is no information to indicate they are exempt or have met the work requirement since then. Follow Applications policy in these situations. See Examples, Expedited Service .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Screening Requirements 1
heading: Screening Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Screening Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(k)(1)(i)(I)-(J)

DSS staff must screen the application for expedited service the day the application is received from:

SSA, or

the EDG as directed by SSA.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Screening Requirements 2
heading: SSA Screening of Joint SSI and SNAP Applications

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Screening Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(v)

EDGs not eligible for expedited service follow normal 30-day application processing .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Screening Requirements 3
heading: EDG Not Eligible for Expedited Service

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Screening Requirements

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(a)(2) and (d)(1) and (e) and (i)(4)(i)(A)

EDGs must complete the following minimum actions for expedited service:

complete the application by answering all application questions (this may be done during the interview),

verify identity unless known to ImpaCT, and

complete an interview .

See Postponed Actions for actions the EDG must complete in order to receive continued benefits beyond the first or 2nd month.

Note: See Reapplication After Expedited Service if the EDG’s last approval was expedited.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions 1
heading: Expedited Service Minimum Actions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(vii) and (i)(4)(A)

EDGs qualifying for expedited service must verify the identity of the individual applying, if not already known. Verification of identity cannot be postponed. See Identity for requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions 2
heading: Verification of Identity

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)

An interview must be completed before expedited service benefits are issued.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions 3
heading: Interview

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(1) and (3)

Complete an in-person interview on the application file date , or upon request.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions 4
heading: Applications In-Person

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions

body:
CT Waiver of 7 CFR § 273.2(e)(3)

After the application is registered in ImpaCT and identified as eligible for expedited service, make 2 attempts to cold call the EDG to complete the interview.

Send an On-Demand Interview Notice if the EDG cannot be reached after 2 attempts. The notice advises the EDG to call the Benefit Center, using the toll free number, Monday through Friday, between 8:00 AM and 4:00 PM, to complete an interview, or to schedule an in-person interview. The EDG must call within 7 days.

Note: A 2nd interview is not required if SSA completed the interview and provided a complete application and verification of identity (if required), unless needed to resolve issues or questions.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions 5
heading: Applications Dropped off, Mailed, Submitted Online, or Sent from SSA

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions

body:
CT Waiver of 7 CFR § 273.2(i)(3)(iii) and (4)(iii)(A)-(B)

EDGs who don’t complete an interview and identity was not verified by the 7th-calendar day are automatically sent a NOMI.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions 6
heading: Interview Not Completed and Identity Not Verified

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(a)(2) and (i)(4)(i)(A)

EDGs lose their entitlement to expedited service if they fail to complete the minimum actions within the 7-calendar-day timeframe. Follow normal 30-day processing timeframes.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions 7
heading: Minimum Actions Not Completed

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expedited Service Minimum Actions

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(i)(B)

Although all verifications except identity can be postponed, attempt to verify as many SNAP eligibility factors as possible within the processing timeframes . Review data matches on each adult EDG member to ensure they are eligible. However, do not delay benefits once the expedited service minimum actions are completed.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors 1
heading: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(i)(B)

Work registration is completed by signing the application.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors 2
heading: Work Registration

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(iii)(A)

EDGs must complete all postponed actions by the SOP in accordance with traditional processing timeframes . Verification requirements may be postponed until the second month of SNAP participation.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors 3
heading: Postponed Actions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(iii)(A)-(B) & 7 CFR § 273.10(g)(1)(i)(A)-(B)

Issue a Conditional Approval notice advising the EDG that benefits will not continue after the first or 2nd month until all postponed actions are completed. The notice must contain the following information:

benefits will end if all postponed actions are not completed,

what actions are still needed,

the due date when the actions must be completed, and

changes in benefits based on completed actions are made without advance notice .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors 4
heading: Notice

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(iii)(D)

The case automatically ends after the first or 2nd month if the EDG fails to complete the postponed actions within 30 days of the application file date . No further notice is required.

Exception: The EDG must complete all actions by end of the 2nd month to receive continued benefits when the application is filed after the 15th of the month.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors 5
heading: EDG Fails to Complete Postponed Actions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification of Other Eligibility Factors

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing Timeframes
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing Timeframes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing Timeframes

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(3)(i)-(iv) & 7 CFR § 273.10(a)(1)(i)-(ii) and (3)

Do not delay issuing expedited benefits if the EDG has other programs pending.

Issue benefits when the EDG completes at least the minimum actions so benefits can be accessed no later than 7-calendar days from the application file date. The 7-day timeframe begins the day after the application file date .

Exception: If eligibility for expedited service is not determined at initial screening, the 7-day processing timeframe begins on the date the Department discovers the EDG is entitled to expedited service. See Other Application File Dates .

CGs not eligible for benefits the first month of application, but eligible the 2nd month qualifying for expedited service, must have benefits issued:

within 7-calendar days of the application file date, or

the first day of the 2nd month, whichever is later.

Send the appropriate notice by the 7th-calendar day to advise the EDG of their eligibility determination.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing Timeframes 1
heading: Processing Timeframes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing Timeframes

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(k)(1)(i)(F)

The application file date for expedited service SNAP applications received at SSA is the date the application is received by SSA. SSA will forward SNAP applications within one day of receipt. The 7-day processing timeframe begins the day after the application is received by DSS.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing Timeframes 2
heading: Joint SSI and SNAP Applications Completed at SSA

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing Timeframes

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(3)(i) and (k)(1)(i)(F) & 7 CFR 273.10(a)(1)(ii)

The application file date is the date the resident is released from the institution for joint SSI and SNAP applications, if submitted by a resident of an institution prior to their release. SSA provides notification to DSS of the resident’s date of release.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing Timeframes 3
heading: Pre-Release Institution Residents

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing Timeframes

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(3)(iv)

Provide expedited service within 7-calendar days of the date of discovery when the application:

was screened as not meeting expedited service requirements, and

later found eligible for expedited service.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing Timeframes 4
heading: Late Determination of Expedited Service Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Processing Timeframes

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(3) and (iii) & (i)(4)(iii)(A)-(B)

Provide the longest benefit period possible based on the EDG’s situation.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits 1
heading: Benefit Periods

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits 2
heading: Benefit Issuance

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(iii)(A)

EDGs filing their application before the 15th of the month receive benefits for the initial month .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits 3
heading: Initial Benefit

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(iii)(B)-(C)

EDGs filing their application after the 15th of the month, and found eligible for the initial month and 2nd month, receive a combined benefit .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits 4
heading: Combined Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reapplication After Expedited Service
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reapplication After Expedited Service

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reapplication After Expedited Service

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(iii)(A)-(B)

The 30-day application processing timeframe applies when an EDG that failed to complete postponed actions reapplies for benefits.

Exception: Provide expedited service if the EDG completes the postponed actions and is eligible.

Approve EDGs for expedited service without limits if the EDG:

completes the postponed actions from the last expedited approval, or

was approved under normal 30-day timeframes since the last expedited service approval.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reapplication After Expedited Service 1
heading: Reapplication After Expedited Service

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reapplication After Expedited Service

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes

body: Examples, Expedited Service .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes 1
heading: Resources

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes

body:
7 CFR § 273.2 (f)(6)

Record in ImpaCT the actions taken for the expedited service determination, including:

cold-call dates and times,

ineligibility,

interview notes,

issues and questionable information,

verification sources, and

when processing is delayed for any reason.

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(b)(1)(i) and (f)

Verification is used to confirm the accuracy of statements or information. Verification may be provided:

by mail,

in-person,

over the phone for collateral contact verifications, or

uploaded on ConneCT through MyAccount .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 1
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(d)(1) and (f)(5)

EDGs have the primary responsibility to provide verification to support statements on the application and to resolve any questionable information.

Take the following actions when the EDG fails to provide required verification, or respond to a request for verification without good cause :

Applications: Deny for failure to verify; or approve benefits without the expense.

Recipients: Close the case; or adjust benefits as specified for the eligibility item that changed.

Enter case notes about why the action was taken.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 2
heading: EDG Responsibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(5)

Workers must offer to help the EDG get verification when the EDG reports difficulty in getting verification, or requests help.

Attempt to secure all verification necessary to grant or deny an applicant at first contact. This is known as “first touch resolution”. First touch resolution saves time by eliminating the need for the applicant to follow up with additional contacts.

Complete a form W-1348, Proofs We Need, if there is missing information is needed to determine eligibility.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 3
heading: Agency Assistance

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(b)(5) and (f)(1)-(4)

Workers may discuss the verifications needed with the EDG, but must follow-up in writing using a W-1348, Verifications We Need Checklist. The W-1348 must include the due date to provide the verification. Allow the EDG at least 10-days to return the verification. CT best practice is to include the specific information being requested on the W-1348.

Avoid requesting unnecessary verifications, denying applications, or closing cases when verifications are already available from:

automated sources,

scanning, or

written copy.

See Verification Requirements by Element and Activity for requirements .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 4
heading: Request for Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(4)

Sources of verification include:

Written evidence

Collateral contacts

Home visits

Automated Resources

Refer to Suggested Verification Sources for verification examples .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources 1
heading: Verification Sources

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(4)(i)

Written evidence is the most common verification of the EDG’s circumstances, such as:

Award letter

Employer statement

Rental agreement

Wage stubs
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources 2
heading: Written Evidence

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(4)(ii)

Collateral contact is an acceptable verification if written evidence isn’t available. A collateral contact is a verbal confirmation of the EDG’s circumstances by an individual outside of the EDG. Make the collateral contact by phone or in-person with anyone who can give reliable information. Acceptable collateral contacts include, but are not limited to:

Employers

Landlords

Dependent care providers

Community Service agencies
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources 3
heading: Collateral Contacts

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(4)(iii)

Home visits are also an acceptable verification if written evidence or collateral contacts are not enough to make an eligibility determination. Home visits must always be scheduled in advance with the EDG. Note why the home visit was completed in case notes.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources 4
heading: Home Visits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

body:
7 CFR § 272.1(c)(1)-(2) & 7 CFR § 272.8 & 7 CFR § 272.11 & 7 CFR 272.12 & 7 CFR 272.13 & 7 CFR 272.14 & 7 CFR 272.16 & 7 CFR § 273.2(f)(4)

Use automated resources for verification, whenever possible. However:

workers may request additional verification for questionable information, or

give the EDG the opportunity to provide other verification if they disagree with the automated information.

Do not delay timely approval of benefits if automated resources are unavailable.

Run automated matches for only the following members to obtain information on income and resources to determine EDG eligibility:

applicants,

recipients,

ineligible members , and

disqualified EDG members .

Enter case notes for all actions taken on any automated resource.

Take action on automated match information within 10 days of receipt.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources 5
heading: Automated Resources

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

body:
7 CFR § 272.14

The deceased individual match is completed through SVES , BENDEX , or SDX .

Run the match at:

application,

12-month renewal, or

12-month periodic report for EDGs with 24-month benefit periods.

Send a Notice of Match Results to the EDG requesting information on their household situation. Include the following required information in the notice:

What information the EDG must provide regarding the individual identified as deceased, and

What will occur if the EDG fails to respond to the notice.

Act accordingly if the EDG responds and clarifies their household situation.

If the EDG fails to respond, or refuses to provide information to clarify its situation, remove the individual and their income, and adjust benefits. Issue a NOA advising the EDG of the actions taken.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources 6
heading: Deceased Individual Match

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(11) & 7 CFR § 273.16(i)(2)(ii) and (4)(i)

eDRS is a nationwide database of individuals disqualified for an Intentional Program Violation (IPV) . It is used to prevent participation of individuals IPV disqualified by another State.

Complete a match for each EDG member 18 years of age or older.

Run the match at:

application,

renewal, and

when adding a new EDG member.

Verify any match information received before:

approving an application, or

disqualifying the individual.

Note: This is a 2-step process. Verification is Step 1. Disqualification and required notice is Step 2.

Immediately contact the EDG and/or the State agency that originated the disqualification record to obtain verification that confirms the disqualification record in eDRS. Verification includes but is not limited to electronic or hard copies of:

Administrative disqualification hearing decisions

Court decisions

Signed administrative disqualification hearing waivers

Signed disqualification consent agreements

Other official documents that confirm the disqualification

Exception: If electronic or hard copies are not available, accept a verbal or written statement from the:

originating State agency stating one of the documents exists, or

household confirming the accuracy of the disqualification information.

Scan the documents or written statement into case notes. If verbal confirmation was used, provide the collateral contact information.

After verification is received, enter the disqualification information in ImpaCT and send the required notices. See Intentional Program Violation, Disqualification .

Note: Process the case without the disqualification if the verification is not received. Complete an overpayment if verification is received after benefit approval and the individual should have been disqualified.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources 7
heading: Electronic Disqualified Recipient System (eDRS)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

body:
7 CFR § 272.8 & 7 CFR § 273.2(b)(2) and (f)(7) and (9)(i)-(v)

IEVS has the following matches using the applicant's or recipient's SSN to obtain income and asset information:

Care4Kids child care payment information

Connecticut Child Support Enforcement System (CCSES) child support information

Connecticut Department of Labor

Wage Information

Unemployment Compensation Benefits (UCB)

Connecticut Workers’ Compensation Commission (WC)

SSA Beneficiary and Earnings Data Exchange (BENDEX)

Medicare Part A and Part B payer and premium amount

Retirement Survivors and Disability Insurance (RSDI) benefits

SSN Enumeration Validation

State Data Exchange (SDX)

Supplemental Security Income (SSI)

Internal Revenue Services (IRS)

1099 income: Income reported to IRS that is not from wages such as, but not limited to:

annuities

cash prizes

contract income

dividends

interest

pensions

royalties

State Verification Exchange System (SVES)

40-qualifying work quarters information

Black Lung payment

Citizenship

Social Security income

Social Security numbers

Supplemental Security Income (SSI)

State Wage Information Collection Agencies (SWICA)

Run the match on EDG members at:

application,

renewal, and

when adding a new EDG member.

The following matches are verified upon receipt :

BENDEX

Care4Kids

CCSES

Connecticut Department of Labor UCB information

Connecticut Workers’ Compensation Commission WC information

SDX

SSN Enumeration/Validation

For matches not verified upon receipt, verify any match information received before:

approving an application, or

taking action on an active case.

Complete all actions no later than 45 days after receipt of IEVS information.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources 8
heading: Income Eligibility and Verification System (IEVS)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

body:
7 CFR § 272.16

A match on EDG members 18 years of age or older from a national directory that identifies wage and unemployment information.

Run the match on adult EDG members at application and renewal.

Verify any match information received before approving an application or renewal.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources 9
heading: National Directory of New Hires (NDNH)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

body:
7 CFR § 272.13

A match to identify EDG members 18 years of age or older who are incarcerated.

Run the match on adult EDG members at application and renewal.

Send a Notice of Match Results to the EDG requesting information on their household situation. Include the following required information in the notice:

What information the EDG must provide regarding the individual identified as incarcerated.

What will occur if the EDG fails to respond to the notice.

Act accordingly if the EDG responds and clarifies their household situation.

If the EDG fails to respond, or refuses to provide information to clarify its situation, remove the individual and their income, and adjust benefits. Issue a NOA advising the EDG of the actions taken.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources 10
heading: Prisoner Verification System (PVS) Match

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

body:
7 CFR § 272.11 & 7 CFR § 273.2(f)(10)(i)-(v)

SAVE is a program verifying the validity of EDG members’ non-citizen status. After the USCIS form for the non-citizen is received, use the SAVE system to verify the form is accurate.

Exception : American Indians may not be listed on SAVE.

Run the match at:

application,

reported changes in status, and

when adding a new EDG member who is a non-citizen.

Complete USCIS Form G-845 , for secondary verification when:

the non-citizen’s status was not verified through SAVE, or

there are differences in the SAVE data and the information submitted by the non-citizen.

Submit the G-845 and photocopies of the non-citizen’s forms to USCIS for secondary verification.

Enter in case notes:

why you used the G-845,

the date the G-845 and photocopies were sent to USCIS, and

USCIS’ response.

Scan the G-845 into the case record.

SAVE information is verified upon receipt .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources 11
heading: Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements (SAVE)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification Sources

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes

body:
7 CFR § 272.8(e) & 7 CFR § 273.1(d)(2) & 7 CFR § 273.2(c)(1) and (6) & (f)(6) and (10)(v) and (11)(iii)(C) & 7 CFR § 273.8(c) & 7 CFR § 273.11(g) and (n)(4) & 7 CFR § 273.12(c) & 7 CFR § 273.17(h) & 7 CFR § 274.2(h)(3) & 7 CFR § 274.6(a)(7)(i)

The goal of case notes is to establish a clear and complete record that follows the case actions. Complete case notes:

to support eligibility, ineligibility, and benefit level determinations, and

whenever a manual notice (Notice W3016) is provided.

Provide a clear explanation of what you did and why you did it, including:

Information used to determine eligibility and benefit amount including:

the source of information;

the verification supporting the information;

the method used to determine monthly income and expense amounts such as conversion, averaging, etc.; and

why the information was used.

Actions taken and the reasons to support those actions, such as, but not limited to:

Interviews: who was interviewed, when the interview was completed, how completed (home visit, in-person, phone).

Collateral Contacts: who the collateral contact is, the phone number, date of the contact, and the information the collateral contact provided.

Questionable Information: Why was it questionable and how it was resolved.

Differences: Resolve any differences between information in the application, provided at the interview, from an automated source, or verification.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes 1
heading: Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Household Composition and the EDG/CG
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Household Composition and the EDG/CG

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Household Composition and the EDG/CG

body:
7 CFR § 273.1

To determine who is non-financially eligible for SNAP you must :

Start with everyone who lives together.

Determine who purchases and prepares (shares food) together for home consumption.

Apply relationship rules .

Apply non-financial eligibility rules to determine who is an eligible or excluded member.

No one can be an eligible member of 2 or more certified groups (CG) in any month.

Exception: Residents of shelters for battered individuals , provided the battered individual was a member of the same household as the individual who abused them .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Household Composition and the EDG/CG 1
heading: Household Composition and the EDG/CG

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Household Composition and the EDG/CG

body:
Source #3

Effective Date: 5/15/19
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Household Composition and the EDG/CG 2

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Living Together
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Living Together

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Living Together

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(a)

Everyone who lives together is a household.

Living together means sharing a home where individuals usually sleep and share any common living spaces such as kitchen, bathroom, bedroom or living room. Individuals who share only an access area such as an entrance or hallway or non-living areas such as a laundry room or garage are not considered living together.

Evaluate everyone living together to determine who must be included in the SNAP eligibility determination group (EDG) .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Living Together 1
heading: Living Together

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Living Together

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(c)

An individual who is temporarily absent from the household is considered living with the household. An individual’s absence is temporary if all the following are true:

the individual’s location is known,

the individual lived with the household before the absence (consider newborns to have lived with the household),

there is a definite plan for return, and

the absence lasted or is expected to last 30 days or less.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Living Together 2
heading: Temporary Absence

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Living Together

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Purchase and Prepare
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Purchase and Prepare

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Purchase and Prepare

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(a)(1)-(3)

Individuals who live together and share food must be included in the SNAP EDG . Individuals share food if they:

share in the cost of buying food,

share in preparing the food, regardless of who paid for it, or

eat from the same food supply, regardless of who paid for it.

Types of EDGs are:

An individual living alone.

A group of individuals living together who share food.

An individual (or group of individuals) living with others, but who do not share food with the others.

Note: The intent to purchase food and prepare meals separately also establishes separate EDG status, even if the intent depends on receiving SNAP.

Exception : Individuals identified in Relationships must be in the same SNAP EDG with the other individuals they live with, even if they purchase/prepare separately.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Purchase and Prepare 1
heading: Purchase and Prepare

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Purchase and Prepare

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(2)

An individual and their spouse together may be a separate EDG , even if they purchase and prepare with others, if all of the following are true:

One of them is 60 years or older; and

That individual cannot purchase and prepare meals because of:

a disability considered permanent by SSA , or

another permanent physical or mental non-disease related disability; and

The gross monthly income for the others in the household does not exceed 165% of the federal poverty level .

Discuss the following options with the household:

Apply as one EDG with all household members; or

Apply as a separate EDG (with spouse).
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Purchase and Prepare 2
heading: Elderly and Unable to Purchase/Prepare Food

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Purchase and Prepare

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(1) and (4)

Individuals in the following relationships must be included in the SNAP EDG .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships 1
heading: Relationships

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(1)(i)

Individuals legally married and living together must be in the same EDG.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships 2
heading: Spouses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(1)(ii)

Children include natural, step and adopted children.

Parents and their children 21 years of age and younger who live together must be in the same EDG even if the children have their own spouse or child who lives in the same household.

Note: The step-parent relationship ends when the natural or adoptive parent dies or is divorced from the step-parent. However, if the child remains under the step-parent’s parental control, the child and the step-parent must be in the same EDG.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships 3
heading: Parents and Children

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(c)

If the child under joint physical custody lives with one parent more than 50% of the time, the child is included in that parent’s household when both parents are applying for SNAP.

If the primary parent is not trying to apply for SNAP, and there is no dispute, the secondary parent may receive SNAP for the child(ren).

Children under joint physical custody spending an equal amount of time in each household during a month are included in the EDG:

the parents have agreed upon, or

the EDG that applies first.

Complete the following steps when an EDG member leaves one SNAP household and moves to another SNAP household:

Mark the individual as “out of the household” on the active case. Run eligibility and authorize the case.

Use the “Add Individual” case action (or another acceptable case action to add the individual to the new active case). Complete all mandatory fields. Run eligibility and authorize the case .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships 4
heading: Joint Physical Custody

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(1)(iii)

A minor is an individual under 18 years of age. A minor who lives with, and is under the parental control of an adult, must be in the adult’s EDG, even if the adult is not the minor’s parent.

Parental control means the minor is dependent on the adult for financial or other support.

Exceptions:

Foster care providers and their foster children, see Foster Children and Adults .

Minors living with their spouse or child and with an adult who is not the minor’s parent can be a separate EDG only if they:

do not purchase and prepare food with others, and

are not dependent on the adult for financial or other support.

Example: A 17-year old and her 22-year old spouse live with the 17 year old’s aunt. The spouses are not dependent on the aunt for any support, and purchase and prepare separately from the aunt. The minor and her spouse can be an EDG without the aunt.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships 5
heading: Minor Under Parental Control

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(4)

The household may choose to include or exclude a foster child or foster adult who lives with them. If excluded, the foster child or adult is not eligible as a separate EDG, and the foster care payment is not income to the household.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships 6
heading: Foster Children and Adults

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Relationships

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)

There are 3 types of individuals excluded from the CG :

Disqualified members

Ineligible members

Non-members

Sometimes entire EDGs are ineligible for program benefits.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual 1
heading: Excluded Individual

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(iii)(v) and (vii) and (ix)-(xi) & 7 CFR § 273.2(d)(2) & 7 CFR § 273.11(c)(1)

These are individuals who would be CG members but are not allowed to be. Count their assets, income and deductions as if they were eligible members, see Assets and Income of Disqualified Members .

Criminal Justice Issues - Individuals who are fleeing felons , or probation/parole violators . See Fleeing Felons and Probation Violators and Parole Violators .

Drug Felony Conviction – Individuals convicted of a drug-related felony which occurred after August 22, 1996, involving the possession, use, or distribution of a controlled substance, who are not complying with the terms of their sentence or probation .

Anyone convicted of Federal aggravated sexual abuse, murder, sexual exploitation and abuse of children, sexual assault, or similar State Laws, and who are also not in compliance with the terms of their sentence or parole, or are a fleeing felon.

Intentional Program Violation (IPV) - Individuals who signed an IPV hearing waiver, or were found guilty of an IPV by an administrative disqualification hearing or court of law. See Intentional Program Violation .

Work Registration- Individuals failing to meet work registration requirements .

Quality Control (QC) Non-cooperation – Disqualify the entire EDG if any household member refuses to cooperate with either State or Federal quality control reviewers. See Quality Control .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual 2
heading: Disqualified Members

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(i) and (iii) and (xi) & 273.11(c)(2)-(3)

Ineligible members are individuals who would be CG members, but they do not meet one of the non-financial eligibility requirements in the sections noted below. Count assets as if they were eligible; prorate income and deductions. See Assets and Ineligible Members .

Non-Citizens - Individuals failing to meet eligibility rules in Citizen/Non-Citizen .

Social Security Number (SSN) - Individuals failing to provide or apply for an SSN as per Social Security Number .

Time Limited Able-Bodied Adults Without Dependents (ABAWDs) not meeting the ABAWD work requirement and who used their 3-month eligibility. See Time Limit for ABAWDs .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual 3
heading: Ineligible Members

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b) & 273.11(d)

Certain individuals who live with and may even share food with the EDG are treated as non-members for purposes of determining eligibility and benefits. Non-members’ income and assets are not included when determining eligibility and benefits for the CG .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual 4
heading: Non-members

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(3)(i)-(iii)

A boarder is an individual (or group) who resides with an EDG and pays reasonable compensation for lodging and meals. The EDG may choose to include or exclude a boarder (or boarder group) that pays reasonable compensation. If excluded, the boarder (or group) is not eligible for SNAP as a separate EDG.

Reasonable compensation exists when the total paid by the boarder (or group) is equal to:

The maximum benefit for the boarder (group) size when the boarder (group) pays for more than 2 meals per day.

2/3 of the maximum benefit for the boarder (group) size when the boarder (group) pays for 2 meals or less per day.

See Reasonable Room and Board Compensation

Spouses, parents, and children under 22 are not treated as boarders even if a board payment is being made.

Individuals paying less than reasonable compensation are not boarders, they are EDG members.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual 5
heading: Boarders

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(6)

Live-in attendants live with the EDG to provide medical, housekeeping, dependent care or other similar personal services. Live-in attendants can apply for SNAP as a separate EDG if they choose to, unless they meet a relationship requirement.

Spouses and parents and children under 22 are not treated as live-in attendants even if they provide these types of services.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual 6
heading: Live-In Attendants

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(3)

Individuals who reside in a commercial boarding house are not eligible for SNAP.

Commercial boarding houses are businesses that offer meals and lodging for payment with the intent of making a profit. Determine intent to make a profit from a statement by the applicant or the owner.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual 7
heading: Residents of Commercial Boarding Houses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)

Individuals who receive over 50% of 3 meals daily from an institution (college, nursing home, etc.) where they live are residents of institutions. Residents of institutions are not eligible for SNAP.

Examples : See Residents of Institutions .

Exception: Residents of institutions described in Special Situations .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual 8
heading: Residents of Institutions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(5)

Individuals who pay the EDG a flat-fee for lodging. They do not pay a separate amount for utilities. They do not purchase and prepare food with the EDG they rent from. Roomers can apply for SNAP as a separate EDG if they choose to, unless they meet a relationship requirement .

Spouses and parents and children under 22 are not treated as roomers even if they pay each other for lodging.

Note: Many separate households live together and share shelter expenses. This is not a roomer situation. See Shared Households .

Examples : Shared Households vs Roomers .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual 9
heading: Roomers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(e)(1)-(2)

An EDG with a striking member may or may not be eligible for benefits. See Strikers .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual 10
heading: Strikers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(i)

Students in higher education who do not meet the rules in Students .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual 11
heading: Students

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Individual

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Special Living Situations
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Special Living Situations

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Special Living Situations

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)

Individuals who receive more than 50% of their meals while living in the following institutions may be eligible as part of their own EDG that does not include other people residing at the institution, if they meet program requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Special Living Situations 1
heading: Special Living Situations

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Special Living Situations

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(B)

A public or private nonprofit residential facility for the treatment of drug addicts or alcoholics. The children of a resident (but not the spouse) who lives with them in the center are part of the resident’s EDG .

See Addiction Treatment Centers for special requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Special Living Situations 2
heading: Addiction Treatment Centers

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(C)

A public or nonprofit residence that provides housing for no more than 16 disabled individuals. The resident must be disabled to be eligible for SNAP.

See Group Living for the Disabled for special requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Special Living Situations 3
heading: Group Living for the Disabled

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Special Living Situations

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(E)

A public or private nonprofit facility that provides temporary housing for homeless individuals. The EDG is defined as a single individual or an individual and their spouse and children .

See Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers for special requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Special Living Situations 4
heading: Homeless Shelters

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Special Living Situations

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(D)

A public or private nonprofit residential facility that provides temporary housing for battered individuals. The EDG is the battered individual and their children.

See Shelters for Battered Individuals for special requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Special Living Situations 5
heading: Shelters for Battered Individuals

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Special Living Situations

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(A)

Federally subsidized housing for elderly individuals built under either Section 202 of the Housing Act of 1959 or Section 236 of the National Housing Act. The EDG is the elderly individual and their spouse. Determine eligibility the same as any other EDG.

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Residency
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Residency

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Residency

body:
7 CFR § 273.3(a)

The EDG must live in Connecticut when applying for SNAP. Individuals living in Connecticut for any reason meet the residency requirement, regardless of the length of time here or their plans to stay.

Exception: Individuals on vacation in Connecticut do not meet the residency requirement.

Note: Residency refers to Connecticut residency and not the mailing address. The EDG may have a new mailing address ( change of address ) and still maintain residency as long as they continue to live within Connecticut. This is because Connecticut is a statewide project area.

The EDG is not required to have a permanent dwelling or fixed residence.

For example , an individual still meets the residency requirement if the individual lives in a:

car,

camper,

tent, or

homeless shelter .

Note: Ask the EDG to provide a mailing address where notices can be mailed. The EDG may use any address as long as there is no threat of mail being lost or stolen.

For example , the address can be a friend or relative’s address or a post office box.

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(3)(i) & 7 CFR § 273.3(a)

Contact the other state via the National Directory of State Contacts when an individual moves to Connecticut from another state in the last 90 days. Confirm:

if the EDG had an active case,

when the case stopped, and

when benefits were last issued.

Note: Confirm from 12/1/2023 for ABAWDs if the individual may have received SNAP in another state.

Duplicate benefits cannot be issued for the same month.

Exception: Issue duplicate benefits if the individual is currently a resident in a Connecticut shelter for battered individuals .

Residency in Connecticut must be verified.

Exception : Verification is not required for categorically eligible households, unless it is questionable. Verification of residency through categorical eligibility is provided by:

the program conferring categorical eligibility, or

the receipt of the “ Help for People In Need ” brochure, which is only given to Connecticut residents

Verify residency at application. Accept any document or collateral contact that reasonably verifies residency.

Deny the application if not verified by the due date.

Postpone verification for expedited service month(s), unless verification can be provided in time to meet the 7-day standard of promptness.

Verify new or changed shelter amounts, if questionable. See Verification of Shelter Expenses .

Verify new or changed shelter amounts, if questionable. See Verification of Shelter Expenses .
