heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen/Non-Citizen

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
7 CFR § 273.4

The household attests to citizenship/non-citizenship status for each member, when the citizen question is completed on the SNAP application.

Non-citizens are individuals living in the United States who do not meet citizen criteria .

Non-citizens must meet eligible non-citizen criteria to receive SNAP benefits.

An individual is an ineligible non-citizen when they don’t meet citizen or eligible non-citizen criteria.

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)

United States citizens meet citizenship rules for SNAP.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria 1
heading: Citizen Criteria

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(1)

United States citizens are individuals born in one of the 50 states, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, Guam, the U.S. Virgin Islands, or the Northern Mariana Islands.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria 2
heading: Natural Born Citizens

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(2)

Non-citizen nationals are individuals born in American Samoa or Swain’s Island.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria 3
heading: Non-Citizen National

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria

body:
DOJ Interim Guidance 11-17-97

Naturalized citizens are United States citizens. Children of naturalized citizens are eligible if the child was:

under age 18 when the parent was naturalized on or after 10/14/1940, or

under age 21 when the parent was naturalized before 10/14/1940.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria 4
heading: Naturalized Citizens

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)

Automatic U.S. citizenship is granted to children born in a foreign country upon entry to the U.S. if the child is:

under 18 years of age,

admitted to the U.S. as a lawful permanent resident, and

in the legal and physical custody (as a natural or adopted child) of at least one parent who is a U.S. citizen.

Note: Adoptions must be finalized prior to the child entering the U.S.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria 5
heading: Natural or Adopted Children of a Citizen

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Citizen Criteria

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(3)-(7)

Only some non-citizens who are lawfully present in the United States are eligible for SNAP. To be eligible, a non-citizen must meet one of the following eligible non-citizen criteria.

See Non-immigrant .

Non-Citizen Status: Eligible If: Lawful Permanent Resident (LPR) Or those who have adjusted their status to LPR from: Battered Non-Citizen Conditional Entrant Individuals Granted Parole for a Period of at Least One Year Has been in the U.S. as a qualified non-citizen for 5 years, as long as they meet all other SNAP financial and non-financial eligibility requirements. Or , these individuals may be eligible immediately if they are: blind or disabled , and receiving benefits for their condition, or under age 18, or meets work quarter eligibility , or meets a military requirement , or was lawfully residing in the U.S. and 65 or older on August 22, 1996. Cuban Entrant Haitian Entrant Compacts of Free Association (COFA) citizens of the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and the Republic of Palau Eligible immediately, with no waiting period, as long as they meet all other SNAP financial and non-financial eligibility requirements. Note: These non-citizens retain their eligibility without having to meet the above requirements when converted to LPR status. Those who have adjusted their status to LPR from: Afghan Special Immigrant ( SIV ) Amerasian American Indians Born Abroad Asylee Certain Afghan Nationals Granted Parole Between July 31, 2021, and September 30, 2023 Certain Ukrainian Nationals Granted Parole Between February 24, 2022, and September 30, 2024 Deportation or Removal Withheld Hmong or Highland Laotian Tribal Member Iraqi Special Immigrant (SIV) Refugee Victims of Severe Trafficking These non-citizens who adjust their status to LPR from one of these groups are exempt from the 5-year waiting period in section 403 of PRWORA (8 USC 1613), and are not required to wait for 5 years after becoming an LPR.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 1
heading: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body: See Acceptable Non-Citizen Papers for documents that show non-citizen status for the categories listed below.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 2
heading: Non-Citizen Status Defined

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(B)

A non-citizen from Afghanistan admitted under Section 101(a)(27) of the INA which grants special immigrant status to individuals who worked with the U.S. government in Afghanistan.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 3
heading: Afghan Special Immigrant (SIV)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(F)

An Amerasian who entered under Section 584 of Public Law 100-202, as amended by Public Law 100-461.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 4
heading: Amerasian

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(C)

An asylee who entered under Section 208(b) of the INA.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 5
heading: Asylee

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(i)(G)

A non-citizen who was subject to battery or extreme cruelty in the United States. Extend this status to:

an individual if their child was abused, or

a child whose parent was abused.

Battered non-citizens must meet the following 4 requirements:

Have an approved or pending petition making a prima facie case in one of the following categories:

Form I-130, Petition for Alien Relative, filed by their spouse or the child’s parent;

Form I-130, Petition as a Widow(er) of a U.S. Citizen;

A self-petition under the Violence Against Women Act; or

An application filed as a victim of domestic violence for cancellation of removal, or suspension of deportation.

Been abused in the U.S. by a:

spouse

parent, or

member of the spouse’s or parent’s family residing in the same household.

Show a substantial connection between the abuse and the need for public benefits.

For example : The assistance is needed:

due to the loss of financial support or residence,

to become self-sufficient,

to escape the abuser,

to relieve nutritional risk that results from separation from the abuser, or

to seek medical attention or counseling.

The battered non-citizen, child, or parent no longer lives in the same household as the abuser.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 6
heading: Battered Non-Citizen

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(3)(i)-(ii)

American Indians born in Canada living in the United States (U.S.) under §289 of the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) or alien members of a federally recognized Indian tribe under §4(e) of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act .

Compacts of Free Association (COFA) citizens

On March 9, 2024, President Biden signed into law the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2024 ( PL 118-42 ), which provides that Compacts of Free Association (COFA) citizens of the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and the Republic of Palau who lawfully reside in the United States are eligible for certain federal public benefit programs, including SNAP. COFA citizens are not subject to a waiting period and are immediately eligible for benefits as long as they meet all other SNAP financial and non-financial eligibility requirements.

For more information, see the July 12, 2024, memo .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 7
heading: Certain American Indians born abroad

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(i)(F)

A non-citizen lawfully present under Section 203(a)(7) of the INA as in effect before 04/01/1980.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 8
heading: Conditional Entrant

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(E)

A non-citizen from Cuba who is described by Section 501(e) of the REAA .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 9
heading: Cuban Entrant

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(D)

A non-citizen whose deportation is withheld under Section 243(h) as in effect before 04/01/1997, or removal is withheld under 241(b)(3) of the INA .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 10
heading: Deportation or Removal Withheld

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(E)

A non-citizen from Haiti who is described by Section 501(e) of the REAA .

heading: Haitian Entrant

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 12
heading: Hmong or Highland Laotian Tribal Member

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(B)

A non-citizen from Iraq admitted under Section 101(a)(27) of the INA which grants special immigrant status to individuals who worked with the U.S. government in Iraq.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 13
heading: Iraqi Special Immigrant (SIV)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(i)(A)

A non-citizen admitted for legal permanent residence in the U.S. under the INA .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 14
heading: Lawful Permanent Resident

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(i)(D)

A non-citizen paroled into the U.S. under Section 212(d)(5) of the INA for at least one year.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 15
heading: Parolee

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(B)

A non-citizen admitted as a refugee under Section 207 of the INA .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 16
heading: Refugee

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 17
heading: Trafficking Victim

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(G)

Qualified non-citizens meeting one of the criteria below are eligible.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 18
heading: Military Requirement

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(G)(2)-(3)

On active duty with the U.S. Armed Forces (excluding National Guard) for reasons other than training. Includes the active duty individual’s:

spouse;

unmarried child who is under age 18 or, if a full-time student, under age 22; and

unmarried disabled child who is 18 years of age or older if the child was disabled and dependent on the active duty service member prior to the child’s 18th birthday.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 19
heading: Military Active Duty

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(G)(1) and (3)

A veteran of the U.S. Armed forces who was honorably discharged for reasons not related to non-citizen status. Also include the:

Spouse of the veteran, including after the veteran dies, as long as the spouse does not remarry;

Unmarried child who is under age 18 or, if a full-time student, under age 22;

Unmarried disabled child who is 18 years of age or older if the child was disabled and dependent on the veteran prior to the child’s 18th birthday; and

Unmarried child of the veteran, regardless of age, if the child was dependent on the veteran at the time of the veteran’s death.

Note: “Under Honorable Conditions” does not qualify as an honorable discharge.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 20
heading: Veteran

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(A)(1)-(2)

Non-citizens with 40 qualifying work quarters are eligible.

Count as a qualifying work quarter if worked by:

the non-citizen;

a parent of a non-citizen before the non-citizen reached their 18th birthday, including quarters worked before the non-citizen was born; and

a spouse of the non-citizen during their marriage, as long as the non-citizen remains married to the spouse, or the spouse is deceased.

Each spouse can claim the quarters of the other spouse (unless divorced), and the children can claim the quarters worked by both parents.

Note: Only one qualifying quarter can be earned per quarter. Count as one qualifying quarter if both spouses, or both parents, worked the same quarter.

Count both qualifying quarters of work covered and not covered by Title II of the Social Security Act.

Beginning 1/1/1997, a quarter is not countable if the individual earning the quarter received Federal means-tested assistance at any time during that quarter.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria 21
heading: Work Quarter Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Non-Citizen Criteria

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Ineligible Non-Citizen
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Ineligible Non-Citizen

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Ineligible Non-Citizen

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(2)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.4(a) and (b)(2) & 7 CFR § 273.11(c)(3)

Ineligible non-citizens are not eligible for SNAP. They include:

Immigrants not identified as eligible non-citizens .

Non-immigrants .

Exclude any household member whose non-citizen status is in question, because of an inability or unwillingness to attest to their non-citizen status. Take no further action to get verification of their status.

Ineligible non-citizen household members must cooperate in establishing eligibility for eligible household members by answering questions and providing verification of their:

income,

assets, and

expenses and deductions.

Deny the household if the ineligible non-citizen fails to answer questions or provide required verification needed to determine the household’s eligibility.

See Ineligible Members .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Ineligible Non-Citizen 1
heading: Ineligible Non-Citizen

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Ineligible Non-Citizen

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(b)

Undocumented status is “known" only when a formal determination, subject to administrative review, was completed and is supported by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) or the Executive Office for Immigration Review, such as a Final Order of Deportation.

Report and send a copy of the formal determination to a PA Consultant in the Economic Security Unit in Central Office when a known undocumented non-citizen applies for SNAP benefits. Do not report the non-citizen as an undocumented non-citizen without a formal determination .

Central Office will report the individual to the USCIS .

Undocumented non-citizens are ineligible for SNAP benefits.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Ineligible Non-Citizen 2
heading: Reporting Known Undocumented Non-Citizen

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Ineligible Non-Citizen

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(c)(1)-(2)

Sponsored non-citizens are granted permanent resident status to enter and reside in the United States because an individual or organization sponsor signed an agreement to guarantee financial support of the non-citizen.

Counting the sponsor’s income and assets toward the sponsored non-citizen is called deeming.

Deeming may be required for non-citizens with a sponsor who signed the Affidavit of Support , Forms I-864 , I-864A , or similar agreement, on or after December 19, 1997.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen 1
heading: Sponsored Non-Citizen

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(c)(3)

Some non-citizens are exempt from sponsor deeming even if an Affidavit of Support was signed on their behalf. Non-citizens exempt from sponsor deeming are:

Battered non-citizen . See Battery Exemption to Sponsor Deeming .

Children under age 18.

Indigent non-citizens. See Indigence Determination .

Lawful permanent residents credited with 40 qualifying work quarters .

Non-citizen is residing in the same house as the sponsor because they are one SNAP household.

Non-citizens sponsored by organizations or groups.

Non-citizens whose sponsor signed the Affidavit of Support , Form I-134, prior to 12/19/1997
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen 2
heading: Non-Citizen Exempt from Sponsor Deeming

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen

body:
A non-citizen is exempt from sponsor deeming if the following criteria apply:

The non-citizen, the non-citizen’s child, or, if the non-citizen is a child, the non-citizen’s parent was battered or subject to extreme cruelty while in the U.S. by a spouse, parent or member of the spouse’s or parent’s family living in the same household as the non-citizen;

The non-citizen is no longer living in the same household as the batterer; and

There is a substantial connection between the battery or extreme cruelty and the non-citizen's need for SNAP benefits.

Note: This exemption is good for one year. The non-citizen cannot live with the batterer during the exemption period. After one year, the exemption may be continued if a judge, administrative law judge or the Department of Homeland Security formally recognizes that the battery or extreme cruelty actually occurred.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen 3
heading: Battery Exemption from Sponsor Deeming

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen

body:
An indigent non-citizen is an individual who is unable to obtain food and shelter based on the total of their household income, their sponsor(s) income, and any other assistance received.

A non-citizen is considered indigent if the sponsor does not live with or provide free room and board to the non-citizen, and one of the following is true:

The non-citizen's income, including in-kind income and income actually provided by the sponsor, is less than 130% of the FPL for the non-citizen's household size; or

The non-citizen is living in an institution, rated housing facility, or is applying for or receiving services under a Medicaid Home and Community Based Waiver.

Note: If a non-citizen lives with their sponsor but is applying for or receiving services under a Medicaid Home and Community Based Waiver, treat the non-citizen as though they live apart from the sponsor.

See Non-Citizens – “A” Numbers and SAVE for more information.

Review indigence exclusions every 12 months.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen 4
heading: Indigence Determination

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.4(c)(2)(i)-(v) and (4) & 7 CFR 273.11(c)(3)(i)-(v)

Sponsored non-citizens are responsible to provide information or verification about the sponsor(s), and to obtain the cooperation from the sponsor at application and renewal. Allow the non-citizen at least 10 days to provide the information. Sponsored non-citizens are ineligible non-citizens if information is not provided by the due date. Determine eligibility and benefits for the remaining eligible members.

Deem a portion of the sponsor’s income and assets to the sponsored non-citizen, regardless of whether the income or assets are paid to the non-citizen. Deem the sponsor’s spouse’s income and assets to the sponsored non-citizen if the sponsor is living with their spouse.

A non-citizen can have one joint sponsor if the primary sponsor cannot fully support the financial needs of the non-citizen. Count the deemed income and assets of all sponsors for the non-citizen.

Note: Do not include the sponsor’s or sponsor’s spouse’s income and assets as income or assets of an ineligible non-citizen.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen 5
heading: Sponsor Deeming

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen 6
heading: Deeming of Sponsor's Assets

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen 7
heading: Deeming of Sponsor's Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen 8
heading: Sponsor deeming when children under 18 are included in the SNAP household

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(c)(2)

Deeming of income and assets ends when the:

non-citizen gains U.S. citizenship,

non-citizen has worked or can receive credit for 40 qualifying work quarters ,

non-citizen loses LPR status and leaves the U.S.,

non-citizen receives in removal proceedings a new grant of adjustment of status as relief from removal, or

sponsor dies.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen 9
heading: Expiration of Sponsor Deeming

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Sponsored Non-Citizen

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(ii)(A)-(C) & 7 CFR § 273.4(c)(5)

Inform the household of the following options when verification is required and not provided on a timely basis :

Withdraw their application; or

Determine the eligibility of the remaining members.

Note : The household may request the worker not complete a SAVE query or otherwise contact USCIS . If the household makes that request, they must select one of the above options.

Budget the income, assets, and expenses of the ineligible member per Ineligible Members.

See Acceptable Non-Citizen Papers for sources.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 1
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(2)(i)-(ii)

Verify citizen criteria if questionable. See Suggested Verification Sources .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 2
heading: Citizen Criteria

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(ii)(A)-(C)

Verification is required to determine eligible non-citizen status before approving or adding a non-citizen to SNAP benefits. See Acceptable Non-citizen Papers for the forms that confirm the non-citizen’s status.

After the USCIS form is received showing eligible status, use SAVE to confirm the form is accurate.

Exception: Non-citizens are eligible for SNAP benefits if they are in an eligible status and verification is pending as follows:

Until a SAVE response is received regarding validation of the non-citizen’s USCIS documentation;

Up to 6 months when SSA is reviewing qualifying work quarter history; or

Up to 6 months after a request for verification of non-citizen status was sent to a Federal agency, other than USCIS.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 3
heading: Eligible Non-Citizen

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 272.11(d)

Pursue secondary verification of non-citizen status when:

SAVE cannot confirm the verification, or

there is a difference between the SAVE information and the provided verification.

Complete the agency areas and send a USCIS G-845, Verification Request , and G-845S, Verification Request Supplement if required, for each member, along with a copy of the front and back of the non-citizen’s verification, for review to USCIS .

Scan the G-845/G-845S to the case when returned.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 4
heading: Secondary Verification Needed

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
SAVE must be used to confirm the papers presented by the non-citizen.

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 5
heading: Acceptable Non-Citizen Papers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.4(b)(2)

Do not request verification of non-citizen status for individuals who are not participating in the program. This is true whether the individual claims to be lawfully or illegally present in the U.S.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 6
heading: Ineligible Non-Citizen

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.4(a)(6)(ii)(G)(1)-(3)

Verification of the active duty or veteran status is required.

Active duty can be verified with a:

current Military Identification Card (DD Form 2 Active), or

copy of current military orders

Veteran status can be verified using a discharge certificate (DD Form 214, or the equivalent) with the character of service rating of “Honorable Discharge”.

Note: “Under Honorable Conditions” does not meet this requirement.

Refer the individual to the VA if they don’t have verification.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 7
heading: Military Requirement

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(ii)(A)-(C) and (j)(2)(v) and (4)(iii)(E) & 7 CFR § 273.4(c)(4)-(5) & 7 CFR 273.11(c)(3)(i)-(v)

Sponsored non-citizens are responsible to provide information or verification about the sponsor (and sponsor’s spouse, if appropriate), and to obtain the cooperation from the sponsor at application and renewal. Allow the non-citizen at least 10 days to provide the information.

Verify the following information of the sponsor(s):

Income

Assets

Names and other identifying information for non-citizens the sponsor(s) also sponsor

Exception: Verification is not required if the sponsor(s) gross countable income information was verified for another agency assistance program.

Assist the household in obtaining verification if they have difficulty getting verification. Access the SAVE program to obtain the following sponsor information:

Name

Address

Social Security number

The non-citizen is ineligible if the information or verification is not provided. Budget the non-citizen’s income, assets and expenses following Ineligible Members policy.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 8
heading: Sponsored Non-Citizen

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(ii)(2)(A)-(B)(2)

Verification of qualifying work quarters is required. Verify work quarters through SSA .

Verify last year’s and current work quarters not showing using any of the following sources, including but not limited to:

Income tax forms

Pay stubs

W-2

See Qualifying Work Quarters Determination for the amount of earnings needed for each quarter.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 9
heading: Work Quarter Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(ii)(2)(B)(2)

The individual is an eligible non-citizen when:

the individual disagrees with SSA’s finding of qualifying work quarters, and

provides verification SSA is checking for more qualifying quarters.

Approve the individual, if otherwise eligible, for SNAP up to 6 months from the date of SSA’s original finding.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 10
heading: Pending Dispute of Qualifying Work Quarters

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(b)(7)(i) & 7 CFR § 273.5 & 7 CFR 273.10(e)(2)(i)(D)and(E)

A student is an individual who is enrolled at least half-time in an institution of higher learning. Students are ineligible for SNAP unless they meet one of the eligible student criteria .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students 1
heading: Students

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students

body:
7 CFR § 273.5(c)

Students are enrolled as of the first day of the school term through normal scheduled class periods, vacation, and recess unless they:

graduate;

are suspended, expelled, or drop out; or

don’t intend to register for the next school term (excluding summer school).
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students 2
heading: Enrolled

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students

body:
7 CFR § 273.5(a)

The school defines the number of credit hours needed to qualify a student as at least half-time.

Only students enrolled in institutions of higher education half-time or more are required to meet a student exemption to be eligible for SNAP. Individuals enrolled less than half-time do not have to meet a student exemption to be eligible for SNAP.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students 3
heading: Half-Time Enrollment

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students

body:
7 CFR § 273.5(a)

Business, technical, trade, or vocational schools:

An individual is considered enrolled in an institution of higher education if they are enrolled in a business, technical, trade, or vocational school and in a curriculum that normally requires a high school diploma or equivalent for enrollment.

Individuals enrolled in these types of schools, but in a curriculum that does not normally require a high school diploma or equivalent, are not considered enrolled in an institute of higher education.

Colleges and Universities:

An individual is considered enrolled in an institution of higher education if they are enrolled in a regular curriculum at a college or university that offers degrees regardless of whether a high school diploma is required.

However, individuals enrolled at least half-time at a college, but in a program outside of the regular curriculum, are not considered enrolled in an institution of higher education and, do not need to meet a student exemption. This means if they meet all other eligibility criteria, they are eligible for SNAP.

Examples include remedial education, continuing or community education, professional development, English for Speakers of Other Languages, and workforce development/training programs.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students 4
heading: Institution of Higher Education

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(ix) and (2) and (3)(i)

Verify the student’s:

enrollment in an institution of higher education,

exemption, if applicable, and

questionable information.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students 5
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students

body:
See Verification Requirements by Element and Action .

See Suggested Verification Sources .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students 6
heading: Resources

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students

body:
Source # 16

Effective Date: 4/1/2025
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students 7
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Students

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.5(b)

Students must meet at least one of the following criteria to be eligible for SNAP.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria 1
heading: Eligible Student Criteria

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.5(b)(1)

Under age 18 or age 50 or older.

Note:

The student is under age 18 until the month following the month they turn 18.

The student is age 50 the first day of the month they turn 50.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria 2
heading: Age

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.5(b)(8) and (9) and (10)(i)-(ii)

Responsible for the care of a dependent child under age 6;

Note: The exemption is limited to one adult per child if 2 adults are responsible. Allow the exemption to the adult providing the majority of the child’s care. If 2 adults are responsible for the care of the child, allow the exemption to the adult who is the student only if the student provides the majority of the child’s care.

Responsible for the care of a dependent child at least age 6 but under age 12 if the student does not have adequate child care; or

Full-time student who is a single parent responsible for the care of a dependent child under the age of 12.

The single parent and child must be in the same SNAP EDG .

Includes an adult with parental control over the child as long as the adult is not residing with a spouse.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria 3
heading: Caring for a Dependent Child

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.5(b)(5)

Paid for working at least an average of 20 hours a week;

Note: Unpaid hours due to holiday or illness are in the 20-hour calculation; or

Self-employed at least 20 hours a week and income after allowable expenses is at least equal to the Federal minimum wage multiplied by 20 hours a week.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria 4
heading: Employment

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(xi) & 7 CFR § 273.5(b)(2)

Meets the work registration definition of physically or mentally unfit .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria 5
heading: Physically or Mentally Unfit for Work

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.5(b)(3)

The student gets benefits from TFA .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria 6
heading: TFA Recipient

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria 7
heading: Training

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria

body:
7 CFR § 273.5(b) (6)(i)-(ii)

Participating in a state or federally funded work study program. The student must :

be approved for work study at the time of application;

be approved for work study for the school term; and

anticipate actually working during that time.

Once allowed, the exemption continues until:

the end of the month in which the school term ends;

it is reported the student refuses a work study assignment; or

there is a break between terms of a full month or longer unless the student participates during the break.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria 8
heading: Work Study

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Eligible Student Criteria

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

body:
7 CFR § 273.6(a) and (b)(1)

All EDG members applying for SNAP must provide their Social Security Number (SSN) . Individuals with multiple SSNs must provide all of them. Individuals without an SSN must apply for an SSN before approval.

Explain to the EDG how the SSN will be used, and that providing the SSN is voluntary. However, any individual applying for SNAP who fails to provide or apply for an SSN without good cause will result in ineligibility for that individual.

Note: Do not delay the SNAP application pending verification of a SSN by the Social Security Administration (SSA). Allow the individual to participate pending verification.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number 1
heading: Social Security Number

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

body:
7 CFR § 273.6(b)(2)(i)-(ii) and (3)

Refer individuals who do not have an SSN to their local SSA Office to apply for an SSN. Assist the individual:

to complete the SSN application ( SS-5 ),

obtain verification required by SSA, or

obtain information from SSA regarding the SSN or previous application.

Allow the individual to participate while SSA processes the application once verification of application is provided.

Refer individuals who do not remember their SSN to SSA for verification.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number 2
heading: Obtaining SSNs for SNAP EDG Members

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

body:
7 CFR § 273.6(b)(4)

Parents of a newborn child may apply for a SSN for the child at the hospital, when this service is available. The SSN or verification of application for an SSN must be provided within 6 months following the month the baby is born, or at the next renewal, whichever is later.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number 3
heading: Newborns

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

body:
7 CFR § 273.6(c)

Individuals refusing or failing without good cause to provide or apply for an SSN, are ineligible to participate in SNAP. The individual without the SSN is ineligible, not the entire EDG. Count the ineligible individual’s assets. Prorate the individual’s income and expenses/deductions. See Ineligible Members .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number 4
heading: Failure to Comply

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

body:
7 CFR § 273.6(d)

Verification of good cause is records or other information showing the individual applied for, or made every effort to supply the necessary information for an SSN.

This information may come from any of the following:

A collateral contact

DSS

The EDG/individual

SSA

Note: Because SSA allows mail-in SSN applications, good cause does not include delays caused by:

illness,

lack of transportation, or

temporary absences from the home.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number 5
heading: Determining Good Cause

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

body:
7 CFR § 273.6(d)

Allow an individual who can show good cause for not timely completing a SSN application to participate for the month of application and one additional month. Thereafter, the individual must show good cause monthly in order to continue participating.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number 6
heading: Continued Participation

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

body:
7 CFR § 273.6(e)

Ineligible members may become eligible by providing their SSN, or by providing verification of application for a SSN.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number 7
heading: Regaining Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

body:
7 CFR § 273.6(f)

When determining eligibility for SNAP, SSN’s may be used to:

Determine the accuracy of information given by EDGs

Determine the amount of benefits

Enable mass changes in federal benefits

Prevent duplicate participation

Request and exchange income and asset information through computer matches. See Automated Resources for more information .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number 8
heading: Use of the SSN

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

body:
7 § 273.2(f)(1)(v) & 7 CFR § 273.6(b)(1)

The SSN is automatically verified through the SSA enumeration process.

EDGs must verify any SSN that fails enumeration, including any:

differences, and

information DSS cannot verify.

Send the EDG a W-1348, Proofs We Need, to confirm the number, or provide another type of verification, such as but not limited to, a hard copy of the SSN or SS-5 . See Suggested Verification Sources for other acceptable sources.

Note : Verification is not required for categorically eligible households unless the SSN is questionable.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number 9
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

body:
See Verification Requirements by Element and Action .

See Suggested Verification Sources .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number 10
heading: Resources

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(6)

Enter the following actions in case notes:

Claims of good cause

Collateral contacts

Failure to cooperate

Worker efforts to assist the individual to apply for, or obtain, an SSN.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number 11
heading: Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Social Security Number

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Work Requirements
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Work Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Work Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(a)(1) and (4)

Individuals applying for, or receiving, SNAP benefits are mandatory work registrants unless they are exempt . Mandatory work registrants must meet work requirements to receive SNAP benefits.

Excluded members are not subject to:

Work registration

Voluntary quit requirements

Exception: Strikers must register for work, unless exempt , if their EDG is eligible for SNAP.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Work Requirements 1
heading: General Work Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: General Work Requirements

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Registration
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Registration

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Registration

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(a)(1)(i) and(b)(2)(i)-(ii) and (c)(1)-(2)

Applicants or recipients are registered for work by signing the W-1E, Application.

Determine the work registration status of each individual:

at application,

renewal,

when a member is added to the EDG, and

when a member is no longer exempt.

Code each individual’s work registration status in ImpaCT.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Registration 1
heading: Work Registration

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Registration

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(c)(1)

Explain the following information to mandatory registrants:

the work requirements,

the individual’s rights and responsibilities, and

the penalties for failing to comply with the work requirements.

Explain the information:

at application,

renewal,

when adding a new EDG member, and

when an individual loses their work registration exemption.

Note: The work registration information is also provided in the SNAP notice, as required.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Registration 2
heading: Work Registration Notification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Registration

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(b)(2)(i)-(ii)

Review work registration status when changes are reported and, if necessary, update the work registration code in ImpaCT when acting on that change. Individuals losing their exemption must register at the next renewal.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Registration 3
heading: Work Registration Changes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Registration

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(b)(1)(i)-(viii)

Exempt Individuals Verification Required Age 15 or younger If questionable Age 16 or 17, and a child who lives with a parent, or lives with and is under the parental control of an adult who is not their parent. See Relationships . Or not the head of household . If questionable Age 16 or 17, and is the head of household , and attending school at least half-time, or enrolled in a training or employment program at least half-time. Yes Age 60 or older – exempt starting the month of the individual’s birthday If questionable Caring for an incapacitated individual Yes Caring for a child under age 6 The exemption remains until the next renewal when the child turns 6 in the benefit period. Only one parent or caretaker per child may receive this exemption. If questionable Complying with work participation requirements for TFA benefits. If questionable Drug or alcohol addiction and rehabilitation program participant. Participation can be either as a resident of the treatment center or an outpatient basis. The program must be certified by the Connecticut Department of Mental Health and Addiction Services (DMHAS). Yes Employed or self-employed and working a minimum of 30 hours weekly or receiving earnings that are at least equal to the Federal minimum wage multiplied by 30 hours. Fluctuating work hours should be averaged to arrive at a reasonable estimate of hours expected to be worked in a week. Volunteer hours or hours worked for in-kind benefits are not included in the 30 hours per week calculation. Yes Migrant or seasonal farmworkers who are under contract, or similar agreement, with an employer or crew chief to start work within 30 days. Yes Physically or mentally unfit for work. The unfitness may be temporary or permanent. Exempt persons with a pending SSI disability application. Change work registration status if the application is denied. Yes Student enrolled at least half-time in a school, training program, or institution of higher education . Includes adults going to high school classes half-time or more, or home-schooled if the home school has been recognized as an acceptable secondary education provider. Exemption continues during vacations and breaks when the student intends to register and attend at least half-time for the next school term. Exemption ends when the student graduates, is suspended, expelled, drops out, or changes enrollment to less than half-time. Yes Receiving or has applied for unemployment compensation benefits (UCB) and is complying with work requirements. The individual must register for work with Department of Labor (DOL) as part of the UCB application process. If questionable

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary Quit
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary Quit

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary Quit

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(j)

Mandatory registrants must not voluntarily:

quit a job, or

reduce work hours.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary Quit 1
heading: Voluntary Quit

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary Quit

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(j)(1)-(3)(i)-(ii) and (iv)-(v)

A mandatory registrant voluntarily quit a job if:

the registrant worked at least 30 hours a week, or the job provided weekly earnings equal to the Federal minimum wage times 30 hours;

the quit/job termination occurred within 60 days of the application, or any time after application; and

the reason for the quit/job termination was without good cause .

Note: Participation in a strike by a Federal, State, or local government employee is a voluntary quit without good cause.

A mandatory registrant did not voluntarily quit a job if the registrant:

did not re-enlist in the military;

resigned from the job at the employer’s request for reasons beyond the individual’s control, such as being unable to complete the required work assignments; or

stopped a self-employment business.

Apply disqualification penalties if the registrant voluntarily quit a job without good cause .

Note: Voluntary quit applies to an individual whose only exemption from work registration was employment of at least 30 hours a week (or equivalent earnings).
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary Quit 2
heading: Job Quit

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary Quit

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(j)(1)-(3)(i) and (iii)-(v)

A mandatory registrant voluntarily reduced their work hours if the:

hours were reduced to less than 30 hours a week by the employee, not the employer; and

reduction occurred within 60 days of the application, or any time after application.

A mandatory registrant did not voluntarily reduce work hours to less than 30 hours a week if the reduction in hours is temporary for reasons such as:

illness,

personal business, or

vacation.

Apply disqualification penalties if the mandatory registrant voluntarily reduced work hours without good cause .

Note: Reduced work hours applies to an individual whose only exemption from work registration was employment of at least 30 hours a week (or equivalent earnings) .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary Quit 3
heading: Reduced Work Hours

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary Quit

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with TFA or UCB Work Requirements
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with TFA or UCB Work Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with TFA or UCB Work Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(f)(i)-(iv)

Disqualify EDG members failing to comply with the TFA or UCB program’s work requirements. Apply the disqualification when:

the individual’s only exemption from SNAP work registration was complying with the TFA or UCB work requirements, and

if the non-complying individual was receiving SNAP and the other program at the time of the disqualification.

Disqualify only the individual who failed to comply with the other program’s work requirement. Do not disqualify the entire EDG, even when the designated provider is the individual who failed to comply.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with TFA or UCB Work Requirements 1
heading: Failure to Comply with TFA or UCB Work Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with TFA or UCB Work Requirements

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Good Cause
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Good Cause

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Good Cause

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(i)(1)-(3)(i)-(viii)

Good cause for a work requirement disqualification includes:

Accepting another job of at least 30 hours per week (or equivalent).

Accepting a job offer that provides at least 30 hours a week, or weekly earnings at least equal to Federal minimum wage times 30 hours, and that job did not materialize or meet the hourly criteria or weekly earnings through no fault of their own.

An EDG member accepted a job or enrolled in school at least half-time in another county or State, causing the EDG to move.

Circumstances beyond the registrant’s control.

Examples include, but are not limited to:

EDG emergency

illness of the work registrant or another EDG member requiring their presence

Discrimination by an employer based on:

age,

color,

handicap,

national origin,

political beliefs,

race,

religious beliefs, or

sex.

Enrollment in school at least half-time.

Lack of adequate dependent care is reported for children at least 6 and under 12.

Leaving a job that requires frequent moves to different employers.

F or example migrant or seasonal farm workers , or construction workers.

Retiring before age 60 and the employer recognizes it as retirement.

The job was unsuitable .

Work demands or conditions make it unreasonable to continue employment, such as working without being paid on time.

Consider all the facts from the EDG and others, such as the employer, when good cause is claimed. Enter case notes regarding the individual’s claim of good cause, the facts, and why a disqualification was imposed or not imposed.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Good Cause 1
heading: Good Cause

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Good Cause

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(h)(1)-(2)(i)-(iv)

Do not impose a job quit or reduced work hours disqualification if the job meets one of the following:

The daily commuting time exceeds 2 hours a day, not including taking a child to and from child care.

Note: EDG moves resulting in more than 2 hours commuting time, including moves within the State and from State to State make the job unsuitable. This applies whether an individual leaves a job before or after the move.

The distance to the place of employment is too far to walk, and neither public nor private transportation is available to get the individual to the job site.

The individual is physically or mentally unfit to perform the job.

The individual must join, resign from, or refrain from joining any legitimate labor organization to get or keep the job.

The job is on a piece-rate basis and the average hourly earnings are less than the hourly wage as defined below.

The job is offered within the first 30 days of registration and is not in the individual’s major field of experience.

The job offered is at a site currently subject to a strike or lockout, unless :

the strike has been enjoined under Section 208 of the Labor-Management Relations Act (commonly known as the Taft-Hartley Act), or

an injunction has been issued under Section 10 of the Railway Labor Act.

The risk to the health and safety of the individual is unreasonable.

The wage offered is less than the highest of:

the applicable Federal minimum wage ,

the applicable State minimum wage , or

80 percent of the Federal minimum wage, if the neither the Federal nor State minimum wage is applicable; or

The working hours or nature of the job interferes with the individual’s religious observations, convictions, or beliefs.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Good Cause 2
heading: Unsuitable Employment

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Good Cause

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(a)(1)(i)-(vii) and (b)(iii) and (v) and (f)(5)(iv) and (j)(1)-(3)(vi)-(viii)

Disqualify a mandatory work registrant when the individual fails to meet a work requirement .

Determine good cause before imposing a work requirement disqualification.

Note: The disqualification penalty follows the disqualified individual if they join a new EDG, unless the individual becomes exempt.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications 1
heading: Work Requirement Disqualifications

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(f)(2)(i)(A)-(B) and (ii)(A)-(B) and (iii)(A)-(B)

Disqualify only the individual who committed the penalty.

First violation: 3 months, or the date the individual complies, whichever occurs last .

2nd violation: 6 months, or the date the individual complies, whichever occurs last .

3rd and additional violations: 6 months, or the date the individual complies, whichever occurs last .

Budget the disqualified individual’s income, assets, expenses and deductions following Disqualified Members policy.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications 2
heading: Disqualification Periods

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(c)(3) and (f)(1)(i)-(ii)

Provide a NOA within 10 days of the date non-compliance without good cause is determined.

Include the following information in the NOA:

The specific offense the individual committed.

The proposed disqualification period.

The actions needed to end or avoid the disqualification.

Statement that the EDG may reapply at the end of the disqualification period.

Their right to a fair hearing .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications 3
heading: Notice of Disqualification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(c)(3) and (f)(1)(i)-(ii) and (j)(3)(vi)

Advise applying households of the disqualification using the appropriate notice. The disqualification begins upon issuance of the notice.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications 4
heading: Applying Households

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(c)(3) and (f)(1)(i)-(ii) and (j)(3)(vii)

Provide advance notice within 10 days of the date non-compliance is determined.

The disqualification period begins:

the first month following the end of the adverse action period , unless a fair hearing is requested,

the month following the end of the benefit period, if the failure to comply occurs in the last 30 days of the benefit period, or

the first month after the hearing decision is issued.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications 5
heading: Approved Households

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(f)(5)(iii) and (g) and (j)(4)

EDGs must request to add the individual back to the case, or reapply if the SNAP case was closed. End the disqualification and add the individual or reopen the case when the disqualified person:

becomes exempt from work registration ; or

serves the disqualification period; and

starts work registration by reapplying using a W-1E, Application, or W-1ER, Renewal Application.

Note: Individuals disqualified for failure to comply with TFA or UCB work requirements must comply with those requirements prior to ending the disqualification.

When the entire EDG is disqualified they can become eligible sooner if:

the designated provider leaves the home, or

the designated provider meets a work registration exemption .

When the application is filed in the last month of the disqualification period, use the application for:

denial of benefits for that month, and

the next month’s determination of eligibility.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications 6
heading: Ending the Disqualification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Work Requirement Disqualifications

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary SNAP Employment and Training Program
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary SNAP Employment and Training Program

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary SNAP Employment and Training Program

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(e)(1) and (4)(i)-(iii)

The SNAP Employment and Training Program (E&T) helps recipients obtain employment. SNAP recipients, not receiving TFA, who are able to work upon completion of an E&T component, may volunteer to participate in SNAP E&T.

The SNAP E&T program provides SNAP recipients opportunities to gain skills, training, or experience that improves their ability to obtain regular employment and reduce reliance on SNAP benefits.

Note: SNAP participants are not disqualified for failure to cooperate with E&T requirements because they are volunteers.

Advise recipients about the E&T program and opportunities the program offers at:

application,

renewal, and

when adding a new EDG member who is a mandatory work registrant.

Please refer to the recommended script for discussion points and required documentation:

‘SNAP E&T helps you gain skills and find work through activities like job searches and training. SNAP E&T is a voluntary program in CT and will not impact your SNAP benefit amount or eligibility. If you choose to participate in SNAP E&T you are eligible for expenses that are reasonable and necessary for participation to be reimbursed.

Would you like me to send your contact information to our DSS E&T staff to find out more information from an E&T Provider? What is your best phone number and/or email address?

If you are interested in the future, you can visit the DSS website and contact an approved CT SNAP E&T provider.’

Case notes MUST be added for each interaction and discussion of SNAP E&T:

[Individual Name (s)] was informed of CT’s voluntary SNAP E&T Program, and availability of participant reimbursements.

Individual declined internal referral option.

OR

Email was sent to DSS E&T staff for referral to E&T provider .

When SNAP recipients inform us that they are students of higher education, eligibility staff should follow up with a few probing questions and determine if the student is attending college as a SNAP E&T participant.

If they are attending college via the SNAP E&T program, they are not subject to the technical eligibility requirements. E&T participation is confirmed by contacting the SNAP Team who have access to the SNAP E&T database.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary SNAP Employment and Training Program 1
heading: Voluntary SNAP Employment and Training Program

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Voluntary SNAP Employment and Training Program

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification and Case Notes
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification and Case Notes

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(viii) and (2) & 7 CFR § 273.7(b)(1)(ii)-(iii) and (v) and (i)(4)

Verification is required for questionable information, and as outlined in Work Registration Exemptions . Code the individual as mandatory if the required verification is not returned within 10 days, unless the person meets a different exemption. Do not deny or close benefits for failure to verify an exemption.

See Suggested Verification Sources for acceptable verifications .

heading: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(d)(2) & 7 CFR § 273.2(f)(6) & 7 CFR § 273.7(b)(2) and (c)(1) and (h)(2)(ii)

Enter case notes to support actions taken regarding:

exemptions ,

good cause ,

designated provider , and

disqualifications .

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Time Limit for ABAWDS
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Time Limit for ABAWDS

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Time Limit for ABAWDS

body:
7 CFR § 273.24(c)(1)

An ABAWD (able-bodied adult without dependents) is a person who is age 18 through 64 years of age, is able to work, and is not living with a child under the age of 14. For example:

The person is an ABAWD the month after the person turns 18.

The person is not an ABAWD the month the person turns 65.

Review the status of any ABAWD at:

application,

PRF , and

renewal.

ABAWDs are limited to 3 full months of SNAP benefits, unless they meet the ABAWD work requirements or an exemption .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Time Limit for ABAWDS 1
heading: Time Limit for ABAWDS

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Time Limit for ABAWDS

body:
Source # 18

Effective Date: 11/01/2025
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Time Limit for ABAWDS 2
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Time Limit for ABAWDS

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Exemptions from Time Limits
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Exemptions from Time Limits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Exemptions from Time Limits

body:
7 CFR § 273.24(c)(2)-(6)

Individuals who are exempt from time limits are not required to comply with ABAWD work requirements and are not subject to the 3-month time limit .

Exempt ABAWDs from time limits if they meet one of the following criteria.

Younger than age 18

Age 65 or older

Are unfit for work

Receive benefits in the same SNAP household with a child under age 14

Note: The child does not have to be related to the ABAWD but must be in the ABAWD’s SNAP household. Allow the exemption if the only child in the household would be a member of the SNAP household but is ineligible to participate because they are disqualified or ineligible.

Are pregnant

Meet the discretionary exemption criteria

Meet a General Work Requirements Exemption

Note: Aside from being aged 60 or older

Are a member or descendant of a federally recognized, state-recognized, or terminated Native American tribe

Example : Indian , Urban Indian , California Indian more commonly referred to as Native American, Alaskan Native, Indigenous People, Tribal Member
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Exemptions from Time Limits 1
heading: Exemptions from Time Limits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Exemptions from Time Limits

body:
7 CFR § 273.24(g)(1)-(3)

Discretionary exemptions, approved by the SNAP Division, are granted to ABAWDs for one-month per individual during the 36 month period.

Policies must be applied consistently, and

Without discrimination.

ABAWDs must use their 3-countable months before the exemption is granted.

The cases are tracked in ImpaCT and over-usage may result in a fine from FNS.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Exemptions from Time Limits 2
heading: Discretionary Exemption

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Exemptions from Time Limits

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Work Requirements
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Work Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Work Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(m)(9)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.24(a)(1)-(4)

Any month an ABAWD meets one of the following work requirements is not a countable month :

Works at least 80 hours a month or make at least $217.50 a week.

Note: Work means employment, self-employment, volunteer work, or working in exchange for in-kind income ;

Participates at least 80 hours a month in any of the following qualifying work activities:

SNAP Employment and training (E&T) program operated and supervised by the State.

Programs under Section 236 of the Trade Act of 1974 for workers who have or may lose their job due to increased imports.

Programs under WIOA.

Any employment and training program serving veterans that is offered by the Department of Labor or Veterans Affairs.

Programs under Workforce Partnerships .

Note: Cannot use SNAP E&T funds

Combination of work and participation in qualifying work activities at least 80 hours a month.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Work Requirements 1
heading: ABAWD Work Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Work Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.24(b)(2)

A month is not countable if good cause is found.

To qualify for good cause, the ABAWD must :

still retain the job; or

plan to continue to participate in the activity that meets the work requirement; and

have a good reason that is beyond the individual’s control such as, but not limited to:

a household emergency,

illness of the ABAWD,

illness of another household member requiring the ABAWD’s presence,

lack of transportation, or

other reasons beyond the individual’s control.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Work Requirements 2
heading: Good Cause for Not Meeting the ABAWD Work Requirement

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Work Requirements

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Time Limits
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Time Limits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Time Limits

body:
7 CFR § 273.24(b) and (c)(1)-(6)

An ABAWD is limited to receiving 3-countable months of SNAP benefits in a 36-month period, unless they meet the ABAWD work requirements or an exemption . The 3-countable months can be non-consecutive.

Exception: ABAWDs may receive SNAP for an additional one-time 3-consecutive month period if the person regains eligibility .

Changes in circumstances may cause an individual’s status as an ABAWD to change. Time limits do not apply during periods when the individual is not identified as an ABAWD.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Time Limits 1
heading: Time Limits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Time Limits

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Tracking
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Tracking

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Tracking

body:
7 CFR § 273.24(b)(3)(i)-(ii)

ABAWD countable months are electronically tracked per person with an individual fixed clock. The 36-month clock begins with the month of application and looks forward 35 months for a total of 36 months.

Example: An ABAWD applies December 1, 2023. The individual fixed clock period begins December 1, 2023 through November 30, 2026.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Tracking 1
heading: ABAWD Tracking

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Tracking

body:
7 CFR § 273.24(b)(1)(i)-(iv)

ABAWD eligibility is time-limited to 3 months in any 36-month period in which the ABAWD is subject to, but no t complying with, the ABAWD work requirements. A month is a countable month unless the ABAWD:

is exempt from time limits

meets the ABAWD work requirement

receives prorated benefits

living in an exempt town

granted discretionary exemption

determined to have good cause

in the month of notification from SNAP E&T of a "provider determination"

Exception: Do not count months in Connecticut prior to November 30, 2023 when Connecticut had a statewide waiver of ABAWD rules.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Tracking 2
heading: Countable Month

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Tracking

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(xiv)(B)

Contact the other State if the ABAWD received SNAP benefits out-of-state to:

verify the SNAP benefits stopped, and

confirm countable months in that state.

Count any months that are part of the ABAWD’s 36-month period set in Connecticut.

Example: An ABAWD applies in July 2018 and states they received SNAP in another State in 2017. The individual has an established CT 36-month time period running from June 1, 2016 through May 31, 2019. The individual used one month in CT in June 2016 and one month in the other State in May 2017. The individual’s CT clock must be set to show 2 countable months used, and one countable month remaining.

Example: An ABAWD applies July 1, 2018 and states they received SNAP in another State in June 2018. The individual does not have an established CT 36-month time period. The worker verifies the receipt of SNAP and that benefits in the other state are closed. The ABAWD’s 36-month time period begins in CT and runs from July 1, 2018 through June 30, 2021.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Tracking 3
heading: Out-of-State Countable Months

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Tracking

body:
7 CFR § 273.24(b)(1)(i)-(iv)

When the 3-countable months are used, the ABAWD is no longer eligible for SNAP benefits, unless they meet an exemption or the ABAWD work requirements .

ImpaCT runs a batch process in the 3rd month to prevent further issuances and issues a report identifying the cases. Close the case or remove the ineligible ABAWD and issue an advance notice W-0001N, Notice of Action, advising:

why the individual is ineligible,

what months were countable,

how they can become eligible,

their new benefit amount (if applicable), and

their fair hearing rights.

See Ineligible Members for treatment of the income and assets of an ineligible ABAWD who is a member of an eligible SNAP household.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Tracking 4
heading: No Countable Months Left

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: ABAWD Tracking

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Regaining Eligibility
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Regaining Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Regaining Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.24(b) and (d)(1)-(3)

ABAWDs can regain eligibility at any time, if otherwise eligible, by meeting one of the following requirements:

Become exempt from ABAWD time limits.

Meet an ABAWD work requirement .

Note : Eligibility is not granted until verification is received that the 30-day work requirement participation hours were met.

Meet the requirements for the one-time 3-consecutive months .

There is no limit to the number of times ABAWDs can regain eligibility.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Regaining Eligibility 1
heading: Regaining Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Regaining Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.24(e)

Allow a one-time, consecutive 3 months in a 36-month period when the ABAWD met the ABAWD work requirements after receiving their 3-countable months , but no longer meets the work requirements. The one-time consecutive 3 months start:

when the individual notifies DSS they no longer meet the work requirement, or

DSS determines the individual isn’t meeting the work requirement.

Note: The three additional months only applies to time-limited participants that regain eligibility by meeting the ABAWD work requirement over 30 consecutive days; they do not apply to time-limited participants that become eligible again for other reasons.

Example: The ABAWD was working 80 hours a month since their 3-month countable months were used. The ABAWD reports in September they are no longer working. The one-time consecutive 3-month period runs for the months of October, November and December. The ABAWD becomes ineligible effective January 1 unless they meet a work requirement or an exemption.

When the one-time consecutive 3-month period starts, the 3 months continue uninterrupted for any reason, including case closure.

Example: An ABAWD begins the one-time consecutive 3 months in July. The consecutive months are July, August and September. The case closes effective September 1. The ABAWD reapplies in November but is not meeting any work requirement or an exemption. Even though the ABAWD didn’t receive benefits for September, they cannot receive the last month, because their additional consecutive 3 months ran through September.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Regaining Eligibility 2
heading: One-Time Consecutive 3-Month Period

Verify:

hours worked,

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Categorical Eligibility
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Categorical Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Categorical Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(j)

There are 2 different types of categorical eligibility: regular categorical eligibility (RCE) and expanded categorical eligibility (ECE).

RCE and ECE EDGs are not required to meet certain income and asset tests, and require less verifications .

Categorical eligibility applies to the EDG, not to the individual.

EDGs must follow and meet SNAP rules unless specifically identified in this item.

Determine if the EDG meets RCE first
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Categorical Eligibility 1
heading: Categorical Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Categorical Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(j)(4)(iii)(C) and 7 CFR 273.10(e)(2)(ii)(B)-(C)

Benefits for all EDGs are determined following regular SNAP eligibility rules.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Categorical Eligibility 2
heading: Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Categorical Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(v)

All EDGs follow simplified reporting requirements.

Exception: RCE and ECE EDGs are not required to report changes in assets .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Categorical Eligibility 3
heading: Reporting Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Categorical Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.18(a)(1)(i)

RCE and ECE cannot be canceled retroactively.

Do not establish a SNAP claim when an overpayment is:

due to excess assets for any EDG determined RCE or ECE, or

because an EDG should not have been categorically eligible due to mistake in granting eligibility for the program that made it RCE or ECE.

Claims can be made against RCE or ECE EDGs if an overpayment occurred because:

the benefit determination was not correct,

the EDG was ineligible for RCE , or

the EDG was ineligible for ECE .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Categorical Eligibility 4
heading: Overpayments and Claims

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Categorical Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(6) and (j)(2)

You must record in ImpaCT whether the EDG was or wasn’t found eligible for RCE or ECE.
