body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(i) and (2)

Verification is only needed for countable income.

Verify questionable income.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 1
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(i)

Before initial approval, verify all countable gross income.

Postpone verification for expedited service month(s), unless verification can be provided in time to meet the 7-day standard of promptness.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 2
heading: Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)(i)-(iii)

Act on the reported change if the EDG provides required verifications.

Send the EDG a W-3016, Notification from Department of Social Services, requesting any required verifications not provided with the PRF . Allow the EDG 10 days to verify the reported change.

Do not act on the reported change if the EDG fails to provide required verifications by the W-3016 due date. Verify and act on the change at renewal .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 3
heading: Increased Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(2)(i)-(ii)

Act on the reported change. Verify the change at renewal if verification is not provided with the PRF.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 4
heading: Decrease Benefits/Closes Case

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)(ii) & 273.12(a)(5)(vi)(B) and (c)(1)(ii)

Verify:

Change in the source of income, change in hours or stopping employment.

Amount changes of more than $50 a month from the amount last used to determine the CGs benefit.

Addition of a new EDG member.

Do not act on the reported change if the EDG fails to provide required verifications by the W-3016 due date. Verify and act on the change at renewal .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 5
heading: Benefit Increase

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(vi)(B)(1)-(4)

Decreased benefits are not processed for interim changes with certain exceptions. See Change Processing, Benefit Decrease/Case Closure . Verify changes at renewal.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 6
heading: Benefit Decrease

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)(i)

Before renewal, verify:

Change in the source, including starting, stopping or new income.

Amount changes of more than $50 a month from the amount last used to determine the CGs benefit.

Income of a new EDG member.

Information is incomplete, inconsistent, inaccurate, or outdated.

Do not verify income if the:

source has not changed, and

amount is unchanged, or has changed by $50 or less, unless the information is incomplete, inconsistent, inaccurate or outdated.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 7
heading: Renewal

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(5)(i) and (ii)

Base the income on the EDG’s written statement if good faith efforts to obtain the verification failed because the source failed to cooperate with the EDG and the worker.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 8
heading: Verification Not Available

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(3) & 273.9(c)(7)

Verify school attendance for a child under age 18 with earned income.

Verification of earnings is not required if the child is in school.

Verify the earnings if the child is not in school.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 9
heading: Child's Earnings

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
Verification Requirements by Element and Action

Suggested Verification Sources
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 10
heading: Resources

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expenses and Deductions
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expenses and Deductions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expenses and Deductions

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(xii) & 7 CFR § 273.9(d) & 7 CFR § 273.10(d)(1)(i)-(ii)(2)(4)

EDGs may receive 6 expenses and deductions from their income. These expenses and deductions are:

Dependent Care

Earned Income Deduction

Legally Obligated Child Support

Medical Expenses

Shelter Expenses

Standard Deduction

The EDG does not have to actually make a payment to get a deduction but the EDG must be responsible for the expense.

Exception: Child support must be paid to receive that deduction.

Expenses covered by excluded reimbursements or excluded vendor payments are not allowed.

This section defines allowable expenses. See Determining Income, Expenses, and Deductions for instructions on budgeting the expense.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expenses and Deductions 1
heading: Expenses and Deductions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expenses and Deductions

body:
Source # 4

Effective Date: 7/26/19
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expenses and Deductions 2
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Expenses and Deductions

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(4)

Allow the actual cost of dependent care expenses. Dependent care is limited to individuals:

under the age of 18, or

an incapacitated person of any age.

Note: The individual needing the care does not have to be a SNAP household member or live with the SNAP household as long as someone in the EDG is responsible for their care.

For example , a SNAP household member is responsible for care of her elderly disabled mother who doesn’t live them.

Examples of dependent care providers are:

Before or after school programs

Latch key programs, such as but not limited to:

The Boys and Girls Club

YMCA

YWCA

Private arrangements with individuals not in the EDG

Summer camps
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care 1
heading: Dependent Care

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(c)(3)(x) and (d)(4)

Allow dependent care costs only if the care is needed to permit an EDG member to:

Accept a job or continue working, regardless of whether the income is counted or excluded.

Attend training or education to prepare the individual for work.

Comply with work requirements .

Look for work.

Allow the following costs:

Activity or other fees required to participate in the care;

Transportation costs to and from program sites. Use the actual cost of public transportation or the Federal mileage rate ; and

Unreimbursed billed expense.

Note: Costs of care can be claimed as a medical expense , or a dependent care expense, but not both.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care 2
heading: Expense Allowed

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care 3
heading: Expense Not Allowed

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)

Verify the deduction only if questionable.

Review the Care 4 Kids database for payments. Care 4 Kids information is verified upon receipt .

For required verification not verified through Care 4 Kids, provide the EDG with form W-1224, SNAP Dependent Care Agreement, for each dependent care provider to verify the cost. The EDG and provider must complete and sign the form. Allow the EDG 10 days to return the form. Treat verification not provided in time to meet the action timeliness standard, but submitted later, as a verified change .

Note: The W-1224 is the preferred method of verifying dependent care expenses. The Verification Desk Guide also outlines other acceptable forms of verification, such as a signed statement from the provider, receipts, etc.

Take the following actions if verification is requested.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care 4
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(3)(ii) and (i)(3) and (4)(B)

Process the application without the expense if verification of questionable information is not submitted prior to the end of the 30-day processing period.

Postpone verification for expedited service month(s), unless verification can be provided in time to meet the 7-day processing timeframe.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care 5
heading: Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)-(2)

Increased Benefits:

Act on the reported change.

Verify if questionable. If questionable:

Send the EDG a W-1348, Proofs We Need, requesting any required verifications not provided with the PRF. Allow the EDG 10 days to verify the reported change.

Do not act on the reported change if the EDG fails to provide required verifications by the W-1348 due date. Verify and act on the change at renewal .

Decreased Benefits/Closes Case:

Act on the reported change. Verify questionable changes at renewal if verification is not provided with the PRF.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care 6
heading: Periodic Report

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)-(4)

Do not allow the expense if a new expense is reported and required verification is not submitted by the requested due date.

Take the following actions when a change in payment is reported and benefits will:

Increase – Get verification. Do not change the amount if verification is not provided. Use the previously verified amount.

Decrease - Process the change and get verification at renewal.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care 7
heading: Interim Change

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)(i)-(iv) & 7 CFR § 273.14(b)(4)

Process the renewal without the expense if a new expense or a change in expense is reported and required verification is not submitted by the requested due date.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care 8
heading: Renewal

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Dependent Care

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Earned Income Deduction
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Earned Income Deduction

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Earned Income Deduction

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(2) & 7 CFR § 273.11(a)(2) and (c)(1)-(3) & 7 CFR § 273.18(c)

Subtract 20% of countable gross earned income .

Note: For self-employment income , apply the 20% gross earned income deduction after subtracting allowable business expenses and adding other earned income, but before offsetting any remaining farm self-employment losses. See Self-Employment, Self-Employment Loss .

Exception: Do not allow the 20% gross earned income deduction when determining an overpayment amount if the EDG failed to report earned income correctly or timely. See Overpayment for more details.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Earned Income Deduction 1
heading: Earned Income Deduction

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Earned Income Deduction

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(5)

Allow a child support deduction when the support is legally obligated and is:

Paid by an EDG member for a child who is not part of the SNAP EDG; or

Paid by an EDG member to an agency or individual outside of the EDG if the payments are not returned to the EDG.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support 1
heading: Legally Obligated Child Support

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(xii) & 7 CFR § 273.9(d)(5) & 7 CFR § 273.10(d)(8)

Allow the amount actually paid by the EDG member up to the court-ordered amount. Include court ordered payments for, but not limited to:

Clothing

Court costs

Education

Healthcare

Interest

Legal fees

Monthly child support

Other specific needs of the child or children

Past-due (arrearage) payments if the payments were originally court-ordered.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support 2
heading: Child Support Amount

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(xii) & 7 CFR § 273.9(d)(5) & 7 CFR § 273.10(d)(1)(i)-(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.11(c)(1)-(3)

Do not allow payments if the following occurs:

The payment is made by someone outside the household.

The payment is made through an IRS offset payment .

The payment is defined as:

alimony ,

property settlement, or

spousal support.

The payment is not court-ordered (voluntary).
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support 3
heading: Expense Not Allowed

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(xii)

Before allowing as a deduction, verify the:

actual amount paid,

legal obligation,

legally obligated amount, and

expense is paid to a non-EDG member.

Check CCSES for verification of the:

EDG’s legal obligation to pay child support,

EDG’s amount of obligation, and

payments the EDG actually made.

CCSES information is verified upon receipt .

For required verification not verified through CCSES, provide the EDG with form W-1348, Proofs We Need. Allow the EDG 10 days to provide the verification. Treat verification not provided in time to meet the action timeliness standard, but submitted later, as a verified change .

Take the following actions if verification is requested:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support 4
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(3)(ii) and (i)(3) and (4)(B)

Process the application without the deduction if required verification is not submitted prior to the end of the 30-day processing period.

Postpone verification for expedited service month(s), unless verification is provided in time to meet the 7-day standard of promptness.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support 5
heading: Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)-(2)

Increased Benefits –

Act on the reported change if the EDG provides verifications.

If verifications are not provided:

Send the EDG a W-1348, Proofs We Need, requesting any required verifications not provided with the PRF. Allow the EDG 10 days to verify the reported change.

Do not act on the reported change if the EDG fails to provide required verifications by the W-1348 due date. Verify and act on the change at renewal .

Decreased Benefits/Closes Case:

Act on the reported change. Verify changes at renewal if verification is not provided with the PRF.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support 6
heading: Periodic Report

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)-(4)

Do not allow the expense if a new expense is reported and required verification is not submitted by the requested due date.

Take the following actions when a change in obligation/payment is reported and benefits will:

Increase – Verify. Do not change the amount if verification is not provided. Use the previously verified amount

Decrease - Process the change and verify at renewal.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support 7
heading: Interim Change

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)(i)-(iv) & 7 CFR § 273.14(b)(4)

Process the renewal without the deduction if a new obligation or change in obligation/payment is reported and required verification is not submitted prior to the end of processing time period.

Verify the interim change if a change in obligation/payment was reported, but not verified when reported. Process without the payment if verification is not submitted by the due date.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support 8
heading: Renewal

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Legally Obligated Child Support

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(3)

Allow a medical expense deduction for:

Elderly or disabled EDG members;

EDG members receiving emergency SSI benefits based on presumptive eligibility; or

A non-EDG member meeting the following requirements:

The elderly/disabled definition;

The individual was in the EDG immediately prior to being placed in an institution (hospital, nursing home, etc.) or death of the member;

The individual cannot establish SNAP eligibility on his/her own; and

The EDG is paying the medical expenses of the individual.

Ongoing and one-time expenses are allowed but have different budgeting procedures. See Determining Income, Expenses and Deductions .

Medical expenses start the month the individual:

turns age 60, or

is determined disabled.

Explore medical expenses during the interview process for EDGs with at least one elderly or disabled member.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses 1
heading: Medical Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses 2
heading: Allowable Medical Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(3) and (iii)(B)-(iv) & 7 CFR § 273.10(d)(1)(i)-(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.11(c)(1)-(3)

Do not allow the following medical expenses:

Expenses paid or that will be paid by another source (insurance, Medicare, Medicaid, etc.).

Insurance premiums for health and accident policies such as:

those payable in lump sum settlements for death or dismemberment, or

income maintenance policies such as those that continue mortgage or loan payments.

Schedule One controlled substances, including medical marijuana.

Special diets regardless if the diet is related to a medical condition.

Examples:

Ensure

Juices

Nutritional supplements, etc.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses 3
heading: Expense Not Allowed

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(3)

Allow a monthly medical expense deduction if the EDG’s allowed medical expenses are more than $35.00.

The medical expense deduction is the total cost of the allowed medical expenses minus $35.

Example: The EDG has total allowable medical expenses of $173. The medical expense deduction is $138. ($173 - $35).
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses 4
heading: Medical Expense Deduction

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(iv)

Verify medical expenses, including the amount of reimbursements, before allowing a deduction.

Verify any questionable factors, such as the eligibility of the elderly or disabled individual, or if services provided are allowable.

Provide the EDG with form W-1348, Proofs We Need, for any required verification. Allow the EDG 10 days to provide the verification. Treat verification not provided in time to meet the action timeliness standard, but submitted later, as a verified change.

Take the following actions if verification is requested.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses 5
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(3)(ii) and (i)(3) and (4)(B)

Process the application without the deduction if required verification is not submitted prior to the end of the 30-day processing period.

Postpone verification for expedited service month(s), unless verification is provided in time to meet the 7-day standard of promptness.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses 6
heading: Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(iii)(D)-(E)

Increased benefits – Verify:

new expenses, or

changes of more than $25 in medical expenses reported by the EDG.

Do not allow new or changed expenses if required verification is not provided. Continue with the previously verified expenses.

Decreased benefits - Process the change and get verification of changes more than $25 at renewal .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses 7
heading: Periodic Report

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)-(4)

Act only on those changes in medical expenses reported by a source other than the household when those changes are verified upon receipt and do not require contact with the household.

Example: Act on a Medicare Part B premium change reported through BENDEX which is verified upon receipt.

Do not act on changes in medical expenses reported by a source other than the household which require contact with the household for verification or information in order to take action.

Example: A caregiver reports that the disabled member no longer needs oxygen. Since the EDG would have to be contacted to verify this, no action is taken.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses 8
heading: Interim Change

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)(i)-(iv) & 7 CFR § 273.14(b)(4)

Verify:

previously unreported medical expenses, and

changes in medical expenses of more than $25.

Process the renewal without the deduction if required verification is not submitted prior to the end of the processing time period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses 9
heading: Renewal

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(ii)-(iii)

Use the households’s shelter expenses for the calculation of net income and the benefit amount.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 1
heading: Shelter Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(ii)(A)

The shelter expense must be a continuing charge:

leading to ownership of the residence, or

rental payments for the residence.

Only allow expenses for the residence the household lives in.

Exception: Expenses for an unoccupied home are allowed if the home meets the unoccupied home requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 2
heading: Basic Shelter

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(ii)(A)(E)

Allow costs of current rent or mortgages including:

All mortgage loan repayments, including 2nd mortgages and home equity loans as long as the home property is used as collateral for the loan.

Condominium and association fees .

Escrow payments for property taxes and insurance .

Interest fees on mortgage/mobile home payments.

Payments to buy a mobile home .

Rent payments, including lot rent.

Unreimbursed repair or replacements costs for a home that was substantially damaged by a natural disaster, such as a flood or fire .

Do not allow the following costs:

Amounts added to the current rent for previously unpaid rent.

Deposits or down payments.

Extra charges, such as late payment fees.

Repair or replacement costs unless they result from a natural disaster such as fire or flood.

Note: Do not allow the costs if they will be covered by a reimbursement.

Note: Expenses can only be allowed if they are owed to someone outside of the household and the household makes a money payment. If someone outside the household pays the household's expenses, they are not allowable.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 3
heading: Rent and Mortgage

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(ii)(B)

Allow property taxes, including state and local assessments, as shelter expenses.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 4
heading: Property Taxes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(ii)(B)

Allow the cost of insurance on the structure itself, such as fire, flood and liability insurance, if the policy separates the costs of the structure from the contents. Allow the entire amount if the insurance policy does not separate these costs.

Allow service charges such as an added fee for monthly billing but do not allow late payment charges.

Do not allow renters insurance .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 5
heading: Property Insurance

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(ii)(D) and (iii)(C)

Allow shelter costs of an unoccupied home if the household intends to return to the home and the members are away from the home for:

abandonment because of a natural disaster or casualty loss,

employment,

illness, or

training.

Do not allow unoccupied home costs if:

the home is rented out or leased during the absence of the household members; or

current residents are claiming shelter costs for the home for SNAP in any state.

Allow only one utility allowance to cover both residences. Allow the highest applicable utility allowance.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 6
heading: Unoccupied Home

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(ii)(A)-(E)

Give each SNAP household a deduction for their part of the basic shelter expenses when separate SNAP households share a residence, and one gives its share of basic shelter expenses to the other who then pays the expense. See Shared Households .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 7
heading: Multiple SNAP households in One Residence

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(iii)(A) and (E)

Allow one utility allowance. Assign the appropriate utility allowance based on the household’s utility obligation. The utility allowances are:

Standard Utility Allowance (SUA)

Limited Utility Allowance (LUA)

Telephone Allowance (TUA)

See Utility Allowances for amounts.

See Determining the Utility Allowance .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 8
heading: Utility Allowances

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(ii)(C)

Cooking Fuel

Cooling (air-conditioning)

Electricity

Excess heating or cooling charges

Fees charged for installation of utilities

Garbage and trash collection

Heating fuel, including propane tank rental fee and wood purchases when wood is the primary heating source

Installation of a utility

Phone, includes landlines or cell phones

Sewage

Water

Well and septic tank installation and maintenance
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 9
heading: Allowable Utility Costs

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(iii)(A) and (C)

A household qualifies for SUA if they meet one of the following:

Is responsible for paying heating or cooling costs directly to the provider, landlord, or to someone with whom the household shares these expenses.

Note: This includes paying excess or flat fees for these costs.

Contains a member who is age 60+ and/or disabled member and:

Received a DCB payment of at least $20.01 in the current month or previous 12 months.

Received a SUA$ payment of at least $20.01 in the current month or previous 12 months.

Receives a HUD , FmHA , or other program energy assistance payment, reimbursement or vendor payment.

The SUA covers all utilities, including the phone.

Heating costs must be for primary heating and does not include:

Electric blankets

Heat lamps

Use of a cooking stove to heat

Cooling costs are for central or room air conditioners, but does not include fans.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 10
heading: Standard Utility Allowance (SUA)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(iii)(A) and (C)

The LUA amount includes all non - heating/ non - cooling costs including installation/maintenance.

Assign the LUA to a household that does not qualify for the SUA, but is responsible for paying any portion of at least 2 of the following expenses directly to the provider, landlord, or to someone with whom the household shares these expenses:

Electricity

Garbage/trash collection

Gas or other fuel used for any purpose other than heating or cooling

Phone

Water/sewage

Well and septic tank installation and maintenance
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 11
heading: Limited Utility Allowance (LUA)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(iii)(A) and (D)

Give the telephone allowance to households when this is their only utility expense. Allow the full TUA, including when the household shares telephone expenses. Telephone includes landlines and cell phones.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 12
heading: Telephone Allowance (TUA)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(iii)(E)-(F)

Give each SNAP household the full amount of the appropriate SUA, LUA or TUA as long as each household is responsible for the cost.

Example: Two SNAP households split the heating costs for the residence, each receives the full SUA.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 13
heading: Multiple SNAP Households in One Residence

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.11(c)(2)(iii)

Allow an excess shelter deduction for the total costs of the Basic Shelter and Utility Standard in excess of 50% of the household’s income, after all other deductions are subtracted, up to the excess shelter deduction maximum .

Exception: Households with an elderly or disabled member do not have a maximum for the shelter deduction, unless the only elderly or disabled member is ineligible .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 14
heading: Excess Shelter Deduction

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(i)

Allow a homeless shelter deduction for the total costs of shelter and utility expenses in excess of 50% of the household’s income, after all other deductions are subtracted when the household meets the definition of homelessness and the household incurs, or is reasonably expected to incur shelter costs in that month. If a homeless household incurs shelter costs in excess of the homeless shelter deduction maximum , the household can claim actual costs up to the excess shelter deduction maximum .

Note: Some homeless households may have shelter/utility costs outside of the norm - i.e. laundry costs, payments to a hotel/motel, costs of living in a vehicle, etc. which can be applied toward the homeless shelter deduction. There is no limit to the number of times a homeless household can claim this deduction.

Exception: Homeless households who are in receipt of the SUA$ or who are entitled to the traditional SUA are not eligible to receive this deduction.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 15
heading: Homeless Shelter Deduction

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(iii) and (2)(i) and (3)(i)-(ii)

Verification is not required unless the information is questionable.

Exception: Verify utility expenses for an unoccupied home if claiming the expense.

Provide the household with form W-1348, Proofs We Need, for any required verification. Allow 10 days to provide the verification. If verification is submitted after the action timeliness standard, treat is as a verified change .

Take the following actions only if verification is requested:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 16
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(3)(ii) and (i)(3) and (4)(B)

Process the application without the expense if verification for a questionable expense is not submitted prior to the end of the 30-day processing period.

Postpone verification for expedited service month(s), unless verification can be provided in time to meet the 7-day standard of promptness.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 17
heading: Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)-(2)

For increased benefits, act on the reported change if not questionable.

If questionable, send the household a W-1348, Proofs We Need, requesting any required verifications not provided with the PRF. Allow 10 days to verify the reported change.

If required verification is not provided by the due date:

If a new expense was reported (at the current address or due to a move), do not allow the expense.

If a change in a current expense was reported, do not act on the change.

Verify and act on the change at renewal .

For decreased benefits/closes case, act on the reported change. Verify questionable changes at renewal if verification is not provided with the PRF.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 18
heading: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)-(4)

For increases, act on the reported change if not questionable.

If questionable , send the household a W-1348, Proofs We Need, requesting any required verifications not provided with the PRF. Allow 10 days to verify the reported change.

If required verification is not provided by the due date:

If a new expense was reported (at the current address or due to a move), do not allow the expense.

If a change in a current expense was reported, do not act on the change.

Verify and act on the change at renewal .

Decreases are not processed for interim changes with certain exceptions. See Change Processing, Benefit Decrease/Case Closure . Verify questionable information at renewal.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 19
heading: Interim Change

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)(i)-(iv) & 7 CFR § 273.14(b)(4)

Process without the expense if a new expense or a change in expense is reported and required verification is not submitted by requested due date.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses 20
heading: Renewal

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelter Expenses

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Standard Deduction
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Standard Deduction

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Standard Deduction

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(1)(i) and (iii) & 7 CFR § 273.11(c)(1)-(3)

All SNAP households receive a standard deduction from the household’s total income based on the number of eligible members. See Standard Deduction .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Standard Deduction 1
heading: Standard Deduction

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Standard Deduction

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Income, Expenses and Deductions
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Income, Expenses and Deductions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Income, Expenses and Deductions

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(a)(1)(i)-(ii)

Establish eligibility by determining:

if the EDG meets program rules, and

if eligible, the benefit amount.

Base eligibility on the EDG’s anticipated circumstances for the month in which benefits will be issued. Determine eligibility for the entire month using the best estimate of:

income,

expenses, and

information available for that month.

Calculate a monthly amount by gathering the following information for each income and expense source:

when started,

how often received,

when received,

period of time intended for, and

known future changes.

Use the information to:

budget current monthly circumstances, and

identify future changes, if any.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Income, Expenses and Deductions 1
heading: Determining Income, Expenses and Deductions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Income, Expenses and Deductions

body:
Source #

Effective Date:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Income, Expenses and Deductions 2
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Income, Expenses and Deductions

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Month of Application
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Month of Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Month of Application

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(a)(1)(i)-(iii) and (3)-(4) and (c)-(d) and (e)(3)

Use the EDG’s circumstances for the entire month of application. Count income actually received or anticipated to be received during the application month, as follows:

The actual income the EDG received up to the date of application.

Note : Budget income received in the application month even if it does not reflect ongoing income.

Any other income the EDG anticipates receiving for the remainder of the application month.

Average income received less often than monthly over the period the income is intended to cover.

Example : see Income Received Less Often than Monthly .

Count allowable expenses incurred or anticipated to be incurred during the application month.

Note : Allow recurring expenses billed less often than monthly if the expense will be billed in the benefit period. See Averaging Expenses .

Prorate benefits for the application month based on the application file date .

See Migrants or Seasonal Farmworkers for special month of application income treatment for destitute migrant or seasonal farmworker EDGs.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Month of Application 1
heading: Month of Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Month of Application

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(1)(i)-(ii)

Budget income the EDG is reasonably certain to receive.

Do not budget income if the amount or receipt date is uncertain.

Example see Income Date and Receipt Unknown .

Count nonrecurring lump sum income as an asset, not as income.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income 1
heading: Anticipating Monthly Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(2)(i)

Count income only in the month it is anticipated to be received, unless the income is averaged .

Count income that stops until it is no longer received. Remove the stopped income the following month.

Example: See Income Stops .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income 2
heading: Income Only in the Month Received

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(2)(iii)

Count income received monthly or semi-monthly for the month it was intended when the income is received in a different month because of mailing cycles, holidays, or weekends.

Example: See Income for the Month Intended .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income 3
heading: Income for the Month Intended

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(1)(i)-(ii)

Representative income is income amounts within the range of what would be the typical or usual amount of the EDG’s income. Talk with the EDG to determine their representative income based on their situation.

Income significantly higher or lower would not be representative.

Examples of non-representative income include:

Nonrecurring overtime

One-time bonuses

Work missed due to sickness or emergencies
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income 4
heading: Representative Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(2)(ii)

Do not count wages withheld by the employer unless the wages were withheld at the request of the employee.

Exception: Count wages legally obligated to the employee.

Count wages paid in advance only if they can be reasonably anticipated .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income 5
heading: Wages Held by the Employer

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Anticipating Monthly Income

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(1)(ii)

Determine income for the month using the best estimate of income available to arrive at a representative amount. Use the prior 30-days income if it represents the EDG’s anticipated income.

Exception: Use a longer period of time if the prior 30-days income is not representative.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income 1
heading: Determining Monthly Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(2)(i)

Convert income paid more often than monthly to a monthly amount. Conversion factors are:

The average weekly amount multiplied by 4.3

The average bi-weekly amount multiplied by 2.15

Received twice a month ( semi-monthly ) multiplied by 2
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income 2
heading: Income Conversion

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income

body:
QC Handbook

Stable income is income received in a fixed amount from the same source on a regular schedule.

Examples include, but are not limited to:

Maximum UCB

Retirement income

Social Security benefits

SSI

State employee wages

Convert stable income received more often than monthly to a monthly amount.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income 3
heading: Stable Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(3)(i)-(iii)

Average income when the income amounts or receipt dates are inconsistent. To determine an average amount:

Add the gross income for the time period used;

Divide the total gross income (Step 1) by the number of pay dates used;

Multiply the average pay (Step 2) by the conversion factor , if applicable.

The result is the gross monthly average.

Use the prior 30 days or a longer period of time if necessary to accurately anticipate the EDG’s representative income .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income 4
heading: Income Averaging

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(3)(ii)

Average contractual income intended as annual income over a 12-month period, even if the income is paid in a shorter time frame.

Average contractual income that is not the EDG’s annual income over the period the income is intended to cover.

Examples: See Contractual Income .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income 5
heading: Contractual Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(1)(i)-(ii)

Average fluctuating income that is usually paid at a set time but in different amounts.

Examples include, but are not limited to:

Child support payments

Wages with inconsistent hours

Examples: See Fluctuating Income .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income 6
heading: Fluctuating Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(3)(ii)

Average income received less often than monthly over the time period the income is intended to cover.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income 7
heading: Income Covering More Than One Month

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(1)(ii) and (3)(i)

Average intermittent income that is not paid regularly, or starts and stops without a known schedule.

Examples include, but are not limited to:

On-call work

Temporary jobs

Intermittent income also includes unexpected income that cannot reasonably be expected to recur, such as a cash gift.

Exclude up to $30 of income per federal fiscal quarter when the income is received too infrequently or intermittently to be reasonably anticipated .

Average the income if over $30 per quarter and it can be reasonably anticipated to be received.

Examples: See Intermittent Income .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income 8
heading: Intermittent Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(3)(ii)

See Self-employment for budgeting procedures.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income 9
heading: Self-Employment Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Income

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Monthly Expense Deductions
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Monthly Expense Deductions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Monthly Expense Deductions

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(d)

Monthly expenses used to determine deductions are:

Dependent Care Expenses

Earned Income Deduction

Legally Obligated Child Support Expenses

Medical Expenses

Shelter Expenses

Standard Deduction
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Monthly Expense Deductions 1
heading: Monthly Expense Deductions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Monthly Expense Deductions

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(d)(1)(i)-(ii)

Do not allow an expense if:

It is an amount from past billing periods, even if actually paid in the current month.

The expense is paid for by some in-kind benefit, such as food.

The service is provided by someone residing in the EDG.

The expense is paid by someone outside of the EDG. These payments may be reimbursements or vendor payments .

Exceptions :

Expenses paid by someone outside of the EDG who states the money paid for the expense is a loan and must be paid back to them.

Utility expenses are allowed if paid by an energy assistance payment. See Standard Utility Allowance .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Monthly Expense Deductions 2
heading: Disallowed Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Monthly Expense Deductions

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(d)(2)-(4)

Determine expenses for the month using expenses that represent the EDG’s anticipated expenses.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses 1
heading: Determining Monthly Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(d)(4)

Allow expenses reasonably certain to be billed during the benefit period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses 2
heading: Anticipating Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(d)(2)

Allow expenses only in the month they are billed or otherwise become due, regardless of when the EDG intends to pay the expense.

Exception : See Averaging Expenses .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses 3
heading: Billed Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(2)(i) and (d)(5)

Convert expenses billed more often than monthly to a monthly amount. Conversion factors are:

The average weekly amount multiplied by 4.3

The average bi-weekly amount multiplied by 2.15

Billed twice a month ( semi-monthly ) multiplied by 2
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses 4
heading: Expense Conversion

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(d)(3)

EDGs may choose to have fluctuating expenses averaged. These are expenses usually billed at a set time but in different amounts.

EDGs may also choose to have expenses which are billed less often than monthly averaged forward over the time between scheduled billings, or if there is no scheduled billing, averaged forward over the period the expense is intended to cover.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses 5
heading: Averaging Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(d)(3)

EDGs may choose to have one-time only expenses:

allowed in only one month, or

averaged over the entire benefit period in which they are billed. Averaging begins the month the change is effective.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses 6
heading: One-Time Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(d)(3)

EDGs may choose to have one-time only medical expenses:

allowed in only one month, or

averaged over the remaining months of their benefit period. Averaging begins the month the change is effective.

Exception: EDGs with 24-month benefit periods reporting the medical expense in the first 12 months, have the option of averaging the expense over the remaining months of:

the first 12 months of the benefit period, or

the 24 months of the benefit period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses 7
heading: One-Time Medical Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Monthly Expenses

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Limits
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Limits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Limits

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Limits 1
heading: Income Limits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Limits

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(j)(2)(ii)(A)-(B) and (iii) and (v) and (4)(iii)(B) (ii)(A)-(B) and (iii) & 7 CFR 273.9(a)(1)

Regular Categorical Eligibility (RCE) EDGs do not have a gross or net income limit.

Expanded Categorical Eligibility (ECE) EDGs gross income must be below 200% of the FPL . ECE’s do not have a net income limit.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Limits 2
heading: Categorical Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Limits

body:
7 CFR 273.9(a)(1)(i)

The total of all non-excluded gross income of all EDG members must not be greater than the gross income limit for that EDG size unless :

the EDG is RCE ; or

an EDG member is elderly or disabled .

Exception: The gross income limit applies if the only elderly or disabled member is ineligible .

When the EDG’s circumstances change and they become entitled to a different income limit, apply the new income limit whenever the EDG’s eligibility, benefit level, or benefit period changes, whichever occurs first.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Limits 3
heading: Gross Income Limit

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Limits

body:
7 CFR 273.9(a)(2)(i)

The EDG’s net income cannot be greater than the net income limit for the EDG’s size, unless the EDG is categorically eligible .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Limits 4
heading: Net Income Limit

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Limits

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(e)(1)-(2)

Benefits are calculated automatically by ImpaCT.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels 1
heading: Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(e)(1)(i)-(ii)

Using the W-1216 , manually calculate the EDG’s net monthly income as follows. Do not round at any step in the calculation process.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels 2
heading: Manual Net Income Calculation

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(e)(1)(i)(A)-(I)

Total gross monthly earned income.

Add total gross monthly unearned income.

Subtract farm self-employment losses , if any from the result of Step 2. The result is the EDG’s total gross income.

If the result of Step 3 is over the gross income limit for that EDG size, stop here and deny the case.

Exceptions : See Gross Income Limit .

Subtract 20% of the total earned income from Step 1 from the total gross income result of Step 3.

Subtract the standard deduction for the EDG’s size.

Subtract the allowed net medical expenses (after the $35 is deducted).

Subtract the allowed dependent care expenses .

Subtract the allowed child support expenses . The result, thus far, is the remaining income.

Total allowed shelter expenses , then subtract 50% of the remaining income (determined in Step 9) to arrive at a net shelter amount.

Compare the net shelter amount (determined in Step 10) to the excess shelter deduction or homeless shelter deduction . If eligible for

homeless shelter, deduction and costs are below the standard, apply the homeless shelter deduction. If costs are in excess of the homeless

shelter deduction, apply actual costs up to the excess shelter deduction. When comparing net shelter amount to excess shelter deduction,

select the lower of the two amounts.

Exception: EDGs with an elderly or disabled member are not subject to the shelter deduction maximum. Allow the total excess shelter.

Exception: Allow the lower of the excess shelter deduction or the excess shelter amount if the only elderly or disabled member is ineligible .

Subtract the excess shelter amount (determined in Step 11) from the remaining income to arrive at the net income for the EDG.

If the total is over the net income limit for that EDG size, stop here and deny the case.

Exception : Categorically eligible EDGs do not have a net income test.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels 3
heading: Net Income Calculation

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(e)(1)(i)-(ii) and (2)(ii)-(iii)(A)

Multiply the net income times 30%.

Round up to the nearest dollar if the 30% amount ends in cents (Example: $100 X 30% = $30.30, rounded to $31).

Deduct the rounded 30% figure from the EDG’s maximum benefit .

No benefit is issued if the initial month benefits are less than $10.

After the initial month , EDGs with one or 2 individuals are eligible for the minimum benefit if the end result is less than that amount.

After the initial month, EDGs with 3 or more individuals are eligible for the actual amount. If the actual amount is:

$1, $3, or $5, round up to $2, $4 or $6, respectively;

zero, close the case.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels 4
heading: Benefit Calculation

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes

body:
Income Examples

Suggested Verification Sources
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes 1
heading: Resources

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(6)

Enter case notes to support case actions when determining eligibility, including but not limited to the following information:

Dates and amounts of income verifications used

EDG’s statement about their anticipated circumstances

Explanation of changes

Pay date vs pay period ending date

Questionable or unclear information
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes 2
heading: Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Self-Employment
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Self-Employment

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Self-Employment

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(b)(1)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.11(a)-(b)

Self-employment income is earned directly from a business or profession, rather than a specified salary or wages from an employer.

Self-employment exists when there is no employer/employee relationship.

Tax and Social Security payments are the self-employed individual’s responsibility.

Examples include, but are not limited to:

Dependent care providers

Farmers

Independent contractors

Newspaper carriers

Income received from a partnership or S-Corporation is self-employment income.

Income received from a C-Corporation is not self-employment income. Treat income from a corporation as follows:

Wages and profits paid from a corporation are earned income. Budget like any other earned income .

Annualize dividends and other unearned income paid from the corporation.

Note: Do not budget corporation losses identified on IRS Form Schedule E.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Self-Employment 1
heading: Self-Employment

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Self-Employment

body:
Source # 4

Effective Date: 7/26/19
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Self-Employment 2
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Self-Employment

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Assets
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Assets

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Assets

body:
7 CFR § 273.8(e)(3) and (5) and (9) and (g)

Exclude self-employment income from the asset calculation during the period when the income is annualized or averaged .

Example: Applicant states income tax amounts are anticipated to stay the same for the current year. Gross income is $50,000 (Schedule F, Line 9). She has $51,000 in her checking account. Count $1,000 as an asset ($51,000 – $50,000). Self-employment income cannot be counted as an asset during the period of time the income is intended to cover.

Note: Exclude property needed for business and farm self-employment .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Assets 1
heading: Assets

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Assets

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(b)(1)(ii)

Self-employment income is generally reported on income tax forms . Determine the income from the following forms if tax forms are not filed:

Business accounts

Bookkeeping records

Other records maintained by the business owner that allows a calculation of the income and expenses

Sales and expenditures records
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income 1
heading: Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(b)(1)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.10(a)(2) and (3)

Gross self-employment is the anticipated or actual total, before expenses, of all money the business produces, including the full amount of capital gains .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income 2
heading: Gross Self-Employment Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(b)(1)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.10(a)(2) & 7 CFR 273.11(a)(2)

Net self-employment income is the gross self-employment income minus allowable self-employment expenses .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income 3
heading: Net Self-Employment Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(3)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.11(a)(1)

Budget self-employment income over a 12-month period if the reported income represents an EDG’s annual support, even if the income is received in a shorter period of time.

Example: An EDG of 4 operates a highly profitable business during the summer months earning $40,000 after expenses. By January the EDG reports they have exhausted their income and need SNAP assistance. The self-employment income is annualized because it is the household’s annual support. They do not have any other income available in the non-self-employment months.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income 4
heading: Annualizing Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(c)(3)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.11(a)(1)(i)

Average income over the number of months the income is intended to cover if the income is not intended for the EDG’s annual support .

Example: An individual operates their own concession stand during the summer months and waits tables during the winter months. The self-employment income is averaged over the summer months because the income is only intended for those months. The individual has other income available during the other months.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income 5
heading: Averaging Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Allowable Expenses
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Allowable Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Allowable Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 7 CFR 273.10(d)(3)-(5) & 7 CFR § 273.11(a)(2) and (b)(1)

Allowable self-employment expenses include, but are not limited to:

Advertisement costs

Business portion of the total shelter expense

Business supplies

Insurance for buildings, business vehicles, machinery, and trade

Labor expenses, including employer taxes, insurance premiums, and wages

Lease or rental payments, including for equipment, storage, warehouse

Legal and professional fees

Licensing fees and permits

Lodging while away from home

Maintenance and repairs on business equipment and property

Payments (principal and interest) for the purchase prices of income-producing real estate, capital assets , equipment, machinery

Providing meals for non-EDG members

Supplies, including feed, fertilizer, plants, raw materials, seeds, stock

Taxes paid on income producing property

Convert the allowable expenses to a monthly amount following annualizing or averaging income policy.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Allowable Expenses 1
heading: Allowable Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Allowable Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(ii)(A)-(E) & 7 CFR § 273.11(b)(1)

Allow only the business portion of the total shelter expense when the business is located in the home or on the home property. Prorate the self-employment expense based on the percent of the property used for an in-home office or if it is on the home property. Allow the remaining portion as the EDG’s shelter expense deduction .

Note: Do not count the same deductions twice, as both shelter costs and self-employment expenses.

Example: The applicant has a gift shop in her home where she sells handmade jewelry. The home has 6 rooms including the gift shop. Divide the basic shelter costs for the building by 6 (rooms) and allow 1/6 of the cost as a self-employment expense. Allow the EDG 5/6 of the basic shelter costs and the appropriate utility standard as its shelter expense. Do not allow any of the utility costs as a self-employment expense.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Allowable Expenses 2
heading: Business Expense vs EDG Shelter Expense

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Allowable Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(b)(2)(i)-(iv)

The following self-employment expenses are not allowed:

Amounts exceeding the payment made by a boarder

Depreciation

Fines and Penalties

Income taxes:

Federal

State

Local

Personal expenses including but not limited to:

Charitable contributions

Dependent care – allowed as a dependent care deduction

Funds for the owner’s retirement or health insurance

Life and medical insurance payments

Normal living expenses

Portion of shelter expenses used for the home

Transportation to and from the work site
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Allowable Expenses 3
heading: Disallowed Self-Employment Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Allowable Expenses

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Calculation
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Calculation

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Calculation

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(b)(1)(ii) and (c)(9) & 7 CFR § 273.10(a)(2)(i)

Subtract the allowable self-employment expenses from the gross self-employment income to determine the net self-employment income . Determine the EDG’s monthly net income by:

dividing the net self-employment income by 12 months for annualized income , or

dividing the net self-employment by the number of months the income is intended to cover for averaged income .

Budget the net self-employment income following Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Calculation 1
heading: Income Calculation

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Calculation

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(a)(1)(i)

A new business is one that has been operating less than 12 months.

Average the income and expenses the business received and projects receiving in the coming year, over the period of time the business has been operating.

Example: The EDG started a daycare in their home 4 months ago. Their gross self-employment income for the 4 months is $3,200. They had $200.00 in allowable expenses in the 4 months. They expect the income and expenses to be the same in the coming year. Determine the EDG’s eligibility based on their business records to-date. $3,200/4=$800, $200/4=$50, $800-$50=$750 per month. Budget $750.

Budget zero if the business has not y et received income.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Calculation 2
heading: New Business

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Calculation

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(a)(1)(i)

Budget income based on anticipated amounts if the EDG reports the averaged or annualized amount does not accurately reflect the EDG’s actual monthly amounts because of a substantial increase or decrease in the business.

Use income and expense amounts for the months after the substantial change and, if needed, add additional amounts the EDG anticipates, and divide by the number of months used. Do not use prior earnings.

Example: An individual farms hay and last year had an annual gross self-employment income of $80,000. After a hurricane, the majority of the crop was lost this year. The farmer anticipates the remaining crop will only bring in $15,000. The farmer did not have crop insurance. Determine eligibility using the anticipated income of $15,000 instead of the previous year’s income.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Calculation 3
heading: Substantial Change

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Income Calculation

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Specific Types of Self-Employment
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Specific Types of Self-Employment

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Specific Types of Self-Employment

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(b)(1)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.11(b)(3)(ii)(A)

Income received for a commercial boarding house is earned income, after deducting allowable self-employment expenses .

Income received for non-commercial boarding houses is earned income if the boarder is not included in the SNAP EDG. Count as allowable expenses the actual verified costs of providing room and meals.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Specific Types of Self-Employment 1
heading: Boarders

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Specific Types of Self-Employment

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(b)(3)(i)

Count as earned income payments received for providing dependent care in the EDG’s own residence.

Note: Review CCMIS for dependent care payments paid through Care 4 Kids .

Determine the allowable expense using the actual verified allowable costs of providing the dependent care.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Specific Types of Self-Employment 2
heading: Dependent Care Providers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Specific Types of Self-Employment

body:
General Definition

Count as earned income payments received from cultivating land or crops, or raising animals.

Examples include, but are not limited to:

Crops

Dairy

Fish

Fruit

Livestock

Nurseries

Poultry

Ranches

Timber

See special budgeting policy for self-employment loss from farming.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Specific Types of Self-Employment 3
heading: Farm Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Specific Types of Self-Employment

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(b)(1)(ii) and (2)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.10(c)(3)(ii) &

Count as earned income payments received from rental property when self-managed at least an average of 20 hours per week.

Count as unearned income, after allowable expenses , when the property is self-managed less than an average of 20 hours per week.

Income from a roomer is rental income.

Many separate households live together and share shelter expenses . This is not self-employment income.

Note: Refer to BP 072 to determine how to enter the income into ImpaCT.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Specific Types of Self-Employment 4
heading: Rental Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Specific Types of Self-Employment

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Self-Employment Loss
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Self-Employment Loss

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Self-Employment Loss

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(e)(1)(i)(A) & 7 CFR § 273.11(a)(2)(ii)(A)-(B)

When the expenses are greater than income, the result is a loss.

Deduct non-farm losses from other net self-employment income . Do not deduct from any other income.

Deduct farm losses from other self-employment income.

Deduct any remaining farm loss from the total amount of earned income (minus the earned income deduction ) and unearned income.

Exception: Do not budget as a farm loss if the annual gross farm income is less than $1000.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Self-Employment Loss 1

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(i) and (2)

Verify:

Income

Expenses

Differences or questionable information

Verify dependent care payments by:

completion of the W-1224, SNAP Dependent Care Agreement Form, by the parent and provider showing the actual payment, or

using CCMIS .

Suggested Verification Sources

Shared Households vs Roomers

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(6)

Enter case notes to support the actions taken on the case:

Confirming questionable or unclear information.

To show how income and expenses were determined.

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(e)(1)

Eligible addiction treatment centers are exempt from institution rules.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers 1
heading: Addiction Treatment Centers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers

body:
7 CFR § 271.2 & 7 CFR § 273.11(e)(2)(i)

Eligible addiction treatment centers must be:

approved by the USDA , FNS as a retailer; DAA facilities wishing to apply with USDA FNS to become authorized to accept and use a recipient’s benefits, may apply at: https://www.fns.usda.gov/snap/retailer-apply , or

a private nonprofit organization or institution, or a publicly operated community mental health center funded or eligible for funding under Part B of Title XIX of the Public Health Service Act, certified by the state of Connecticut.

Submit information to the SNAP Public Assistance Consultant assigned to your field office for a determination of eligibility if the center’s eligible status is unknown.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers 2
heading: Eligible Addiction Treatment Centers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(B) & (7 CFR § 273.7(b)(1)(vi) & 7 CFR § 273.11(e)(1) and (2)(ii)

Residents of eligible drug or alcohol addiction treatment centers qualify for SNAP as separate EDGs from others living in the center.

Exception: Children living with their parent who is a resident of the treatment center must be included as part of the resident parent’s SNAP EDG.

The resident and their EDG must meet all SNAP eligibility requirements.

Individuals regularly participating in a drug or alcohol treatment program are exempt from work requirements .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers 3
heading: Resident Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(n)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.11(e)(1)

A designated employee of the treatment center must apply as the authorized representative (A-Rep) for the resident while the resident is in the treatment center.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers 4
heading: Applying for SNAP

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(e)(2)(ii)

Residents of treatment centers are eligible for the same service and processing standards as other SNAP recipients.

Treatment center EDGs must meet the same eligibility requirements as other SNAP recipients with the exception of shelter expenses.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers 5
heading: Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(e)(2)(ii)

Allow any identified shelter expenses paid by the resident. If a single payment covers multiple expenses, deduct the following identified costs from the payment:

medical expenses (if applicable, allow toward the medical deduction)

amount returned to the resident for personal use, and

the maximum SNAP benefit for the number of individuals in the EDG.

Allow any remaining amount as the shelter expense.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers 6
heading: Shelter Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(e)(2)(iii) & 7 CFR § 273.12(a)

The center as A-Rep must report as required by the Simplified Reporting rules.

Exception: The center is required to report when the resident leaves the center. See Center Requirements When the Resident Leaves .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers 7
heading: Reporting Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(e)(3)(ii)

The treatment center must provide the DSS with a monthly or semi-monthly list of currently participating residents, including a statement signed by a responsible center official attesting to the validity of the list.

DSS ensures periodic, random on-site visits to the center are conducted to assure the list of eligible residents and records are consistent and up-to-date.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers 8
heading: Treatment Center Information Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(e)(7)

Centers are responsible for all:

losses or misuse of benefits, and

overpayments occurring while acting as the individual’s A-Rep.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers 9
heading: Center's Benefit Responsibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(e)(6)(i)-(iv)

When the resident leaves the treatment center it must :

notify the State agency of the EDG’s change in address and new address if known, and that they are no longer the EDG’s A-Rep;

provide the EDG its EBT card within 5 days of the EDG’s departure, if the center had the EBT card;

provide the EDG with a change report form, and advise the EDG to return the form to DSS within 10 days; and

return a prorated share of the EDG’s benefits based on the number of days the EDG resided at the center.

If the resident leaves before the center can take those actions, then the center must :

notify the State agency within 5 days so that the State agency can transfer the refund from the GLA or Center’s bank account to the EDG’s EBT account; and

return the EDG’s EBT card to DSS within 5 days of the EDG leaving.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers 10
heading: Center Requirements When the Resident Leaves

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Addiction Treatment Centers

body:
Source # 3

Effective Date: 5/15/19

heading: Last Update
