citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(f)(1) & 7 CFR § 273.11(c)(1)(i)-(ii)(A)-(D) and (m)-(n) & 7 CFR § 273.16(b)

These are individuals who are not allowed to be EDG members because of one of the following:

Drug felony conviction

Failure to comply with work requirements

Fleeing felons and probation/parole violators

Anyone convicted of a Federal aggravated sexual abuse, murder, sexual exploitation and abuse of children, sexual assault, or similar State laws, and who are also not in compliance with the terms of their sentence or parole, or are a fleeing felon.

Intentional program violation

Count the disqualified member’s non-excluded income and assets.

See Income and Assets .

Allow all income deductions and expenses of the disqualified member. See Expenses and Deductions .

Do not count the disqualified member in the EDG size when:

Determining the standard deduction

Comparing the EDG’s income with the income limits

Determining the benefit amount
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members 1
heading: Disqualified Members

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(n)(4)(i)(B)

Individuals disqualified for IPV are not allowed to act as the A-Rep for the EDG, unless the agency determines no one else is available to serve as the A-Rep.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members 2
heading: Authorized Representatives (AREPs)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(j)(2)(viii)(A) and (E) and (4)(iv)(A) and (C) & 7 CFR § 273.4(a)(3)-(7) & 7 CFR § 273.6(c) & 7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.11(c)(2)-(3)(i)-(ii)(A) & 7 CFR § 273.24(b)

Ineligible members are individuals who did not meet one of the following eligibility requirements:

Non-citizens

SSN

Time limits for Able-Bodied Adults Without Dependents (ABAWDs)

Count the ineligible member’s non-excluded assets. See Assets .

Prorate and budget the ineligible member’s non-excluded income, expenses and deductions. See Income and Expenses and Deductions .

Apply the gross income limit and excess shelter deduction maximum if the only elderly or disabled member is ineligible.

Do not count the ineligible member in the EDG size when:

Determining the standard deduction

Comparing the EDG’s income with the income limits

Determining the benefit amount
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members 3
heading: Ineligible Members

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(c)(2)-(3)(i)-(ii)(A)

1. Determine the ineligible member’s monthly countable earned income and unearned income separately. See Income , and Determining Income, Expenses and Deductions .

2. Total the number of eligible and ineligible EDG members.

3. Divide the earned/unearned amount in Step 1 by the number of individuals in Step 2.

4. Multiply the amount in Step 3 by the number of eligible EDG members. The result is the prorated income amount.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members 4
heading: Prorating Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members

body:
7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)(iii)(F) & 7 CFR § 273.11(c)(2)-(3)(i)-(ii)(A)

Prorate the following expenses paid by or billed to the ineligible member. Allow only the eligible EDG members’ prorated portion of:

Dependent care

Legally obligated child support

Medical expenses

Shelter

Exception : Allow the full SUA even if paid by or billed to the ineligible member.

Prorate as follows:

1. Determine the ineligible member’s monthly allowable deduction for each expense. See Expenses and Deductions , and Determining Income, Expenses and Deductions .

2. Total the number of eligible and ineligible EDG members.

3. Divide the allowable expense amount in Step 1 by the number of individuals in Step 2.

4. Multiply the amount in Step 3 by the number of eligible EDG members. The result is the prorated expense amount.

Examples: See Budgeting for Ineligible Members .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members 5
heading: Prorating Deductions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(3)(i)-(iii) and (5)-(7)(i) and (vi) and (e)(1)-(2) & 7 CFR § 273.5(a) and (d) & 7 CFR § 273.11(d)(1)-(3)(i)-(iii)

Non-members are individuals who live with and may share food with the EDG but are not included as part of the EDG:

Boarders

Live-in attendants

Residents of commercial boarding houses

Residents of institutions

Exception: Residents of institutions identified in this item

Roomers

Strikers

Note: An EDG with a striking member may or may not be eligible for benefits. If the EDG is eligible, the striker is a member. See Strikers .

Students

For a full description, see Non-Members .

Exclude the income and assets of non-members when determining eligibility and benefits for the EDG.

Exception : Cash payments from the non-member to the EDG are income to the EDG.

Allow only the amount the EDG paid or contributed when deductions are shared with a non-member. Prorate the deduction and allow the EDG’s share if the EDG’s amount cannot be determined.

Do not count the non-member in the EDG size when:

Determining the standard deduction

Comparing the EDG’s income with the income limits

Determining the benefit amount

Determining the excess shelter deduction
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members 6
heading: Non-members

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members

body:
Source #

Effective Date:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members 7
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Excluded Members

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(j)(1)-(9)

Do not increase SNAP benefits when the EDG’s TFA, SAGA, or SSUP benefits decrease or end due to a penalty for:

intentionally failing to comply with a program requirement, or

intentional program violation or fraud in that program.

See Budgeting .

Note: Do not end SNAP benefits when the EDG’s TFA, SAGA, or SSUP program ends due to the EDG’s failure to comply with a requirement of the program.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements 1
heading: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(j)(1)-(9)

Do not apply this policy when:

A child is not eligible because of a family cap

Information regarding the means-tested program penalty cannot be obtained. Record the actions taken to try to obtain the information.

The EDG/individual fails to complete a procedural requirement

Examples:

Completing a PRF

Providing required verification

The EDG/individual fails to complete an action they are unable to perform

The EDG/individual fails to comply with a requirement at application for the means-tested program

The EDG/individual fails to reapply or complete the application process at renewal

The EDG/individual reaches a time-limit

The EDG/individual was not receiving SNAP at the time of the means-tested program disqualification/penalty

The EDG/individual disqualification/penalty was applied at renewal of the means-tested program and there was a break in participation in the means-tested program.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements 2
heading: Exceptions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(j)(3)-(4)

Reduce the SNAP benefits by 20% when a member is sanctioned for a means-tested program non-compliance.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements 3
heading: Budgeting

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(j)(2) and (5)

Budget the penalty until the:

individual complies with the means-tested program requirements,

individual leaves the household,

individual is ineligible for the means-tested program for a reason not related to the penalty, or

the means-tested program ends.

Review the case annually for disqualifications lasting longer than 12 months to determine if the disqualification still applies.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements 4
heading: Penalty Time Period

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(j)(8)

Continue to act on changes for SNAP not related to the means-tested program penalty.

Example: The EDG reports and verifies they now pay dependent care for their one-year-old child. Add the dependent care and budget as a deduction. Continue to apply the 20% SNAP reduction.

Example: The EDG’s earned income decreases and the TFA benefit increases because of the decrease in earned income. Budget the:

new amount of earned income,

new amount of TFA benefits, and

the 20% SNAP reduction.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements 5
heading: Acting on Changes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Comply with Means-Tested Program Requirements

body:

heading: Last Update

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felons and Probation/Parole Violators
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felons and Probation/Parole Violators

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felons and Probation/Parole Violators

body:
7 CFR § 272.1(c) and (g) and (i) & 7 CFR § 273.2(f) & 7 CFR § 273.11(n)(1)(ii) and (2)-(5) & 7 CFR § 273.12

Fleeing felons are individuals trying to escape or avoid custody for a crime, or attempted crime, classified as a felony (or high misdemeanor in New Jersey) under a Federal or State law.

Probation or parole violators are individuals who have violated a condition of their probation or parole ordered under a Federal or State law.

Only individuals verified through law enforcement as fleeing felons or probation or parole violators are disqualified members for SNAP. See Disqualified Members .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felons and Probation/Parole Violators 1
heading: Fleeing Felons and Probation/Parole Violators

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felons and Probation/Parole Violators

body:
Source #

Effective Date:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felons and Probation/Parole Violators 2
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felons and Probation/Parole Violators

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(n)(1)(ii) and (2)-(5)

An individual is a fleeing felon or a probation or parole violator if they meet the following criteria.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status 1
heading: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(n)(1)(i)

An individual is a fleeing felon if a law enforcement officer presents a felony arrest warrant to obtain the location and other information about the individual. The felony warrant must contain one of the following National Crime Information Center Uniform Offense Classification Codes:

Escape (4901);

Flight to Avoid (prosecution, confinement, etc.) (4902); or

Flight-Escape (4999).
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status 2
heading: Fleeing Felon

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(n)(2)

An individual is a probation violator if:

a Court of law rules that the individual violated a condition of their probation ordered under a Federal or State law, and

local law enforcement is actively seeking the individual to enforce the conditions of the probation.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status 3
heading: Probation Violator

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(n)(2)

An individual is a parole violator if:

the Connecticut Department of Corrections, Parole Board (for state offenses) or a Court of law (for Federal offences) rules that the individual violated a condition of their parole ordered under a Federal or State law, and

local law enforcement is actively seeking the individual to enforce the conditions of the parole.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status 4
heading: Parole Violator

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status

body:
2014 Farm Bill – Section 4008

Individuals who have been convicted of certain crimes and are not in compliance with the terms of their sentence are ineligible for SNAP benefits. These crimes are:

aggravated sexual abuse under section 2241 of title 18

murder under section 1111 of title 18

an offense under chapter 110 of title 18

a Federal or State offense involving sexual assault, as defined in section 40002(a) of the Violence Against Women Act of 1994

an offense under State law determined by the Attorney General to be substantially similar to title 18 crimes listed above.

Individuals who are not in compliance with their sentence are considered disqualified members .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status 5
heading: Noncompliant Convicted Felons

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(n)(3)(i)-(iii)

Actively seeking means a Federal, State, or local law enforcement agency:

states an intent to enforce an outstanding felony warrant, or arrest an individual for a probation or parole violation within 20 days of the date the law enforcement agency requested information about the individual;

presents a felony arrest warrant ; or

states an intent to enforce an outstanding felony warrant, or arrest an individual for probation or parole violation within 30 days of the date of a status information request about a specific warrant/violation from DSS.

An individual is not a fleeing felon or a probation or parole violator if the law enforcement agency does not intend to enforce the felony warrant or to arrest the individual for a probation or parole violation within 30 days of the status information request from DSS.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status 6
heading: Actively Seeking

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Fleeing Felon/Probation or Parole Violator Status

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(n)(4)

Verify the individual’s status as a fleeing felon or a probation or parole violator before disqualifying them for SNAP.

Law enforcement requests for information do not verify the status of the felony warrant or probation or parole violation status.

Verify the status of a felony warrant or probation or parole violation only if a law enforcement agency presents a felony arrest warrant , or states intent to arrest an individual for probation or parole violation.

Request the following status information from the law enforcement agency:

the conditions of a felony warrant or a probation or parole violation, and

whether the law enforcement agency intends to actively seek the individual.

Allow the law enforcement agency 20 days to respond to a status information request.

If the law enforcement agency intends to enforce the felony warrant, or arrest the individual for a probation or parole violation within 30 days of the date of the status request,

delay any actions on the case until the 30-day period ends, and

verify with the law enforcement agency if an attempt was made to execute the felony warrant or arrest the probation or parole violator.

Deny or end the individual’s eligibility if action was taken within the 30 days.

The individual is not a fleeing felon or probation or parole violator if no action was taken within the 30-day period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification 1
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Processing
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Processing

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(n)(5)

Continue to process the application, including expedited services, while waiting for verification of the status of the fleeing felon or probation or parole violator. Process the application without considering the individual’s fleeing felon or probation or parole violation status if needed to meet the required SNAP processing timeframes.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Processing 1
heading: Application Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Application Processing

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(2)(i)

Follow change processing policy to act on decreases in EDG size when a member has been verified as a fleeing felon or a probation or parole violator. See Change Processing, Action on Interim Changes .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting 1
heading: Change Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

body:
7 CFR § 271.2 & 7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(C) & 7 CFR § 273.11(f)

Eligible Group living for the disabled facilities are exempt from institution rules. Eligible group living for the disabled is a:

public or private nonprofit residential setting,

serving no more than 16 residents, and

certified by the FNS as a retailer, or

approved by the appropriate agency or agencies of the State under regulations or comparable standards issued under Section 1616(e) of the Social Security Act.

Submit information to the SNAP Public Assistance Consultant assigned to your field office for a determination of eligibility if the facility’s eligible status is unknown.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled 1
heading: Group Living for the Disabled

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(f)(1)

Residents of any age must be disabled .

The resident or the EDG must meet all SNAP eligibility requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled 2
heading: Resident Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(C) & 7 CFR § 273.2(n) & 7 CFR § 273.11(f)(1)(i)-(ii) and (6)

Residents may apply as a:

group of one, or

any group of residents applying as an EDG.

Residents may:

apply themselves

Note: The facility must decide if the resident can apply for SNAP on their own behalf based on the resident's physical and mental ability to handle their affairs.

choose their own A-Rep , or

use the designated employee of the facility as their A-Rep.

Residents applying themselves do not lose their eligibility if the facility:

loses its approval from the State appropriate agencies, or

its status as an A-Rep is suspended due to FNS disqualifying the facility as a retailer.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled 3
heading: Applying for SNAP

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(f)(1)(i)

Residents of group living for the disabled facilities are eligible for the same service and processing standards as other SNAP recipients.

Facility EDGs must meet the same eligibility requirements as other SNAP recipients, with the following exception :
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled 4
heading: Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(f)(i)

Allow any identified shelter expenses paid by the resident. If a single payment covers multiple expenses, deduct the following identified costs from the payment:

medical expenses (allow toward the medical deduction)

amount returned to the resident for personal use, and

the SNAP maximum benefit for the number of individuals in the EDG.

Allow any remaining amount as the shelter expense.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled 5
heading: Shelter Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(f)(7)

Residents approved as a one-member EDG may:

give their benefits to the facility to purchase food for meals served to the group or individual,

purchase and prepare their own food, or

purchase meals prepared and served by the facility.

The facility will ensure each resident's SNAP benefits are used for meals intended for that resident when the resident chooses personal meals instead of group meals.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled 6
heading: Use of Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(f)(4)

Residents applying themselves are responsible to report their own changes.

The facility must report as required by simplified reporting if an employee of the facility acts as the A-Rep .

Exception: The facility is required to report when the resident leaves the center. See Facility Requirements When the Resident Leaves .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled 7
heading: Reporting Changes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(f)(2)

The facility must provide the DSS with a periodic list of currently participating residents, including a statement signed by a responsible center official attesting to the validity of the list.

DSS ensures periodic, random on-site visits to the center are conducted to assure the list of eligible residents and records are consistent and up-to-date.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled 8
heading: Facility Information Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(e)(7)-(8) and (f)(6)

Residents applying on their own behalf are responsible for any overissuances.

Facilities are responsible for all:

losses or misuse of benefits, and

overpayments occurring while acting as the individual’s A-Rep.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled 9
heading: Benefit Responsibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(e)(6)(i)-(iv) and (f)(5)

When the resident leaves the treatment center it must :

notify the State agency of the EDG’s change in address and new address if known, and that they are no longer the EDG’s A-Rep;

provide the EDG its EBT card within 5 days of the EDG’s departure, if the center had the EBT card;

provide the EDG with a change report form, and advise the EDG to return the form to DSS within 10 days; and

return a prorated share of the EDG’s benefits based on the number of days the EDG resided at the center.

If the resident leaves before the center can take those actions, then the center must :

notify the State agency within 5 days so that the State agency can transfer the refund from the GLA or Center’s bank account to the EDG’s EBT account; and

return the EDG’s EBT card to DSS within 5 days of the EDG leaving.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled 10
heading: Facility Requirements When the Resident Leaves

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

body:
Source #

Effective Date:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled 11
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Group Living for the Disabled

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers

body:
7 CFR § 271.2 & 7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(E) & 7 CFR § 273.11(h)

Homeless shelters are exempt from institution rules. Homeless shelters are public or private nonprofit facilities that provide temporary housing for homeless individuals.

Homeless individuals are individuals who lack a fixed and regular nighttime residence, including, but not limited to, individuals who will imminently lose their nighttime residence, or whose primary nighttime residence is:

A supervised shelter designed to provide temporary accommodations (such as a welfare hotel or congregate shelter);

A halfway house or similar institution providing temporary residences for individuals intended to be institutionalized;

Temporary accommodations in the residence of another individual, for not more than 90 days; or

A place not designed for or ordinarily used as a regular sleeping accommodation for human beings.

Examples:

Bus Stations

Hallways

Lobbies

Homeless meal providers are:

Public or private nonprofit establishments, authorized by FNS and approved by an appropriate State or local agency that feed homeless individuals.

Examples:

Halfway houses

Soup kitchens

Temporary shelters

Restaurants contracting with an appropriate State agency to offer meals at low or reduced prices to homeless individuals.

Submit information to the SNAP Public Assistance Consultant assigned to your field office for a determination of eligibility if the meal provider’s eligible status is unknown.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers 1
heading: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(E) & 7 CFR § 273.2(n)(4)(D)

Homeless EDGs may apply for benefits separately from others living in the homeless shelter. See Household Composition and the EDG/CG, Homeless Shelters .

Homeless EDGs may pick an A-Rep to act on behalf of the EDG to access benefits for the EDG’s use.

Exception: Homeless meal providers cannot act as the homeless EDG's A-Rep.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers 2
heading: Applying for SNAP

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(e) & 7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(vi)

Homeless EDGs are eligible for the same service and processing standards as other SNAP recipients.

Homeless EDGs are not required to have a permanent dwelling or fixed residence. Ask the EDG to provide a mailing address where notices can be mailed. See Residency .

Homeless EDGs must meet the same eligibility requirements as other SNAP recipients.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers 3
heading: Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers

body:
7 CFR § 273.24(c)(2)-(6)

Exempt chronically homeless ABAWDs from time limits if they are determined unfit for work .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers 4
heading: Work Requirements for Homeless ABAWDs

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(h)

Homeless EDGs may use SNAP benefits to purchase and prepare their own food, or prepared meals from authorized:

FNS approved retail stores, and

homeless meal providers .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers 5
heading: Use of Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers

body:
Source # 16

Effective Date: 04/01/2025
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers 6
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
General Definition

Migrant farmworkers temporarily leave a principal place of residence in another state to come to Connecticut to accept seasonal employment in an agricultural or fishing activity

Seasonal farmworkers accept seasonal employment in an agricultural or fishing activity.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 1
heading: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(a)(1) and (c)(1) and (n)(4)(iii)

Migrant or seasonal farmworkers may apply for SNAP for their EDG or choose an A-Rep to act on behalf of the EDG to access benefits for the EDG’s use.

If the migrant or seasonal farmworker’s employer acts as the A-Rep, ensure the:

EDG freely requested the employer to act as their A-Rep,

employer correctly represents the EDG’s circumstances,

EDG receives the correct amount of benefits, and

A-Rep properly uses the benefits for the EDG.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 2
heading: Applying

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(a)(2)

Migrant or seasonal farmworker EDGs are eligible for the same service and processing standards, and must meet the same eligibility requirements as other EDGs, with the following exceptions :
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 3
heading: Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(vi)

Residency verification may be waived if verification cannot be done in time to meet expedited processing timeframes.

Attempt to verify with the state the migrant EDG moved from the:

case closure date, and

date benefits were last issued.

See Residency .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 4
heading: Residency

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(1)(ii)

Migrant or seasonal farmworker EDGs may qualify for expedited benefits. See Expedited Service . Additionally, migrant or seasonal farmworker EDGs qualify for expedited service if the EDG meets destitute requirements .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 5
heading: Expedited Service

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(1)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.10(e)(3)(i)-(vii)

Only migrant or seasonal farmworker EDGs can be determined as destitute. Destitute means the EDG’s liquid assets do not exceed $100 and their only income for the month of application:

was received from a stopped source before the date of application,

Income received on a monthly or more frequent basis is stopped from that source if the income will not be received again from the source during the remainder of the month.

Income received less often than monthly is stopped from that source if the income is not received in the month the next payment would normally be received.

is from a new source but more than $25 will not be received by the 10th calendar day after the date of application,

Income received on a monthly or more frequent basis is from a new source when income has not been received from that source 30 days before the application file date .

Income received less often than monthly is from a new source if the income was not received from that source during the last normal interval between payments.

Migrant or seasonal farmworker EDGs can be destitute even if they receive both stopped income prior to the date of application, and income from a new source after the date of application, provided the EDG:

will not receive any other income in the month of application, and

any income of more than $25 from a new source will not be received by the 10th day after the date of application.

EDG members changing jobs but continuing to work for the same employer/grower are receiving continuing income, not income from a new source. The employer/grower is the source, not the crew chief. Consider migrant or seasonal farmworker EDGs traveling with a crew chief from one job source to another source as moving from a stopped source to a new source.

Verify destitute status but only if expedited processing timeframes will not be delayed.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 6
heading: Destitute Defined

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f) & 7 CFR § 273.9(c)(1)(i)(D) and (ii)(C) and (5)(i)(A) and (7) & 7 CFR § 273.10(a)(1)(ii) and (2) and (3)(ii) and (e)(3)(i)-(vii)

Determine a destitute migrant or seasonal farmworker EDG’s eligibility and benefits for the month of application using all sources of income received between the first of the month and the application date. Disregard income from a new source that will be received after the date of application.

Exclude reimbursements for travel expenses incurred by migrant or seasonal farmworkers. Count travel expense advances included in a written work agreement or statement that will be subtracted later from the EDG’s income.

Budget the EDG’s income for the period the income is intended to cover.

Note: Exclude a child’s earnings . If the child’s earnings cannot be identified separately from the rest of the EDG’s earnings,

divide the total earnings by the number of members that work, and

exclude the child’s share of the income.

Do not annualize contract or self-employment income over a 12-month period even when the migrant or farmworker’s annual income is earned over a shorter period of time.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 7
heading: Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(b)(vii)

Exempt migrant or seasonal farmworkers:

working 30-hours per week or more, or

under contract, or a similar agreement, with an employer or crew chief to begin work within 30 days.

See Work Registration Exemptions .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 8
heading: Work Registration

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(i)(viii)

Migrant or seasonal farmworkers leaving a job to move to another job meet good cause for leaving or reducing employment, even if work at the new job site has not begun.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 9
heading: Good Cause for Leaving Employment

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(2) and (3)(i) and (4)(ii)

Do not target migrant or seasonal farmworkers for more intensive verification than other SNAP EDGs. See Verification and Case Notes .

A collateral contact may be made with an individual outside the EDG, such as but not limited to:

Crew chiefs

Employers/growers

Migrant services agencies

See Suggested Verification Sources .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 10
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(a)(1)(ii)

Proration of benefits is required for the initial month of approval. The initial month for migrant or seasonal farmworker EDGs is the first month the EDG is approved following a period of more than one month since the last approval. The last approval may have been in Connecticut or another state.

Do not prorate benefits if the EDG received benefits the prior month.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 11
heading: Proration of Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(a)(2) and (e)(3)(ii)-(iii) and (vii)

Disregard income from a new source in the first month of the new benefit period if income of more than $25 will not be received from this new source by the 10th calendar day after the date of the EDG’s normal issuance cycle.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 12
heading: Renewal

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
7 CFR § 273.15(h)(i)(2)

Fair hearing requests from migrant or seasonal farmworker EDGs must be processed faster than normal hearing decisions when the EDG plans to move from the state. Process the hearing request to ensure the EDG receives a decision, and has benefits restored if applicable, before the EDG leaves the state.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 13
heading: Fair Hearings

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

body:
Source #

Effective Date:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers 14
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Migrant or Seasonal Farmworkers

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals

body:
7 CFR § 271.2 & 7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(D) & 7 CFR § 273.11(g)(1)

Shelters for battered individuals are exempt from institution rules.

Shelters for battered individuals are public or private nonprofit residential facilities providing temporary housing for battered individuals. A portion of the shelter must be set aside on a long-term basis to serve only battered individuals if the shelter serves other individuals.

Determine if the shelter meets the definition above and is known to the agency. Shelters authorized by FNS to redeem benefits as a wholesaler are also an eligible facility. No further determination is needed.

Note: Enter case notes regarding the determination of the shelter’s eligibility.

Submit pertinent information to the SNAP Public Assistance Consultant assigned to your field office for a determination of eligibility if the shelter’s eligible status is unknown.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals 1
heading: Shelters for Battered Individuals

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(c)(1) & 7 CFR § 273.11(g)(2) and (3)

Shelter residents and their children may apply for benefits as a separate EDG from others living in the shelter for battered individuals.

EDGs in shelters for battered individuals may pick an A-Rep to act on behalf of the EDG to access benefits for the EDG’s use.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals 2
heading: Applying for SNAP

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i)(3)(i) and (v) & 7 CFR § 273.11(g)(5)

Ensure the abuser’s EDG eligibility and benefit reflect the change in EDG composition due to the shelter EDG’s departure. Provide the appropriate notice of the change. See Change Processing .

Shelter resident EDGs are eligible for the same service and processing standards, and must meet the same eligibility requirements as other EDGs, with the following exceptions:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals 3
heading: Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(g)(3)

Allow only the deductions of the battered individual and their children.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals 4
heading: Deductions

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(g)(2)

Duplicate benefits may be issued the first month the EDG moved into the shelter if the EDG was part of a SNAP EDG that included the abuser.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals 5
heading: Duplicate Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals

body:
7 CFR § 273.11(g)(3)

Count only the income of the battered individual and their children.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals 6
heading: Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals

body:
7 CFR § 273.8(d) & 7 CFR § 273.11(g)(3)

Count only the assets of the battered individual and their children.

Exclude jointly-owned assets as inaccessible. See Excluded Jointly Owned Assets .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals 7
heading: Assets

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals

body:
7 CFR § 271.2 & 7 CFR § 273.1(b)(7)(vi)(D)

Residents of shelters for battered individuals may use their SNAP benefits to:

purchase and prepare their own food, or

purchase meals prepared and served at the shelter.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals 8
heading: Use of Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals

body:
Source #

Effective Date:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals 9
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Shelters for Battered Individuals

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(e)

Strikers are individuals who join other employees in stopping or interrupting work to protest work conditions, or to force the employer to accept the employees’ demands. Individuals are strikers until they:

return to work,

are fired, or

quit their job.

Individuals who are not strikers include but are not limited to:

employees whose workplace is closed because of a lockout ;

employees not part of the bargaining unit on strike who do not want to cross the picket line due to fear of injury or death; and

individuals unable to work due to striking employees.

Examples:

Employees laid off or furloughed due to the strike.

Truck drivers not working because products are not being produced.

individuals exempt from work registration the day before the strike began.

Exception: Does not include the exemption for being employed 30 hours or more.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers 1
heading: Strikers

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(e) & 7 CFR § 273.2(a)(1) and (c)(1) and (n)(1)(i)

The striker EDG may apply for SNAP or choose an A-Rep .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers 2
heading: Applying for SNAP

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(e) & 7 CFR § 273.2(j)(4)(v)(C)

EDGs with striking members are not eligible for SNAP unless the EDG:

was eligible for benefits the day before the strike began, and

are eligible for benefits when they apply.

Striker EDGs receive the same service and processing standards, and must meet the same eligibility requirements as other EDGs, with the following exceptions:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers 3
heading: Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(e)(1)-(2) & 7 CFR § 273.9(2)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.10(e)(1)(i)

1. Determine the gross income by budgeting the:

current income of the non-striking members, and

the higher of the striker’s income from the:

monthly income before the strike began ( pre-strike income ), or

current income .

2. Determine the EDG’s net income by allowing:

deductions for the current month, and

the earned income deduction for all EDG members, including the striker.

Continue to follow Steps 1 and 2 above as long as the individual is a striker .

Count strike benefits, including picket pay, as unearned income .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers 4
heading: Income

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(j)(3)(ii)

Federal, State, or local government employees who go on strike have voluntarily quit their job without good cause.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers 5
heading: Voluntary Quit

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers

body:
Source #

Effective Date:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers 6
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Strikers

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Simplified Reporting
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Simplified Reporting

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Simplified Reporting

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(i)

All SNAP EDGs follow simplified reporting policy.

Simplified Reporting (SR) requires the EDG to report fewer changes during a benefit period than other programs. Simplified reporting has 2 parts:

Mandatory reporting requirements , and

Periodic report form (PRF) .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Simplified Reporting 1
heading: Simplified Reporting

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: SR Requirement Notification
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: SR Requirement Notification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: SR Requirement Notification

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(ii)(A)-(D)

Provide the following reporting requirement information to the EDG, both verbally and in writing at application and renewal .

How SR works

Reporting requirements, including:

What to report .

What to verify.

When the W-1054, Periodic Report Form (PRF), is due .

How to get assistance filing the PRF.

What happens when the PRF isn’t completed and returned .

How to receive help to complete and file the PRF.

The toll free phone number (1-855) 626-6632, or 211 to ask questions or get help completing the PRF.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: SR Requirement Notification 1
heading: SR Requirement Notification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: SR Requirement Notification

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mandatory Reporting Requirements
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mandatory Reporting Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mandatory Reporting Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(2) and (5)(iii)(G)

Traditional SNAP EDGs must report the following changes:

When the EDG’s gross income exceeds the gross income limit (130% of the FPL ) based on the number of eligible EDG members as of the most recent:

approval, or

renewal

When an ABAWD’s work hours are less than 80 hours per month (20 hours per week averaged monthly).

When a household receives lottery or gambling winnings equal to or greater than $4,500.

ESAP -eligible SNAP households have separate mandatory reporting requirements .

All EDGs must report these changes by the 10th day of the month following the month the change occurred.

Changes may be reported:

by mail,

by phone,

in-person, or

online through MyAccount .

Reporting other changes may be done at any time, but is not required. See Change Processing .

EDGs reporting changes through their TFA or SAGA programs have met their SNAP reporting requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mandatory Reporting Requirements 1
heading: Mandatory Reporting Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mandatory Reporting Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(v)

EDGs that report when their gross EDG income exceeds 130% FPL but remain eligible for SNAP have met their 130% gross income reporting requirement. The EDG does not have to report their income changes again until the next PRF or renewal , whichever occurs first.

Note: EDGs with income over 130% FPL at application who are eligible do not have an income reporting requirement until their next PRF or renewal, whichever occurs first .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mandatory Reporting Requirements 2
heading: Gross Income Reporting Requirements after Exceeding 130% FPL

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mandatory Reporting Requirements

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(d)

File a claim against the household following Overpayments and Claims and Intentional Program Violation policies when the household:

fails to report a required change, and

received benefits they were not eligible to receive.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mandatory Reporting Requirements 3
heading: Failure to Report

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mandatory Reporting Requirements

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(i) and (iii)(A)-(G)

All Applicable EDGs must complete a PRF once in the middle of their benefit period. EDGs with 12-month benefit periods must provide a complete report in the 6th month.

ImpaCT automatically generates the PRF. The complete report must be returned by the due date.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 1
heading: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(1)(i)-(iii) and (v)

The EDG must report the following changes on the PRF:

Changes in earned income of more than $125 from the amount last used to determine the EDG’s benefits.

Changes in unearned income of more than $125, except changes in TFA or SAGA benefits.

Changes in the source of income, including starting or stopping a job, or changing jobs if the job change includes a change of income.

Changes in household composition, such as the addition or loss of a member.

When an ABAWD’s work hours are less than 80 hours per month (20 hours per week averaged monthly).

Changes in residence (where the household physically lives), and the new residence’s shelter expenses.

Changes in the amount of legally obligated child support.

When a household receives lottery or gambling winnings equal to or greater than $4,500.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 2
heading: PRF Requirements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(iii)(D)and(E) and CT Process

PRFs are complete when:

the EDG reports "I made no changes" or "I reported changes", and

if “I reported changes”, at least one change is reported on the form, and

All pages of the PRF are received, and

the form is signed and dated.

Note: regulations do not specify how the household must provide the date or that it must be a household provided date.

Register complete PRFs and process reported changes to take effect the month after the report month .

Advise the EDG of the action taken, including when:

the benefit decreases ,

the benefit increases ,

the benefit remains unchanged, or

the EDG is found ineligible .

Note: Provide adequate notice for decreases and case closures.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 3
heading: Complete PRF

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(iii)(C)-(D) and (c)(1)-(3)

Act on reported changes as follows:
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 4
heading: Changes Reported

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)(i)-(ii)

Act on the reported change if the EDG provides required verifications.

Send the EDG a W-3016, Notification from the Department of Social Services, requesting any required verifications not provided with the PRF. Allow the EDG 10 days to verify the reported change.

Do not act on the reported change if the EDG fails to provide required verifications by the W-3016 due date. Verify and act on the change at renewal .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 5
heading: Change Increases Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(2)(i)-(ii)

Act on the reported change. Verify the change at renewal if verification is not provided with the PRF.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 6
heading: Change Decreases Benefits/Closes Case

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(iii)(E)

EDGs failing to provide a PRF by the due date are automatically mailed a Notice of Missing Report. The notice advises the EDG:

they must complete and return the PRF and within the 10-day extended filing period after receipt of the notice, and

their case will close unless they provide a complete PRF within the 10-day extended filing period.

ImpaCT automatically closes the case if the EDG doesn’t provide a complete PRF.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 7
heading: No PRF Received

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 8
heading: Incomplete PRF

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(iii)(E) and CT Process

ImpaCT automatically sends the EDG a Notice of Missing Signature and a Missing Signature sheet if the PRF is identified as “not signed”. The notice advises the EDG:

the EDG must sign and return the Missing Signature sheet to complete the PRF process, and

their case will close unless they provide a complete PRF within the 10-day extended filing period after receipt of the notice.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 9
heading: Missing Signature

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(iii)(E)

Phone the EDG to obtain the answers to the unanswered questions. Enter case notes of the phone call and the EDG’s responses.

If the EDG could not be reached by phone, send the EDG a W-3016, Notification from the Department of Social Services, and the incomplete PRF with the unanswered questions highlighted. The notice advises the EDG:

what questions the EDG must answer for the report to be complete,

what required verifications the EDG must provide, and

their case will close unless they provide a complete PRF within the 10-day extended filing period after receipt of the notice.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 10
heading: Questions Not Answered

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(iii)(E)

Provide benefits no later than 10 days after their normal issuance if the EDG provides a complete PRF within the 10-day extended filing period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 11
heading: EDG Completes the Process within the 10-Day Extended Filing Period

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(iii)(F)

Reinstate the case if the EDG:

requests a fair hearing claiming they filed a complete PRF but DSS doesn’t have the PRF, and

a complete PRF is filed before the end of the issuance month.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 12
heading: EDG Files a Hearing Due to a Missing PRF

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

body:
Waiver of 7 CFR § 273.2(c) and (e)(1) & 7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(iii)(F)

Reinstate benefits when the EDG provides a complete PRF within 30 days after case closure. Reinstate benefits, effective the date the completed PRF is received, for the remaining months of the benefit period. Do not extend the benefit period.

A new application and interview are not required.

Prorate benefits from the date the PRF is returned complete.

An adequate notice W-0001N, Notice of Action, is sent to advise the EDG of the action taken on their reinstatement, including when:

the benefit decreases

the benefit increases ,

the benefit remains unchanged, or

when the EDG is found ineligible.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF) 13
heading: EDG Completes the Process within 30 Days of Closure

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Periodic Report Form (PRF)

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification and Case Notes
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification and Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification and Case Notes

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)(i)-(ii)

Verification of changes reported on the PRF is only required if the reported change will increase benefits . See Verification Requirements by Element and Activity for required verifications.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification and Case Notes 1
heading: Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification and Case Notes

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(6) & 7 CFR § 273.12(c)

Enter case notes on the actions taken based on the PRF information, including any phone conversations made to complete the PRF .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification and Case Notes 2
heading: Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Verification and Case Notes

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change Processing
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change Processing

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)

All EDGs are included in and follow simplified reporting policy. Changes in an EDG’s situation become known to the agency when:

reported by the household, or

information is received from other sources within the agency or outside the agency.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change Processing 1
heading: Change Processing
