citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Action on Interim Changes
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Action on Interim Changes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Action on Interim Changes

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(vi) & (c)

Interim changes are changes that become known outside of application , Periodic Report Form (PRF) , or renewal .

The date a change becomes known is the date:

The EDG reports the change by:

in-person,

mail,

online through ConneCT , or

phone;

The information regarding an EDG change is received from other sources .

Review the change within 10 days of the date the change becomes known to determine the appropriate actions to take for:

Benefit increases

Benefit decreases/Case closures

Unclear information
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Action on Interim Changes 1
heading: Action on Interim Changes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Action on Interim Changes

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Increase
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Increase

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Increase

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)(i)-(iii)

EDGs must provide required verifications when the change increases benefits.

Increase the benefit amount the month after the month the change becomes known if required verification was timely provided. See EDG Fails to Provide Verification and EDG Verifies After the 10-day Verification Request Period when verification is not provided timely.

When the change can’t be made by the month after the month the change became known, restore the underpayment .

Example: The EDG reports on August 28 their income decreased by $60 and required verification was provided. The worker timely acted on the change on September 2. The benefits are increased for October and a restoration of the underpayment is issued for the month of September.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Increase 1
heading: Benefit Increase

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Increase

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)(iii)

Determine benefits without making the change when the EDG fails to provide required verifications by the due date. Obtain the required verification at renewal.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Increase 2
heading: EDG Fails to Provide Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Increase

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)(iii)

Increase benefits the month after the month the change was verified for EDGs failing to provide verification within 10 days.

Example: The EDG reports on August 10 their income decreased by $60 but verification was not provided. The worker requested verification with a due date of August 21. The EDG verifies the income decrease on September 15. Increase the EDG’s benefit effective for October. Do not issue an underpayment for the month of September.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Increase 3
heading: EDG Verifies After the 10-day Verification Request Period

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Increase

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Decrease/Case Closure
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Decrease/Case Closure

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Decrease/Case Closure

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(2)(i)

Take action to decrease the benefit within 10 days after the change becomes known if:

the EDG requested their case be closed,

TFA or SAGA benefits changed, or

the information is verified upon receipt .

Act on the change at renewal for all other changes that decrease benefits.

Provide EDGs with the appropriate notice when benefits are decreased or the case is closed.

Exception: See Notice Not Required .

Benefit decreases or case closures are effective no later than the month following the month the adverse action period ends.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Decrease/Case Closure 1
heading: Benefit Decrease/Case Closure

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Decrease/Case Closure

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change of Address
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change of Address

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change of Address

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(4)(i)

EDGs reporting a change of address (COA) must also report the shelter expenses for the new address. Verification of shelter expenses is not required unless questionable. Verification of residency is required if there is an indication the EDG moved out-of-state.

Change the EDG’s address. Determine the new benefit amount based on the shelter expenses for the new address. Only allow expenses for which information was provided.

Note : Provide voter registration services to all applicants and recipients of SNAP. A COA affects voter registration. See Voter Registration Services .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change of Address 1
heading: Change of Address

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change of Address

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(4)(i)

Send the EDG a W-3016, Notification from the Department of Social Services, to request the shelter expense information when the new shelter expense information is not provided with the COA, or is questionable. Allow the EDG 10 days to respond.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change of Address 2
heading: Shelter Expense Information Needed

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change of Address

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(4)(i)

Determine the EDG’s benefits without allowing a shelter deduction when the EDG fails to provide their new shelter expense information , or verification of questionable expenses , by the W-3016 due date.

Send the EDG the appropriate notice advising their benefits were determined without the deduction.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change of Address 3
heading: EDG Fails to Provide Required Shelter Expense Information

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Change of Address

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Returned Mail
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Returned Mail

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Returned Mail

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Returned Mail 1
heading: Returned Mail

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Returned Mail

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(4)(i)

Mail returned with a forwarding address is a reported COA . Process the change following Change of Address policy. Begin closure if the EDG moved out-of-state.

Notify the EDG of the actions taken using the appropriate notice .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Returned Mail 2
heading: Forwarding Address Provided

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Returned Mail

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(3)(i)-(iii) & 7 CFR § 273.13(c)

Enter in case notes that the mail was returned as undeliverable with no forwarding address. Act on the COA at the next periodic report or renewal , whichever occurs first.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Returned Mail 3
heading: Forwarding Address Not Provided

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Returned Mail

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)

Act on medical expense changes reported by the EDG , or if the source is verified upon receipt .

Do not act on changes in medical expenses when:

the change is reported by a source other than the EDG, and

the change action cannot be completed without contacting the EDG for verification.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses 1
heading: Medical Expenses

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Medical Expenses

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Data Matches
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Data Matches

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Data Matches

body:
7 CFR § 272.1(c)(1)-(2) & 7 CFR § 272.8 & 7 CFR § 272.11 & 7 CFR 272.12 & 7 CFR 272.13 & 7 CFR 272.14 & 7 CFR 272.16 & 7 CFR § 273.2(f)(4) & 7 CFR § 273.12(e)(3)(ii)

Act on information from data matches within 10 days of receipt.

Exception: Complete all action for IEVS information no later than 45 days after receipt. Follow policy in benefit increase or benefit decrease/case closure when acting on changes reported through data matches.

The following data matches are verified upon receipt :

BENDEX

Care 4 Kids

Connecticut Department of Labor UCB information

CCSES

SAVE

SDX

SSN Enumeration/Validation
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Data Matches 1
heading: Data Matches

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Data Matches

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(3)(ii)(B)

Send a W-3016 for data matches not verified upon receipt. Allow the EDG 10 days to respond. Include in the notice:

what information the EDG must provide, and

what will happen if the EDG fails to provide the required information.

Remove the individual named in the data match from the case if the EDG fails to provide required information by the W-3016 due date. Issue a NOA providing advance notice .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Data Matches 2
heading: Unclear Data Match Information

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Data Matches

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Changes Reported Through Other Programs
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Changes Reported Through Other Programs

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Changes Reported Through Other Programs

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(f)(1)-(3)(i)-(iii)

Changes reported for other programs are reported for SNAP. Other programs include, but are not limited to:

TFA

SAGA

Act on any changes reported for other programs only if the other program’s grant changed.

Note : SNAP benefits cannot be ended solely due to the other program’s benefits ending. Determine SNAP continued eligibility based SNAP eligibility requirements.

Notify the EDG of any changes in work registration or ABAWD status resulting from the other program’s reported change.

Advise the EDG, using the appropriate notice , what actions were taken on their SNAP case because of changes reported for other programs.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Changes Reported Through Other Programs 1
heading: Changes Reported Through Other Programs

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Changes Reported Through Other Programs

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Unclear Information
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Unclear Information

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Unclear Information

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(3)

You may get information about an EDG’s circumstances which is not clear enough to determine the effect on the EDG’s eligibility or benefit amounts. The information may come from a third party or from the EDG.

If there is not enough information reported to request specific verification, send a W-3018 using the "We Need More Information" screen under the following circumstances. The information:

is new information that indicates significant differences with information used to approve the case.

Note: The reported information must be about circumstances that existed at the time of the interview. Or ,

is change information fewer than 60 days old from the current month, and

if accurate, would have required the EDG to report the information. See Mandatory Reporting Requirements .

Allow the EDG 12 days to complete actions to clarify, and verify (if applicable) the unclear information. By populating the "We Need More Information" screen, the W-3018 form will include :

the due date, and

what will happen if the EDG fails to respond.

Act on the information at PRF or renewal , whichever occurs first, if the information doesn’t meet the above circumstances.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Unclear Information 1
heading: Unclear Information

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Unclear Information

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)-(3)(i)(B)

Determine whether the change will increase or decrease/close the case. Act on the change following Benefit Increase or Benefit Decrease/Case Closure policy.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Unclear Information 2
heading: EDG Responds to the W-3018

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Unclear Information

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)(3)(i)(B)

The system will issue a NOA providing advance notice for case closure if the EDG fails to clarify, and verify (if applicable) the requested information by the W-3018 due date.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Unclear Information 3
heading: EDG Fails to Respond to the W-3018

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Unclear Information

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(e)(1)-(3)(ii)

Mass changes are changes to eligibility or benefits on all SNAP cases, or a large number of cases, based on State or Federal government regulations or laws. The change is effective at the same time for all cases.

These changes include, but are not limited to:

income eligibility standards

shelter deduction limit

standard deduction

monthly benefit amounts

utility standards

TFA and SAGA benefits

cost-of-living adjustments (COLAs) for Federal program payments, such as but not limited to:

RSDI

SSI
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes 1
heading: Mass Changes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(e)(1)(i)-(ii)

ImpaCT processes these mass changes. Generally, this update is effective in October. ImpaCT issues a NOA to EDGs whose benefits changed. Pending applications or cases in override are not updated. ImpaCT issues a report identifying cases not updated requiring worker action .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes 2
heading: Mass Changes for SNAP Benefits, Deductions, Eligibility Standards and State Utility Standards

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(e)(3)

ImpaCT processes the SNAP benefit mass change for any cases receiving Railroad Retirement, Social Security Benefits, or SSI. ImpaCT issues a NOA to EDGs whose benefits changed. Pending applications or cases in override are not updated. ImpaCT issues a report identifying cases not updated requiring worker action .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes 3
heading: Mass Railroad Retirement, Social Security, SSI Cost of Living Adjustment (COLA)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(e)(2)(i)-(ii)

ImpaCT processes a SNAP benefit mass change for any cases with a companion TFA or SAGA case, when a mass change in TFA or SAGA benefits occurs. ImpaCT issues a NOA to EDGs whose benefits changed. Pending applications or cases in override are not updated. ImpaCT issues a report identifying cases not updated requiring worker action .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes 4
heading: Mass TFA or SAGA Changes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(e)(1)-(4)

Workers receive a report for any cases not updated by ImpaCT. Individual mass change notices are required.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes 5
heading: Worker Required Actions for Mass Updates

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(e)(5)

EDGs have the right to request a fair hearing when they disagree with a change made by a mass change.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes 6
heading: Fair Hearings

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(e)(6)(i)-(iii)

EDGs requesting a fair hearing are eligible to be reinstated or receive continued benefits, at the amount received before the mass update, if the EDG:

did not waive its right to continued benefits,

makes a timely hearing request , and

claims benefits were:

computed incorrectly, or

the rules were misapplied/misinterpreted.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes 7
heading: Continued Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Mass Changes

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reinstatement
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reinstatement

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reinstatement

body:
Waiver of 7 CFR § 273.2(c) and (e)(1)

Reinstate the case if the EDG provides or verifies missing information:

within 30 days of closure when the case closed due to failure to provide or verify information, and

the EDG had months remaining in the benefit period before the case closed.

A new application and interview are not required.

Reinstate the case for the remaining months of the benefit period. Do not extend the benefit period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reinstatement 1
heading: Reinstatement

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reinstatement

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Report
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Report

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Report

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(d)

File a claim against the EDG following Overpayments and Claims and/or Intentional Program Violation (IPV) policies when the EDG:

fails to report a required change, and

received benefits they were not eligible to receive.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Report 1
heading: Failure to Report

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Failure to Report

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c) and (e)(4) & 7 CFR § 273.13(a) and (c)

Notify EDGs when benefits increase, decrease, or have no change.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification 1
heading: Notification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c) and (e)(4) & 7 CFR § 273.13(a) and (c)

The notice includes the following information:

The reason for the change (or that benefits did not change).

How the EDG’s benefit changed.

The change effective date.

Contact information for questions.

The EDG’s right to a fair hearing.

The EDG’s right to continued benefits (for decreases or closures).

The EDG is responsible for any overissuance if continued benefits are issued and the fair hearing is reversed.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification 2
heading: Benefit Change Notice

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c) and (e)(4)

Notify the EDG when a reported change:

increased benefits, or

the benefits remain unchanged.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification 3
heading: Benefit Increase / No Change in Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification

body:
7 CFR § 273.13(a) and (c)

Notify the EDGE when a reported change will decrease or close benefits. These notices are issued using:

adequate notice , or

advance notice .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification 4
heading: Benefit Decrease

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification

body:
7 CFR § 273.13(a)(3)(i)-(vi)

Adequate notice for a decrease or closure means the EDG receives the notice no later than the date the EDG would normally receive their next benefit.

Provide adequate notice for decreases or case closures when the change was reported:

in writing and signed by the EDG, or on a complete PRF , and

the EDG’s eligibility or benefit amount can be determined based on the information the EDG provided.

Note: All other decrease or closure actions must use advance notice .

EDGs receiving adequate notice have the right to:

a fair hearing , and

continued benefits when a fair hearing is timely requested.

Note: Return benefits to the previous amount within 10 days of the EDG’s request for a fair hearing, if the request was made by the date specified in the notice.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification 5
heading: Adequate Notice

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification

body:
7 CFR § 273.13(a)(1)

Advance notice means the EDG receives a notice of a decrease or closure action at least 10 days before the action. This is the adverse action period. The first day of the 10-day adverse action period begins the day after the notice is mailed.

Send an advance notice for all decreases not receiving adequate notice .

EDGs have the right to:

a fair hearing , and

continued benefits when a fair hearing is requested before the end of the adverse action period.

Note: Return benefits to the previous amount within 5 days of the EDG’s request for a fair hearing, if the request was made by the date specified in the notice.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification 6
heading: Advance Notice

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(e)(4)(i)-(iii)

Issue individual notices for mass changes when:

benefits decrease, or

the case closes.

EDGs have the right to:

a fair hearing , and

continued benefits when a fair hearing is timely requested.

Note: Provide continued benefits only if the EDG’s request is based on:

improper computation of eligibility or benefits, or

misapplication or misinterpretation of Federal law or regulation.

If continued benefits are allowed, return benefits to the previous amount within 5 days of the EDG’s request for a fair hearing, if the request was made by the date specified in the notice.

Provide at least adequate notice .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification 7
heading: Notice for Mass Changes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(e)(1)(ii) & 7 CFR § 273.13(b)(1)-(14)

Notice is not required for the following situations:

Case closure when all members of the EDG are deceased, based on reliable information.

EDG did not provide postponed verifications and was assigned a full benefit period for expedited service .

EDG voluntarily requests to end their case:

in writing, or

in-person to the worker.

Group living for disabled perso ns or addiction treatment center loses its:

certification from the state agency, or

FNS certification as a retailer.

Mass changes for the SNAP Benefits, Deductions, Eligibility Standards and State Utility Standards Update .

Overpayment recoupment is started and the EDG was previously notified.

Reduction because the EDG failed to make an agreed upon payment on a claim .

Reductions due to anticipated changes at benefit approval, and the EDG was notified at benefit approval.

Reduction or case closure when an EDG member is disqualified for IPV .

TFA approval when SNAP and TFA were applied for at the same time, and the EDG was previously notified of the potential decrease.

Underpaid benefits were fully restored and the EDG was notified in writing the increased benefits would end.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification 8
heading: Notice Not Required

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notification

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes

body: Verification Requirements by Element and Activity .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes 1
heading: Resources

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes

body:
7 CFR § 273.12(c)

Enter the following case notes:

The reported change.

The date change became known.

The actions taken on the case based on the change.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes 2
heading: Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Resources and Case Notes

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal

body:
7 CFR § 273.14(a)

Renewal is a review of the EDG’s eligibility factors to determine their continued eligibility for SNAP benefits.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal 1
heading: Renewal

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal

body:
Source # 15

Effective Date: 10/01/2024
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal 2
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e) & 7 CFR § 273.14(b)(1)(i) and (iii) and (2)-(4) & Waiver 7 CFR § 273.2(e)

A renewal is complete when:

a W-1ER , Renewal Application , is provided,

all required verifications are provided, and

an interview is completed by the EDG or A-Rep , if required.

Exception: Elderly or disabled EDGs with no earned income are not required to complete an interview at renewal.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process 1
heading: Renewal Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process

body:
7 CFR § 273.14(b)(1)(i) and (iii)

EDGs must receive notice that their current benefit period is ending and what they must do to continue to receive SNAP benefits. ImpaCT mails a renewal application with a Notice of Expiration (NOE).

NOEs advise the EDG:

Their benefit period is ending

The due date to submit a timely-filed renewal application for uninterrupted benefits

The renewal application can be submitted:

at any field office,

by mail,

online through MyAccount , or

through SSA for pure SSI EDGs.

Where to file the renewal application if not submitted online (scanning center mailing address)

Of their right to file an renewal application with only a:

signature, and

date;

Of their interview requirements

Of their responsibility to reschedule a missed interview

Of the results of failure to timely apply for renewal

Of their fair hearing rights
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process 2
heading: Notice of Expiration (NOE)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process

body:
7 CFR § 273.14(b)(2)

ImpaCT mails a renewal application with a NOE the month before the end of the EDG’s current benefit period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process 3
heading: Renewal Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(c)(1)(iv) & 7 CFR § 273.14(c)(1)-(4)

To file timely, EDGs must provide a signed and dated renewal application by the 15th day of the last month of their benefit period. The renewal application can be submitted:

by mail,

in-person,

online through MyAccount , or

through the local Social Security office.

The file date is the date the renewal application is:

received/date stamped in the:

DSS office, or

Social Security office; or

received online during:

normal business hours, or

the next business day when received after normal work hours.

Note: Regulations do not specify how the household must provide the date, or that it must be a household provided date.

EDGs lose their right to uninterrupted benefits when they fail to timely file.

The case automatically closes at the end of the benefit period if the renewal application is not provided by the last day of the benefit period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process 4
heading: Timely Renewal Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(1)-(3) & 7 CFR § 273.14(b)(3)(i)-(iii) and Waiver of 7 CFR § 273.2(e)(2) & 7 CFR 273.14(b)(3)

EDGs must complete an interview as part of the renewal process. EDGs who do not submit their renewal application and complete an interview are sent a notice and advised to contact the Benefit Center to complete their interview.

Exception: Unless requested, an interview is not required if the household qualifies for the ESAP waived interview criteria:

the EDG’s adult members are elderly or disabled, and have no earned income .

The SNAP renewal form is submitted timely and information is complete.

Mandatory verifications are complete and not questionable.

The household has been approved for continuing eligibility.

Note: When an interview is requested by an elderly/disabled EDG with no earned income , attempt 2 cold calls before scheduling an on-demand interview. If the scheduled interview is missed, a Notice of Missed Interview (NOMI) must be sent before denying the case for the reason – “Failed to keep interview appointment for renewal.”
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process 5
heading: Interview

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(1)

Complete an interview with one of the following individuals:

a responsible member of the household, or

the authorized representative (A-Rep) .

Note: Individuals may bring anyone they choose to the interview.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process 6
heading: Who to Interview

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(1) and (2)

After the renewal application is registered in ImpaCT, make 2 attempts to cold call the applicant to complete the interview.

Send a Renewal Telephone Interview notice after 2 attempts if the EDG cannot be reached. The notice advises the EDG to call the Benefit Center, using the toll free number, Monday through Friday, during Benefit Center hours, to complete an interview, or to schedule an in-person interview.

Exception : Complete a face-to-face interview for the following reasons:

EDG or A-Rep request

In-person renewal

Needed to resolve issues or questions

Note: EDGs who notified DSS they wish to complete in-person interviews at renewal are advised in their NOE to go to a DSS office to complete their interview within 12 days. ImpaCT is coded so the correct NOE is issued.

Face-to-face interviews may be completed at:

any field office,

a mutually acceptable location, or

the applicant’s residence.

Note: The interview must be scheduled in advance if conducted at the applicant’s residence.

Complete required interviews for EDGs with multiple programs at the same time whenever possible.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process 7
heading: How to Complete the Interview

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(1) and (3)

Treat the interview as a confidential discussion of the EDG’s situation.

Review ImpaCT for EDG information known to the agency.

Review all eligibility factors with the EDG or A-Rep.

Review the renewal application by:

going over each question, and

resolving any unclear or missing information with the individual being interviewed.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process 8
heading: Interview Process

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(1) & 7 CFR § 273.7(c) & 7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(ii)

Provide the following information and materials to the EDG or A-Rep during the renewal interview:

Eligibility Determination Document (EDD)

How SNAP benefits can be used

Informational materials

Processing timeframes

Referrals to other services as needed

Reporting requirements. See Mandatory Reporting Requirements .

Self-addressed return envelope

TFA time limits do not apply to SNAP benefits

The EDG’s rights and responsibilities

W-1348, Proofs We Need form, listing the required verifications needed to determine eligibility and the due date to return the verifications.

Note: Have the individual applying sign the Eligibility Determination Document when completing an in-person interview.

Verification forms

Work registration materials, if applicable

Advise the applicant required actions must be completed within 10 days of the interview.

Provide voter registration services to all applicants and recipients of SNAP. See Voter Registration Services .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process 9
heading: Information to Provide at the Interview

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)(i)-(iv) & 7 CFR § 273.12(c)(2) & 7 CFR § 273.14(b)(4)

EDGs must provide required verifications at renewal, including but not limited to:

ABAWD work hours

Data match information

Income changes if the source or amount changed by more than $50 or the information is incomplete, inaccurate, inconsistent, or outdated.

Interim changes

Medical expenses previously unreported

Newly obtained SSNs

Non-citizen sponsor deeming

Reported medical expenses that changed by more than $25

Other changes requiring verification

Provide the EDG with a W-1348, and allow the EDG 10 days to provide the required verifications.

EDGs can provide verifications:

in-person,

by mail, or

by uploading verifications online through MyAccount.

See Suggested Verification Sources

See Verification Requirements by Element and Activity
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process 10
heading: Verifications

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Renewal Process

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: SNAP Renewal through SSA
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: SNAP Renewal through SSA

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: SNAP Renewal through SSA

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(k)(2)(i)-(ii)(A)-(B) & 7 CFR § 273.14(c)(4)

EDGs with only SSI recipients applying for SNAP renewal at the SSA have submitted an application when the signed application is received by the SSA.

SSA completes the interview and forwards the following items to the appropriate field office:

Completed W-1ER, Renewal Application

Transmittal form

Available verifications

Second interviews are not required unless needed to resolve issues or questions. Follow Timely Processing requirements.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: SNAP Renewal through SSA 1
heading: SNAP Renewal through SSA

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: SNAP Renewal through SSA

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Timely Processing
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Timely Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Timely Processing

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(g)(2) & 7 CFR § 273.14(d)(1)-(2)

Provide uninterrupted benefits to eligible EDGs when they:

Timely file a renewal application,

complete the interview , if required, and

provide all required verifications before the end of the current benefit period.

Notify the EDG of their renewal determination using the appropriate notice.

Note: See Agency Caused Delays if timely processing is not met, or EDG Caused Delays if required actions are not completed by the end of the renewal period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Timely Processing 1
heading: Timely Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Timely Processing

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(h)(1) & 7 CFR § 273.14(e)(1)-(3)

Send the EDG a notice advising the EDG their renewal application is still being processed, when the renewal is received but not processed before the end of the current benefit period.

Benefits stop at the end of the benefit period if the EDG doesn’t complete all required actions.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing 1
heading: Delayed Processing

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing

body:
7 CFR § 273.14(e)(1) and (3)

Complete the following actions when the EDG timely submitted a renewal application, but was not processed within 30 days of the file date:

Authorize benefits the same day the EDG is determined eligible.

Issue a full month’s benefit.

Note: Issue underpayments when the EDG could not participate the first month of the new benefit period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing 2
heading: Agency Caused Delays

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(2)-(3) & 7 CFR § 273.14(b)(3)(iii)

A renewal Notice of Missed Interview (NOMI) is sent to EDGs failing to complete an interview by the on-demand interview due date .

The NOMI advises the EDG:

it has not completed their interview,

it must call to complete the interview or their renewal application will be denied,

the days, work hours and phone number to call the Benefit Center, and

the date by which the interview must be completed.

The case closes at the end of the benefit period if the EDG fails to complete a required interview.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing 3
heading: Failure to Complete an Interview

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing

body:
7 CFR § 273.14(b)(4)

Give the EDG form W-1348, Proofs We Need, if additional verification is required. Allow the EDG 10 days to provide the required verification.

The case closes at the end of the benefit period if the EDG fails to provide all required verifications by the end of the benefit period.

Exception: Hold the renewal to the end of the 10-day timeframe when the W-1348 due date goes beyond the end of the benefit period. Reinstate the case within 5 days of receipt of the required verifications when:

the benefit period ended, and

the EDG provides the required verifications by the W-1348 due date.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing 4
heading: Failure to Provide Verification

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing

body:
7 CFR § 273.14(e)(2)

Reopen the case and prorate benefits from the date the EDG completes the required actions if;

the EDG submitted their renewal application before the end of the benefit period, and

the renewal was denied because required actions were not completed by the end of the benefit period, and

the EDG completed the required actions within 30 days of the end of the benefit period.

Exception : Benefits are not prorated for migrant or seasonal farmworker EDGs who complete the renewal process within 30 days of the end of the benefit period. Issue a full month’s benefits.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing 5
heading: Actions Completed Within 30 Days of the End of the Benefit Period

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)(i) & 7 CFR § 273.10(a)(1)(ii) and (2) & 7 CFR § 273.14(e)(3) and (f)

Renewal applications submitted after the end of the benefit period but within 30 days of the end of the benefit period are late renewals.

Process as an initial application including screening for expedited service. A new application is not required.

Exception: Follow renewal verification requirements.

Screen and provide expedited services , if eligible.

Prorate benefits from the renewal application filing date .

Exception : Benefits are not prorated for migrant or seasonal farmworker EDGs who apply for renewal within 30 days of the end of the benefit period. Issue a full month’s benefits.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing 6
heading: Applications Submitted Within 30 Days After the Benefit Period Ends

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(a)(2) and (c)(7) and (d)(1) and (i)(4)(iv) & 7 CFR 273.14(b)(2)

A new W-1E Application is required.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing 7
heading: Applications Submitted More Than 30 Days After the Benefit Period Ends

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Delayed Processing

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.1(d)(1) & 7 CFR § 273.7(j)(3)(vii) & 7 CFR § 273.10(a)(2) and (c)(3) and (d)(4) and (e)(2)(v) and (e)(3)(vii) and (f) and (g)(2) & 7 CFR § 273.14(a)(d)(2)

Determine eligibility based on circumstances anticipated for the benefit period starting the month following the last month of the current benefit period.

Review ImpaCT for:

case information,

inconsistencies, or

verifications not previously provided that are due at renewal. See Change Processing and Simplified Reporting .

Use information reported on the renewal application , obtained during the interview, and any required verifications to determine the EDG’s eligibility for the new benefit period.

Keep the head of household at renewal, unless the EDG selects someone else.

Disqualify members or the EDG when a member voluntarily quits a job or reduces their hours without good cause during the last month of the benefit period. Begin the disqualification the first day of the new benefit period.

Clear any outstanding data matches .

Budget the EDG’s income and deductions following Determining Income, Expenses and Deductions policy. Exception: See Destitute EDGs .

Send the appropriate notice to advise the EDG of their renewal determination.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility 1
heading: Determining Eligibility

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(a)(2) & 7 CFR § 273.14(d)(2)

Benefits are not issued for the new benefit period before the previous benefit period ends.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility 2
heading: Issuing Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(i) & 7 CFR § 273.14(f)

EDGs are not eligible for expedited service if the EDG files a renewal application before the end of their current benefit period.

Provide expedited service , if eligible, to EDGs filing renewal applications after the end of their current benefit period.

Note: A new application is required if the renewal application is filed more than 30 days after the end of their current benefit period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility 3
heading: Expedited Service

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(a)(2)-(3)

EDGs ineligible for the initial renewal month but eligible for the next month do not need to reapply. Begin the EDG’s benefit period the month of application, but issue benefits beginning the next month. Do not issue benefits for the initial month.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility 4
heading: Ineligible for Benefits the First Month of the New Benefit Period

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(e)(3)(i)-(vii)

Destitute rules apply for the first month of the renewal period for migrant or seasonal farmworkers.

Exclude income from a new source the first month of the renewal period when the income:

is less than $25, and

will not be received by the 10th calendar day from the SNAP issuance.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility 5
heading: Destitute EDGs

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility

body:
7 CFR § 273.7(b)(1)(i) and (iv) & (2)(ii) & (c)

Review the following at renewal:

Work exemptions , including but not limited to:

Children who turned 16 years of age during the current benefit period, must :

register for work, or

meet another exemption.

Parents caring for a child who turned 6 during the current benefit period must :

register for work, or

meet another exemption

temporary disability ends

ABAWD status

Changes to the EDG composition and head of household status. Enter case notes for any changes to the head of household.

Advise the EDG of:

ABAWD work requirements ,

general work requirements ,

their rights and responsibilities , and

penalties for failure to comply .

Update the work registration status if needed.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility 6
heading: Work Registration

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining Eligibility

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Periods
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Periods

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Periods

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(f)(1)-(5)

Assign a new benefit period to eligible EDGs. See Benefit Periods .

Begin the new benefit period the month following the last month of the previous benefit period .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Periods 1
heading: Benefit Periods

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Periods

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes

body:
7 CFR 273.2(f)(6)

Record in ImpaCT any actions taken for the renewal determination, including:

cold-call dates and times,

delayed processing,

eligibility and benefit level,

ineligibility,

interview notes,

issues and questionable information,

verification sources, and

work registration changes.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes 1
heading: Case Notes

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Case Notes

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 271.2

Benefits are SNAP assistance issued to an EDG through an electronic benefit transfer (EBT) card .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits 1
heading: Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits

body:
Source # 15

Effective Date: 10/01/2024
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits 2
heading: Last Update

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefits

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Initial Month
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Initial Month

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Initial Month

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(a)(1)(i)-(iii)

An initial month is the first month the household is approved for benefits after any period the household was not approved for, or receiving benefits.

Exceptions: Initial month for:

Migrant or seasonal farmworkers is the first month the EDG is approved following a period of more than 30 days since the end of the EDG’s previous benefit period.

Joint SSI and SNAP applications filed before the individual’s release from an institution is the month the individual is released.

EDGs applying on the first of the month and found eligible receive a full initial month’s benefits. EDGs applying after the first of the month and found eligible receive prorated benefits .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Initial Month 1
heading: Initial Month

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Initial Month

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Combined Benefit
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Combined Benefit

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Combined Benefit

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(g)(2) and (i)(4)(iii)(C) & 7 CFR § 274.2(c)

Combined benefits are benefits issued at the same time for the initial month ( prorated amount ) and the following month (full month’s benefits).

Issue combined benefits when the application file date is after the 15th of the month, and

the EDG is approved to receive expedited benefits in the initial month and the following month, or

the EDG completed the application process following 30-day processing and is eligible to receive benefits the initial month and the following month.

Example: The EDG applies on November 16. The EDG is eligible for expedited service and the following month’s benefits. Issue benefits for November 16 through November 30, and December’s full month’s benefits.

Example: The EDG didn’t complete their SNAP renewal and their benefits ended in September. The EDG reapplies on October 20. The EDG is eligible for the application month and the following month’s benefits. Issue benefits for October 20 through October 31, and November’s full month’s benefits.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Combined Benefit 1
heading: Combined Benefit

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Combined Benefit

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Prorated Benefits
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Prorated Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Prorated Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(a)(1)(ii)

Prorated benefits are issuances of less than a full month’s benefits.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Prorated Benefits 1
heading: Prorated Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Prorated Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(e)(3) & 7 CFR § 273.10(a)(1)(ii)

Issue prorated benefits for initial month benefits when the EDG:

Applies after the first day of the month. Benefits are issued from the application file date through the last day of the month.

Is at fault for a delay in the application process. Benefits are issued from the date the EDG takes the required action to the end of that month. See EDG Caused Delay .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Prorated Benefits 2
heading: Application

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Prorated Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(a)(2) & 7 CFR 273.14(e)(3)

Issue prorated benefits at renewal if the EDG:

Files for renewal within 30 days after the end of the benefit period. Benefits are issued from the application file date through the last day of the month.

Exception: Do not prorate benefits if a migrant or seasonal farmworker EDG files for renewal within 30 days after the end of the benefit period.

Is at fault for a delay in the renewal process. Benefits are issued from the date the EDG takes the required action to the end of that month. See EDG Caused Delays .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Prorated Benefits 3
heading: Renewal

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Prorated Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 273.10(a)(1)(ii)(A) and (iii)(A) and (C)

ImpaCT determines the prorated benefit amount.

Complete a manual calculation, if needed, as follows:

Subtract from 31 the day of the:

application file date , or

the date verifications are provided for late renewals or EDG caused application delays.

Multiply the result of Step 1 by the full benefit the EDG should receive.

Divide the result of Step 2 by 30. The result is the pre-rounded amount of prorated benefits.

Round down the result of Step 3 to the nearest whole dollar for amounts of .01 through .99 cents.

Example : Application file date is January 14 and the full benefit amount is $194:

31 – 14 (application file date) = 17

17 x $194 (full benefit amount) = $3298

$3298 divided by 30 = $109.93

$109.93 round downs to $109, the prorated benefit.

Note: Prorated benefits less than $10 are not issued for the initial month only .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Prorated Benefits 4
heading: Determining Prorated Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Prorated Benefits

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Minimum Benefits
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Minimum Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Minimum Benefits

body:
7 CFR 273.10(e)(2)(ii)

Eligible EDGs with one or 2 members receive no less than the minimum monthly benefit .

Exception: Benefits are no t issued when the prorated initial month’s issuance is less than $10. Full benefits start the first day of the month following the initial month, if eligible.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Minimum Benefits 1
heading: EDGs of One or 2 Members

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Minimum Benefits

body:
7 CFR 273.10(e)(2)(ii)-(iii)(A) and (iv)

ImpaCT automatically denies or closes EDGs with 3 or more members when the monthly benefit is zero.

heading: EDGs of 3 or More Members

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Issuance Schedule
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Issuance Schedule

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Issuance Schedule

body:
7 CFR § 274.2(d)(1)

Initial month benefits are available after SNAP approval. Ongoing SNAP benefits are issued once a month from the first to the 3rd calendar day, based on the first letter of the head of household ’s last name.

First letter of the last name is:

A-F = Benefits are available on the first calendar day.

G-N = Benefits are available on the 2nd calendar day.

O-Z = Benefits are available on the 3rd calendar day.

Corrective payments are issued as needed. See Underpayments for more information.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Issuance Schedule 1
heading: Benefit Issuance Schedule

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT)
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT)

body:
7 CFR § 274.1(b)(1) & 7 CFR 273.3(b)

An EBT account is created upon application approval. EDGs access SNAP benefits using reusable plastic EBT cards to purchase eligible items at a retail food store .

The EDG may contact Customer Service at (1-888) 328-2666 if they have questions about accessing their benefits. Additionally, the EDG may establish a web account through ConneCT to:

Change their PIN

Check their SNAP balance

Contact Customer Service

Note: The EBT account is closed when all EDG members are deceased, and the SNAP case is closed. See Expunged EBT Benefits .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) 1
heading: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT)

body:
7 CFR § 274.2(b) and (f)

Authorize the EBT card through ImpaCT to the head of household and up to one A-Rep and one authorized shopper . The EBT card is mailed no later than the next day after it is authorized. The EBT card should be available within 2-mailing days. EBT cards have no expiration date.

A-Rep and authorized shopper cards contain their name and the head of household’s identification number.

EBT cards for homeless individuals are mailed to the:

local office, or

the mailing address the individual provided where notices and the EBT card can be received.

Note: The card must be available and allow the individual to access their benefits no later than the expedited service or normal processing timeframes .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) 2
heading: Issuing EBT Cards

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT)

body:
7 CFR § 274.2(b) and CT Process

Initial cards are activated when all the following occur:

Benefits are available.

The EBT card has been issued.

The individual contacts Customer Service and sets a PIN .

To set a PIN the individual must provide:

the 18-digit card number and

the head of household’s:

date of birth, and

last 4 digits of their SSN .

Contact the EBT Unit at (860) 424-4855 if the individual is unable to set a PIN.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) 3
heading: Activating the EBT Card

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT)

body:
7 CFR § 274.2(f) & 7 CFR § 274.6(b)(2) and (4) and CT Process

Request a new EBT card:

when there’s a change in the:

head of household,

A-Rep, or

authorized shopper, or

the card holder reports their card was:

damaged,

lost,

never received, or

stolen.

Note: Benefits are not replaced for losses occurring before the report was made. DSS will replace benefits accessed after the individual reports a lost or stolen EBT card.

Replace the card through the Benefit Issuance - Card Replacement page in ImpaCT. The replacement card is mailed to the individual. Previous cards are deactivated when a replacement is requested.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) 4
heading: Replacing EBT Cards

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT)

body:
7 CFR § 274.2(f)(1)-(2) & 7 CFR § 274.6(b)(1)-(3) and CT Process

Advise the card holder they can change their PIN at any time by:

contacting Customer Service, or

changing their PIN online through their web account.

Changing a PIN requires:

Client ID (9-digit numeric – starting with 2 zeros)

Existing PIN (6-digit numeric)

New PIN (six-digit numeric)

Customers who forget their PIN must call the DSS Benefit Center to delete their IVR accounts. Benefit Center staff reset the IVR PIN through the Worker Portal.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) 5
heading: Changing PINs

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT)

body:
7 CFR § 274.6(b)(6)(ii)

EDGs receive a notice from State Office when a 4th EBT card replacement is requested in a 12-month period. The notice provides the following information:

The EDG requested a greater number of EBT card replacements than normal in a 12 month period.

The number of replacement EBT cards issued.

Their EBT transactions are now being monitored.

What is considered misuse or fraudulent use of SNAP benefits.

DSS will investigate the EDG for misuse or fraud if additional cards are ordered.

Properly using or replacing their EBT card.

Contact number for questions.

If an investigation is completed, State Office advises the field office of any required actions.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) 6
heading: Excessive Card Replacements

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT)

body:
7 CFR § 274.2(h)(1) and (3)

Inactive EBT accounts are SNAP benefits not used by the EDG in 90 days. An automated notice is sent to the EDG providing the following information:

Their benefits have not been accessed in 90 days , and asks if they are having issues with their account.

Lists some possible issues and solutions.

Contact numbers if the EDG has questions.

Benefits will be removed from their account if no withdrawals are completed.

EDGs can access inactive benefits until the benefits are expunged.

Exception: See Paying a Claim .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) 7
heading: Inactive EBT Accounts

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT)

body:
7 CFR § 274.2(h)(2) and (3)

Expunged benefits are SNAP benefits not used by the EDG in 274 days. An alert is sent to the worker to start closure action.

Exception: Benefits are immediately expunged when a deceased individual match shows all members of the EDG are deceased, and the information is verified.

Expunged benefits are returned to the Federal SNAP program.

Exception: See Paying a Claim .

EDGs cannot access or have expunged benefits returned to their account.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) 8
heading: Expunged EBT Accounts

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT)

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 271.2 & 7 CFR § 274.7(a)

The EBT card can be used to purchase eligible food at any retail food store. EBT benefits can be used to pay a SNAP claim .

Advise EDGs at application and renewal how to properly use their benefits. Click here for more information on using SNAP benefits.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits 1
heading: Using SNAP Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 271.2

Retail food store generally means an establishment authorized by FNS to accept benefits.

The following require authorization by FNS:

Communal Dining Facility

Farmers’ markets

Meal delivery service

Retail grocery/convenience stores

Wholesale food distributors

Note: This list is not all inclusive. FNS may authorize other types of establishments.

The following establishments may be authorized by FNS to accept benefits or may be authorized representatives/shoppers for the residents:

Group Living for the disabled facilities

Homeless shelters

Residential addiction treatment centers

Shelters for battered individuals
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits 2
heading: Retail Food Store

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 271.2 & 7 CFR § 274.7(a) and (g)(1)-(4)

EDGs can use SNAP EBT cards to buy eligible foods, such as but not limited to:

Authorized prepared meals

Communal dining facilities

Meal delivery services

Retail food stores

Baby formula

Breads

Cereals

Dairy products

Fish

Fruits

Meats

Non-alcoholic beverages

Poultry

Seeds and plants which produce food to eat

Snacks

Vegetable
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits 3
heading: Eligible Items

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 271.2 & 7 CFR § 274.7(j)

EDGs cannot use their SNAP EBT cards to purchase non-food items, such as but not limited to:

Bag fees

Beer

Cigarettes

Cleaning supplies

Cosmetics

Delivery fees

Energy drinks classified by the FDA as supplements (have a supplement facts label rather than a nutrition facts label)

Hot foods

( Exception: Authorized prepared meals )

Household supplies

Hygiene items

Liquor

Live animals (except shellfish, fish removed from water, and animals slaughtered prior to pick-up from the store)

Medicines

Paper products

Paying credit accounts

Pet food

Soaps

Supplements

Tobacco

Vitamins

Wine

Note on bottle deposit/container fees:

SNAP benefits may only be used to pay the amount required by the State and only for containers that meet the criteria covered in the State law .

SNAP benefits may not be used to pay for container deposit fees in excess of the amount of any fee reimbursement established under State law .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits 4
heading: Ineligible Items

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits

body:
7 CFR § 273.17(b) and (d)(4) & 7 CFR § 273.18(c)(1) and (e)(7)(ii) and (f) and (g)(1)-(3)

EDGs can pay a SNAP claim using benefits from their SNAP account. EDGs can use their:

active,

inactive , or

expunged benefits.

The EDG must provide written or verbal permission to repay a claim using their SNAP active benefits.

Provide a notice to the EDG when inactive benefits are applied to a claim. Allow the EDG 10 days to dispute using their inactive benefits.

Do not reduce the initial month’s benefits to offset a claim , unless the EDG agrees to the reduction.

Offset restored benefits by the amount of any outstanding claim. See Overpayments and Claims for more information about claims and collection.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits 5
heading: Paying a Claim

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Using SNAP Benefits

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of the Value of Lost Food
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of the Value of Lost Food

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of the Value of Lost Food

body:
7 CFR § 274.6(a)(1)-(4)

EDGs losing food purchased with SNAP benefits may qualify to have the value of their lost food replaced. The loss of food must be due to an EDG misfortune, such as but not limited to:

Flood

Fire

Hurricane

Power outage of at least 4 hours .

Equipment malfunction (refrigerator or freezer) or other disaster.

Approved replacement benefits are issued for the cost of the lost food, up to the amount of the monthly benefits last issued. If the issuance included restored benefits, replace the full value of the restored benefits.

The EDG must :

report the loss within 10 days of the misfortune, and

complete, sign, and return a Request for Replacement of Food Purchased with SNAP Benefits ( W-1225 / W-1225S ) , within 10 days of the report date.

Note: If the 10th day falls on a weekend or holiday, the report must be made by the next workday.

Do not issue a replacement if the Request for Replacement of Food Purchased with SNAP Benefits isn’t received by the due date.

Replacement benefits must be available to the EDG within 10 days after the reported loss, or within 2 work days of receipt of the Request for Replacement of Food Purchased with SNAP Benefits, whichever is later .

There is no limit to the number of times an EDG can receive replacement benefits, but replacements are issued only once per incident.

Note: Federally approved natural disasters are handled differently than food lost due to an EDG misfortune. State Operations will issue Disaster SNAP (D-SNAP) instructions when a Federally declared disaster affects Connecticut. The EDG cannot receive both D-SNAP benefits and replacement benefits due to a misfortune.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of the Value of Lost Food 1
heading: Replacement of the Value of Lost Food

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of the Value of Lost Food

body:
7 CFR § 274.6(a)(3)-(7)

Complete the following steps to determine the EDG’s eligibility and replacement amount:

1) Determine the date of the EDG misfortune .

2) Give the EDG a Request for Replacement of Food Purchased with SNAP Benefits. The EDG must :

complete and sign the form, and

return the form within 10 days of reporting the loss. The form is due the first workday following the weekend or holiday if the 10th day falls on a weekend or holiday.

3) Using W-1226 / W-1226S , verify the loss through:

a collateral contact,

records from a community agency, such as, but not limited to the:

fire department,

power company,

Red Cross, or

by completing a home visit .

4) Review ImpaCT to ensure a benefit replacement request has not already been made.

5) Determine the benefit amount issued. The replacement amount is equal to the cost of the lost food, up to the amount of monthly benefits last issued.

6) Record your actions related to the replacement request including the following information:

Type of EDG misfortune

Date of the loss

Date power was restored (if the misfortune was due to a power outage of 4 hours or more)

7) Scan the Request for Replacement of Food Purchased with SNAP Benefits to the case record.

8) Issue replacement benefits:

Within 10 days after the loss report, or

2 workdays from receipt of the signed Request for Replacement of Food Purchased with SNAP Benefits, whichever is later;

9) Send a W-1227, Denial of Request for Replacement of Food Purchased With SNAP Benefits, advising the EDG of the approval or denial.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of the Value of Lost Food 2
heading: Determining Eligibility for Replacement

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of the Value of Lost Food

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits

body:
Due to a change in federal law, DSS is now piloting a process of replacing stolen SNAP benefits between October 1, 2022 – December 20, 2024 . The process will begin June 2, 2023.

For the purposes of this pilot, stolen benefits are SNAP benefits that are taken from an EBT card due to skimming , cloning or other fraudulent methods.

Note: This process is not to be used to replace P-EBT benefits or for EDGs who have had their physical EBT cards stolen.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits 1
heading: Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits

body: If an EDG reports benefits were stolen between October 1, 2022- June 1, 2023 , they have until July 2, 2023 , to report and request a replacement.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits 2
heading: Retroactive

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits

body: If benefits were stolen between June 2, 2023- December 20, 2024 , EDGs have 30 calendar days from the date they discover their benefits were stolen to notify the department.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits 3
heading: Current

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits

body:
Complete the following steps to determine the EDG’s eligibility and replacement amount:

EDGs have 30 calendar days from the date they discovered their benefits were stolen to request a replacement.

Once an EDG reports a loss of benefits, DSS will immediately (but no later than 5 business days ) advise the EDG to complete the Request for Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits form (W-3037/W-3037S).

A case note must be entered along with the date the Request for Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits Form (W-3037/W-3037S) was issued.

EDGs must be notified that they must change their PIN number or may request a new EBT card prior to replacement benefits being issued.

The EDG will have 10 calendar days from the date the form is sent to complete and return the Request for Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits form via mail, in-person, or online.

DSS will have 30 business days from receipt of the Request for Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits form to review claims requests, make a replacement determination, and issue replacement benefits, if appropriate.

Replacement benefits due to theft cannot exceed the amount of two months' of SNAP benefits or the amount of the actual reported loss, whichever is less.

Benefits lost due to theft cannot be replaced more than two times in a federal fiscal year (FFY) running October-September.

Depending on the outcome, DSS will either issue approved replacement benefits or issue a denial. In either instance, the Notice of Action – Request for Replacement of Stolen Benefits letter (W-3036N/ W-3036NS) will be issued to the EDG.

If the EDG disagrees with determination, they may request a fair hearing within ninety (90) days.

All EDGs who make a request for the replacement of SNAP benefits will be provided a Notice of Action – Request for Replacement of Stolen Benefits form. This form informs EDGs of the following: “If you think DSS made a mistake you may request a hearing within ninety (90) days of the date of this notice by calling 1-800-462-0134, or by written request faxed to (860) 424-5729 or mailed to: Department of Social Services, Office of Legal Counsel, Regulations, and Administrative Hearings, 55 Farmington Ave., Hartford, CT 06105.”

Note: Replacement benefits will not be made when a fair hearings appeal is pending for the replacement of stolen benefits.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits 4
heading: Determining Eligibility for Replacement

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Replacement of Stolen SNAP Benefits

body:
See https://portal.ct.gov/snap/replacementbenefits

See Intentional Program Violation (IPV) policies when the EDG knowingly gives false information

See Replacement of SNAP Benefits in the Consolidated Appropriations Act of 2023

heading: Resources:

body:
7 CFR § 273.26(a)

The Transitional Benefits Alternative (TBA) provides benefits to EDGs leaving TFA . TBA ensures the EDG continues to meet their nutritional needs as they move from TFA to work. TBA guarantees the EDG a set benefit amount for 5 months.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Transitional Benefits Alternative (TBA) 1
heading: Transitional Benefits Alternative (TBA)

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Transitional Benefits Alternative (TBA)

body:
Source #

Effective Date:

heading: Last Update

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining the Benefit Amount
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining the Benefit Amount

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining the Benefit Amount

body:
7 CFR § 273.27(a)

Complete a benefit determination using the last SNAP budget that included the TFA income. Remove the TFA income. The result is the EDG’s new net income used to determine the TBA benefit amount.

The TBA benefit remains unchanged for the length of the TBA benefit period .

No changes are allowed , including but not limited to COLA changes:

SUA,

LUA,

TUA, or

benefit amounts.

Exception: Adjust benefits if a member leaves the EDG and applies for SNAP as their own EDG, or in another EDG.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining the Benefit Amount 1
heading: Determining the Benefit Amount

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Determining the Benefit Amount

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Period
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Period

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Period

body:
7 CFR § 273.27(c)

Assign the TBA benefit period of 5 months. A renewal isn’t required to extend the current benefit period for the TBA 5-month benefit period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Period 1
heading: Benefit Period

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Period

body:
7 CFR § 273.2(c)(6) & 7 CFR § 273.13(b)(12) & 7 CFR § 273.27(a) & 7 CFR § 273.28 & 7 CFR § 273.29(c) & 7 CFR § 273.31 & 7 CFR § 273.32

TBA ends when the EDG:

applies and is approved for TFA,

files an renewal application for SNAP during the TBA benefit period,

requests closure of TBA, or

reaches the end of the TBA benefit period.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Period 2
heading: Ending TBA

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Benefit Period

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notices
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notices

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Notices

body:
7 CFR § 273.27(c) & 7 CFR § 273.29(a)-(f)

Do not issue a notice of adverse action when the current SNAP benefit period is shortened due to TBA beginning.

Issue a TBA approval notice to the EDG with the following information:

Eligibility for TBA.

The length of the TBA benefit period.

The EDG may apply for SNAP benefit renewal at any time during the TBA benefit period.

A notice will be sent to the EDG to file a renewal application approximately 40 days before the end of the TBA benefit period if the EDG doesn’t apply for renewal earlier.

If the EDG applies for TFA during the TBA benefit period, the application will also be used as a SNAP application.

An explanation of any changes in the benefit amount from the loss of TFA income.

The EDG is not required to report or provide verification until renewal of any changes occurring during the TBA benefit period.

Reported changes will not be acted on during the TBA benefit period until renewal.

The EDG should apply for renewal if any of the following changes occur:

Change in the EDG size

Decrease in income

Increase in expenses

heading: Notices

citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting Requirements and Changes During the TBA Benefit Period
heading: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting Requirements and Changes During the TBA Benefit Period

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting Requirements and Changes During the TBA Benefit Period

body:
7 CFR § 273.29 & 7 CFR § 273.30

EDGs are not required to report changes during the TBA benefit period .

heading: Reporting Requirements and Changes During the TBA Benefit Period

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting Requirements and Changes During the TBA Benefit Period

body:
7 CFR § 273.30

If changes are reported during the TBA benefit period that would decrease benefits, such as increased EDG income, record the change in case notes. Act on the change at renewal .
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting Requirements and Changes During the TBA Benefit Period 2
heading: Changes Causing a Decrease in Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting Requirements and Changes During the TBA Benefit Period

body:
7 CFR § 273.30

If changes are reported during the TBA benefit period that increase the TBA benefit, offer the EDG the option to complete a renewal for SNAP. If the EDG does not complete a renewal, continue TBA with no change in benefits.
citation_label: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting Requirements and Changes During the TBA Benefit Period 3
heading: Changes Causing an Increase in Benefits

metadata.title: Connecticut SNAP Policy Manual: Reporting Requirements and Changes During the TBA Benefit Period

body:
7 CFR § 273.30

To prevent duplicate participation, act on the change only if an EDG member leaves the EDG to apply as their own EDG, or requests addition to another EDG. Adjust the TBA benefits by removing:

the EDG member who left, and

that member’s income, assets, and deductible expenses.

heading: Member Leaves the EDG
